definition_ms: memakai; memakai dengan cara menyapu pada anggota badan (bedak, gincu, minyak rambut, dsb.)
example_semai: Mai engrak manah nai gemar gep bedak teu.
example_en: Older people in the past liked to apply  rice powder water to their faces.
example_ms: Orang tua dulu-dulu suka memakai bedak sejuk.

example_semai: "Kemoj apak kipergep tik ajeh ha eng, yama hakot ya hek?" Bah Amin kibekelahi ru itenek kedor.
example_en: "Our late father reserved that land for me, why did you take it?" Bah Amin argued with his older sister.
example_ms: "Mendiang bapa menyimpan tanah itu untuk saya, kenapa kamu yang ambil?" Bah Amin berkelahi dengan kakaknya.

definition_ms: menampi; mengasingkan biji-bijian dari sekamnya dengan menggunakan nyiru
example_semai: Wak Hana kigep canuk icengroi sebelom kigap medei kadang-kadang lekat mong icapeh sekop ru batu-batu bacet.
example_en: Wak Hana will winnow the rice before cooking it because sometimes there are pieces of husk and small stones.
example_ms: Wak Hana akan menampi beras terlebih dahulu sebelum memasaknya kerana kadang-kadang terdapat serpihan sekam dan batu-batu kecil.

example_semai: Wak Nana biles ya iamek medei kimasak sayo ihad gerag.
example_en: Wak Nana was scolded by her mother because the vegetables that she cooked were salty.
example_ms: Wak Nana dimarahi oleh ibunya kerana sayur yang dimasak olehnya itu adalah masin.

example_semai: Wak Ngah kicekot sog icenok de gerajos ajeh.
example_en: Wak Ngah is combing her grandaughter's messy hair.
example_ms: Wak Ngah mengikat rambut cucunya yang tidak terurus itu.

example_semai: Bah Adi kipet ha Bah Ali medei kigeram Bah Ali selalo kinyeh ha kek.
example_en: Bah Adi was hitting Bah Ali because he felt angry at Bah Adi for always teasing him.
example_ms: Bah Adi memukul Bah Adi kerana merasa geram Bah Adi selalu mengejeknya.

definition_en: sound of a cat scratching, sharpening its claws
example_semai: Kelem jihnek kuceg eng gerataj ku kemil pelog.
example_en: Last night, I could hear my cat scratching the roof.
example_ms: Malam tadi saya boleh mendengar kucing saya mencakar atap rumah.

definition_en: sound of rain starting to hit the roof, before the rain gets heavy
example_semai: Engrok manik gerataj hicerengai ku kemil pelog sebelom kimanik kilebat.
example_en: We could hear the sound rain drops before the rain getting heavy.
example_ms: Kedengaran bunyi hujan menitik-nitik di atas atap sebelum ianya menjadi lebat.

example_semai: Semak ajeh gerayul medei mong kekenon kuceg naibemain ku semak ajeh.
example_en: The bush was shaking because there were kittens playing in the bush.
example_ms: Semak tersebut kelihatan bergoyang-goyang kerana terdapat anak-anak kucing bermain di semak tersebut.

example_semai: Bah Midi ru ikekawat naikutep gerej medei naiha-beh engrat ha tengroj enai.
example_en: Bah Midi and his friend are collecting the termites because they want to use them as bait for fishing.
example_ms: Bah Midi dan kawan-kawannya mengutip anai-anai kerana mahu menjadikannya sebagai umpan untuk memancing.

definition_ms: gereja; tempat sembahyang bagi orang Kristian
example_semai: Jap Ahad mai Kristian naisembahyang ku gereja.
example_en: Christians worship at the church every Sunday.
example_ms: Orang Kristian bersembahyang di gereja pada setiap hari Ahad.

definition_ms: pura-pura tidak mendengar; tidak menyahut apabila dipanggil
example_semai: Hama kigerenteg tapi kicerengai ma de hibual.
example_en: Even though he pretended not to hear, in fact he heard what we said.
example_ms: Walaupun dia pura-pura tak dengar tapi sebenarnya dia dengar apa yang kita cakap.

definition_ms: sendawa; bunyi yang keluar dari kerongkong akibat kekenyangan
example_semai: Telas kicak ku kedei ajeh kek ajeh kengwat kigerep.
example_en: After eating at the shop, he belched loudly.
example_ms: Selepas makan di kedai itu dia sendawa dengan kuatnya.

example_semai: "Gerhag sih ajeh get lip!" Wak Zana kiles ikenon mase kipermuh ikenon ajeh.
example_en: "Clear out that phlem from your throat, do not swallow it!" Wak Zana scolded her son as she bathed him.
example_ms: "Keluarkan hingus itu, jangan telan!" Wak Zana memarahi anaknya semasa dia memandikan anaknya tersebut.

definition_en: clearing one's throat in order to get other's attention
example_semai: Mase cikgu kineng jar besimbang mase kibeajar, cikgu pen kigerhem nu lijar.
example_en: When the teacher saw the two of us chatting while he was teaching, the teacher cleared his throat to get our attention.
example_ms: Semase cikgu melihat kami berdua bersembang waktu dia mengajar, cikgu pun berdehem pada kami.

example_semai: Gerig adeh kigemar cak saknok binatag.
example_en: Monitor lizards like to eat carcasses.
example_ms: Biawak suka memakan bangkai binatang.

example_semai: Sog Wak Minah ajeh geril hamapen rimnar iamek-apak ajeh sog ubai selajar.
example_en: Wak Minah has curly hair even though both of her parents have straight hair.
example_ms: Rambut Wak Minah adalah kerinting walaupun kedua-dua ibubapanya berambut lurus.

example_semai: Wak Mala kiet nu kedei sog, kibeh isog gerinel.
example_en: Wak Mala went to the hair salon and made her hair curly.
example_ms: Wak Mala pergi ke salon rambut dan membuat rambutnya menjadi kerinting.

example_semai: Sentak kuceg Bah Midi ajeh geriwel.
example_en: The tail of Bah Midi's cat is curly.
example_ms: Ekor kucing milik Bah Midi adalah bergulung-gulung.

example_semai: Hek hagerlau eng.
example_en: You tricked me.
example_ms: Awak menipu saya.

example_semai: Amek eng kigeros kasut sekulah ya sabun kilat.
example_en: My mother is brushing my school shoes using bar soap.
example_ms: Ibu saya memberus kasut sekolah saya menggunakan sabun kilat.

definition_ms: jambatan; titi besar atau kecil yang selalunya dibina menyeberangi sungai atau untuk laluan lalulintas manusia dan kenderaan
example_semai: Gertak de ku beng deg ajeh ya repot.
example_en: The wooden bridge that is beside the house has decayed.
example_ms: Titi yang terdapat di tepi rumah itu sudah reput.

definition_ms: mendengar; dapat menangkap bunyi dengan telinga
example_semai: Wak Ana pek dapat kibukbet medei pek dikdij kigerteg engrok cok naibelawan ku semakdek kelem.
example_en: Wak Ana could not sleep because of hearing the incessant noise of dogs fighting in the middle of the night.
example_ms: Wak Ana tidak dapat tidur kerana tidak putus-putus mendengar bunyi anjing berlawan sesama sendiri pada tengah malam.

example_semai: Berog kuceg ajeh tebeg ya temak medei bikap ya gertek.
example_en: That cat's body is full of scabies because it was bitten by ticks.
example_ms: Badan kucing tersebut menjadi berkudis kerana digigit oleh sengkenit.

definition_ms: garu; mencakar dengan kuku kerana berasa gatal-gatal
example_semai: Bah Aman kiges ijog medei bikap ya kemet.
example_en: Bah Aman is scratching his leg because it was bitten by mosquitoes.
example_ms: Bah Aman menggaru kakinya kerana digigit oleh nyamuk.

example_semai: Hamapan ilei ya selalo kimasak, tapi Wak Ani lekat kisengoh bepasag kui ges.
example_en: Even though she always cooks, Wak Ani is still afraid to put install a cooking gas tank.
example_ms: Walaupun dia sudah sering memasak, tetapi Wak Ani masih takut untuk memasang kepala tong gas.

example_semai: Ku kampug jik jeoi keneng gesak.
example_en: In my village there are lots of chestnut trees.
example_ms: Di kampung kami banyak pokok buah berangan.

example_semai: Get cap awat de pekseg bisuj, doh gesej ku teg hik.
example_en: Do not touch the unwashed bamboo, or your hand will become itchy.
example_ms: Jangan pegang buluh yang belum dibasuh, nanti tangan akan menjadi gatal.

definition_ms: gatal-gatal pada kulit (terkena miang buluh dsb)

example_semai: Pelek gesok adeh bor icanak tapi hunen jarang hiha-kep medei kisui had ku serag taleh.
example_en: Chestnuts are very tasty but now they are very hard to get  because they can only be found in the jungle.
example_ms: Rasa buah berangan adalah sangat sedap tetapi sekarang jarang boleh didapati kerana ianya hanya terdapat di hutan.

example_semai: Bah Amat kipej gesug nu cog.
example_en: Bah Amat is throwing a rock at a dog.
example_ms: Bah Amat membaling batu pada anjing.

definition_ms: menyembelih; memotong atau mengerat leher sehingga keluar darah (daging, anggota tubuh badan)
example_semai: Bah Ajis kiget pog medei iamek kaha-beh rendang.
example_en: Bah Ajis slaughtered the chicken because his mother wanted to make chicken stew.
example_ms: Bah Ajis menyembelih ayam kerana ibunya mahu memasak rendang.

definition_ms: larang; melarang; tidak mengizinkan; tidak membenarkan seseorang untuk melakukan sesuatu dsb
example_semai: Walaupen memberip noh biget et nu tempat ajeh, kek tik lagi kibegalaj.
example_en: Although several times he has been forbidden to go, he is still obstinate.
example_ms: Walaupun beberapa kali dilarang pergi ke tempat itu, dia masih lagi berdegil.

example_semai: Hot gep kui hik ru limau baruleh tok begetau.
example_en: We ought to wash our heads with lime so we don't get dandruff.
example_ms: Harus mencuci kepala kita dengan limau barulah tidak berkelumumur.

definition_ms: membenci; tidak suka akan sesuatu atau seseorang
example_semai: Amen hiui jahat mai pen bigetek.
example_en: If we do evil, people will hate us.
example_ms: Jika kita membuat jahat, orang akan membenci kita.

example_semai: Hik hod hingut teu de cukop, baruleh getek hik sehat.
example_en: We should drink sufficient water, then our skin will be healthy.
example_ms: Kita harus meminum air yang mencukupi, barulah kulit kita sihat.

example_semai: Agok getoh merate-rate.
example_en: Don't spit all over the place.
example_ms: Jangan meludah merata-rata.

example_semai: Taya meskar Bah Yan kitateg medei mong iskru de geu.
example_en: Bah Yan's bicycle tire came off as there was a loose screw.
example_ms: Tayar basikal Bah Yan tertanggal kerana terdapat skrunya yang longgar.

definition_en: Semai traditional festival to celebrate the new year or the harvest
definition_ms: perayaan tradisi Orang Semai bagi menyambut tahun baru atau musim menuai
example_semai: "Ku tahut depan, kampug jik doh pek bibeh arik genulang," Bah Jamil kipasal nu ikekawat.
example_en: "Next year, our village will not celebrate the new year," Bah Jamil told his friends.
example_ms: "Pada tahun hadapan, kampung kami tidak akan membuat sambutan tahun baharu," Bah Jamil memberitahu kepada rakan-rakannya.

definition_ms: mengelap; mengeringkan atau membersihkan sesuatu yang basah atau kotor (lap lantai, lap meja, lap muka dsb); kesat; mengesat; memadam
example_semai: Bah Asril tengah kigit res.
example_en: Bah Asril is wiping the floor.
example_ms: Bah Asril sedang mengelap lantai.

example_semai: Amen hod gij nu sekulah belajar kus awal.
example_en: If you want to get to school quickly, learn to get up early.
example_ms: Jika mahu cepat ke sekolah belajarlah bangun awal.

example_semai: Amek jop eng mong igij, kirasa pehod bile kicacak.
example_en: My grandmother has a decayed tooth, she feels pain every time she eats.
example_ms: Gigi nenek saya rosak, dia merasa sakit apabila makan.

example_semai: Jehuk legos ajeh bigik pegelap neh.
example_en: The rambutan tree was cut down this morning.
example_ms: Pokok rambutan itu ditebang pada pagi tadi.

definition_ms: gila; tidak siuman; tiga suku (ungkapan); tidak waras akal fikiran
example_semai: Mai gila jooi bideg ku Hospitel Bahagia Tanjung Rambutan ku Ipoh.
example_en: Most insane people are admitted to the mental hospital in Tanjung Rambutan at Ipoh.
example_ms: Kebanyakan orang gila diletakkan di Hospital Bahagia Tanjung Rambutan di Ipoh.

example_semai: Mase jijug ju sekulah cikgu kigawej iteg nu jik.
example_en: When we went home from school the teacher waved to us.
example_ms: Semasa kami pulang dari sekolah cikgu melambai tangannya kepada kami.

definition_en: gopher, a type of wood used to build Noah's ark
definition_ms: gofir, sejenis kayu yang digunakan untuk membina bahtera Nuh

definition_en: to carry something in the mouth (by dogs)
definition_ms: menggonggong (oleh anjing); membawa sesuatu dengan mulut
example_semai: Wak Ana kiwig telas kineng icok kigog relai.
example_en: Wak Ana screamed when she saw her dog carrying a python in its mouth.
example_ms: Wak Ana menjerit apabila melihat anjingnya menggonggong ular sawa.

example_semai: Bah Amin kitulog iamek goj sej medei iamek kaha-beh rendang.
example_en: Bah Amin is helping his mother to cut the meat because his mother is about to cook stew.
example_ms: Bah Amin membantu ibunya memotong daging kerana ibunya mahu memasak rendang.

example_semai: "Mong mai bigok pintu, cip neng bok imai," Bah Asin kiurok nu ikenon litau.
example_en: "There is someone at the door, knocking. Go and see who it is?" Bah Asin said to his son.
example_ms: "Ada orang yang mengetuk pintu, pergi lihat siapa itu," Bah Asin berkata kepada anak lelakinya.

definition_ms: pikul; memikul; membawa sesuatu di atas bahu
example_semai: Tenek eng kigol betar ku igelpal.
example_en: My brother carries stinky beans on his shoulder.
example_ms: Abang saya pikul petai di bahunya.

example_semai: Gensir Wak Ana ajeh Gop Kelantan.
example_en: Wak Ana's husband is a Malay guy from Kelantan.
example_ms: Suami Wak Ana adalah orang Melayu dari Kelantan.

definition_ms: menggoreng; memasak dengan minyak (tanpa kuah) di dalam kuali kering
example_semai: Wak Ami kigoreng kengyeg pog.
example_en: Wak Ami is frying the chicken wings.
example_ms: Wak Ami menggoreng kepak ayam.

example_semai: Bah Kamis mong jeoi gos ku icelod.
example_en: Bah Kamis has a lot of ringworm on his back.
example_ms: Terdapat banyak kurap pada belakang badan Bah Kamis.

definition_en: rice crust; hard rice cooked at the bottom or side of the rice pot
example_semai: Kipasal ya mai raknak engrak amen hik cak gos canak hek jadi segat.
example_en: The old people say that if you eat the rice crust you will become a lazy person.
example_ms: Kata orang tua jika kita makan kerak nasi kita jadi seorang pemalas.

example_semai: Hik hod hijaga adat gu lihik.
example_en: We should protect our own group's customs.
example_ms: Kita harus menjaga adat kaum kita sendiri.

definition_en: more; all the more; to become or get plus adjective
example_semai: Apak eng ya guengrak, iumur ya 71 tahut.
example_en: My dad is getting older, he is already 71 years old.
example_ms: Bapa saya sudah semakin tua, umurnya sudah 71 tahun.

definition_ms: mengupas; menanggalkan atau membuang kulit sesuatu benda (buah kelapa, bawang, dll)
example_semai: Mase jis sukaneka ku sekulah, mong pertandingan gubal jeer.
example_en: During the sport day at the school, they held a contest to husk coconuts.
example_ms: Semasa hari sukaneka di sekolah, terdapat pertandingan mengupas kelapa.

example_semai: Ku pukol 4 pegelap, mase iarik lekat guer, Wak Ngah kihol kimutong kihalei.
example_en: At 4 in the morning, when it is still pitch dark, Wak Ngah goes out alone to tap rubber.
example_ms: Pada jam 4 pagi, ketika keadaan masih gelap, Wak Ngah keluar memotong getah seorang diri.

example_semai: Jik mai sengoi, jiseh bak ku gul de bibeh ju keneng jehuk de entoi.
example_en: We, the aboriginal people, pound rice in a mortar made from a large log.
example_ms: Kami Orang Asli menumbuk padi dalam lesung yang diperbuat dari batang kayu besar.

example_semai: Wak Amah kiguna gul batu kiseh ha lada.
example_en: Wak Amah is using the pestle and mortar to pound the bird's eye chillis.
example_ms: Wak Amah menggunakan lesung batu untuk menumbuk cili.

definition_en: to carry a load on the shoulder
example_semai: Bah Wan kigulap dendog nu imadeg.
example_en: Bah Wan carried the log on his shoulder to his house.
example_ms: Bah Wan memikul balak ke rumahnya.

example_semai: Arik adeh Wak Jana kimasak gulei labu ha entek caknak tengah arik.
example_en: Wak Jana is making a pumpkin dish as a side-dish for lunch today.
example_ms: Hari ini Wak Jana memasak gulai labu sebagai lauk bagi makan tengah hari.

example_semai: Kekenon Bah Amat naibeguleng ku kemil tilam.
example_en: Bah Amat's children are rolling on the mattress.
example_ms: Anak-anak Bah Amat berguling-guling di atas tilam.

example_semai: "Jok himain congkak, iguli ya mong adeh," Wak Sita kiajak ha Wak Rika.
example_en: "Let's us play congkak, I have the marbles," Wak Sita invited Wak Rika.
example_ms: "Mari kita bermain congkak, gulinya sudah ada," Wak Sita mengajak Wak Rika.

example_semai: "Gulong ceruk ajeh telas engkecakcak," Wak Ngah kibesat nu icecenok.
example_en: "Roll back the mat after you have finished eating," Wak Ngah reminded her grandchildren.
example_ms: "Gulung tikar itu nanti selepas kamus makan," Wak Ngah berpesan kepada cucu-cucunya.

example_semai: Bah Amran kitanya nu icekgu, "Cekgu, bulehka ngguna bob rujukan amen eng kedei bahbeh kerjak sekulah?"
example_en: Bah Amran asked to his teacher,"Teacher, can I use the reference book if I don't know how to do my homework?"
example_ms: Bah Amran bertanya kepada gurunya, "Cikgu, bolehkah saya mengguna buku rujukan jika saya tidak tahu membuat kerja sekolah?"

example_semai: "Kok adeh iguna ha bahno cengroi, agok guna ha bende kilek," Wak Bibah kiurok nu ikenon menaleh.
example_en: "The function of this can is to scoop the rice, so please don't use it for anything else," Wak Bibah said to her daughter.
example_ms: "Tin ini kegunaannya adalah untuk mencebok beras, jangan gunakannya untuk hal yang lain," Wak Bibah berkata kepada anak gadisnya.

definition_ms: guni; karung untuk mengisi sesuatu benda
example_semai: Kot guni ajeh, eng ngha-isi sampah.
example_en: Take that sack, I want to put garbage in it.
example_ms: Ambil guni itu, saya mahu mengisi sampah.

definition_en: ghost; spirit; the soul or spirit of a deceased person or an animal
definition_ms: roh; semangat; roh atau semangat orang yang sudah meninggal dunia atau haiwan
example_semai: Mai naiurok, Bah Litaw ajeh mong kidek gunig mergas.
example_en: People said that Bah Litaw defended a tiger spirit.
example_ms: Orang berkata bahawa Bah Litaw ada membela roh harimau.

example_semai: Apak eng kiguntik sog menang eng.
example_en: My father is cutting my brother's hair.
example_ms: Bapa saya menggunting rambut adik saya.

example_semai: "Og gures hek, eng ngha-tod sampah," Wak Kila kisemanj nu igengsir.
example_en: "Let me borrow your matches, I want to burn the garbage," Wak Kila asked her husband.
example_ms: "Bagi mancis kamu, saya mahu membakar sampah," Wak Kila meminta kepada suaminya.

example_semai: Wak Cici kigusok minyak angin ku ikui medei ha kiluig.
example_en: Wak Cici is rubbing the ointment on her head as she feel dizzy.
example_ms: Wak Cici menggosok minyak angin di kepalanya kerana merasa pening kepala.

example_semai: Wak Melah kiurok nu eng, "Pelek jeer adeh ha hek."
example_en: Wak Melah said to me, "These coconuts are for you."
example_ms: Wak Melah berkata kepada saya, "Buah kelapa ini untuk kamu."

definition_en: you; 2nd person singular agreement marker for verb
definition_ms: awak; anda; kamu; imbuhan ganti nama diri kedua tunggal untuk kata kerja
example_semai: Hek ya hapanei muh eng.
example_en: You already know my name.
example_ms: Kamu sudah tahu nama saya.

example_semai: Amek kiurok nu wak eng, "Entah hahod tarog adeh, kotle."
example_en: My mother said to my auntie, "If you want this tapioca shoot, (just) take it."
example_ms: Ibu berkata kepada makcik saya, "Jika kamu mahu pucuk ubi ini, ambillah."

example_semai: "Mongka hek neh hakep kak?" bitanya ya Wak Risan. "Haah, mong nek memerip ikor taleh," engrok Wah Ika.
example_en: "Did you catch any fish?"  Wak Risan asked. "Yes, only a few," Wak Ika replied.
example_ms: "Kamu ada dapat ikan?" Wak Risan bertanya. "Ya, hanya ada beberapa ekor saja," Wak Ika menjawab.

example_semai: Ma ihabar engkek nek?
example_en: How are you all doing?
example_ms: Kamu semua apa khabar?

definition_ms: abu; debu; debu; sisa sesuatu objek atau lain-lain benda yang habis terbakar
example_semai: Cok ajeh kigemar dak ku kemil habuk.
example_en: That dog likes to sleep on the ashes.
example_ms: Anjing itu suka tidur atas abu.

example_semai: Apak eng pek dij isekulah, kisekulah had lei derjah 5 taleh. Tapi ha kerana rajet kikerakjak, ilei bihok ya itukeh. Jap tahut igaji biperhuj ru selalo biog bonus.
example_en: My father did not finish school, he only went to school until grade 5. But because he was hard-working, he was loved by his boss. His salary is increased every year and he always earns bonuses.
example_ms: Bapa saya tidak tamat persekolahan, dia bersekolah cuma sehingga darjah 5. Tetapi oleh kerana dia rajin bekerja, dia disayangi oleh bosnya. Setiap tahun gajinya dinaikan dan selalu memerolehi bonus.

example_semai: Kenon eng ihad kisengoh ha chok medei ilei pernah bikap mase lekat kisanget.
example_en: My son is very scared of dogs because he was bitten when he was young.
example_ms: Anak saya sangat takut terhadap anjing kerana dia pernah digigit semasa beliau masih kecil.

definition_ms: menghalang; menahan supaya tidak dapat bergerak
example_semai: Bah Peran kibesat nu eng, "Nong nu Cameron Highland ya bihadang, biget joi ku arik adeh medei mong garaj. Kelemnek manik lebat lei mong jeoi garaj ku nong ajeh."
example_en: Bah Peran gave advice to me, "the road to Cameron Highlands has been blocked, that road cannot be used because there has been a landslide. Last night it rained heavily and lots of earth slid onto that road."
example_ms: Bah Peran berpesan kepada saya, "Jalan itu ke Cameron Highland dihalang, tidak dibenarkan mengikuti jalan tersebut kerana terdapat tanah runtuh. Malan tadi hujan lebat sehingga ada banyak (kejadian) tanah runtuh di (sepanjang) jalan tersebut."

definition_en: to be facing; to be face-to-face
example_semai: Mase ubai bitermuk, Wak Ani ru Bah Meran ubai gei behadap sebegei tanda ubai ajeh kenah-gensir.
example_en: When they got married, Wak Ani and Bah Meran sat face-to-face as a sign that they were husband and wife.
example_ms: Semasa mereka dijodohkan, Wak Ani dan Bah Meran mereka berdua duduk menghadap (satu dengan yang lain) sebagai tanda mereka berdua itu suami-isteri.

example_semai: Amek kitanya nu tenek eng, "Ya hagka temak kenon hek?"
example_en: Mother asked my sister, "has your son's scabies healed?"
example_ms: Ibu bertanya pada kakak saya, "Sudah sembuhkah kudis (pada) anak kamu?"

definition_en: to scream; to shout; to snarl; to scold
example_semai: Apak eng kiurok nu bah eng, "Agok panhai, mabek sanget doh naisengoh."
example_en: My father said to my uncle, "Don't shout too loud! The kids will be scared."
example_ms: Bapa saya berkata kepada pakcik saya, "Jangan terlampau menengking, nanti kanak-kanak menjadi takut."

definition_en: to be extremely poor; to be destitute
example_semai: Bah Mat ajeh, hamapen kihajap tapi pek pernah kisengmanj nu mai.
example_en: Even though Bah Mat is poor, he has never begged from other people.
example_ms: Walaupun Bah Mat miskin, tetapi dia tidak pernah meminta-minta kepada orang lain.

definition_ms: hal; sesuatu yang terjadi; peristiwa atau kejadian
example_semai: "Ma-ma hal doh pasalle nu eng," tuan deg ajeh kiurok nu eng.
example_en: "If there is any issue, just let me know," the landlord said to me.
example_ms: "Apa-apa hal nanti bagitahu saya," tuan rumah itu berkata kepada saya.

example_semai: Ku jaman entem, amen mai nainyaknyik, enai naiet nu halak dea biterbor. Tapi hunen, ya kurang mai naituju nu halak medei ya mong hospitel.
example_en: In old days, when people got sick, they went to a shaman to be healed. But now, there are few who go to see a shaman, because there is a hospital already.
example_ms: Pada zaman dahulu, jika orang merasa sakit, mereka akan pergi (berjumpa) bomoh supaya disembuhkan. Tetapi sekarang, sudah kurang orang berjumpa bomoh kerana sudah ada hospital.

definition_en: terms for a man to address his brother-in-law
definition_ms: panggilan hormat oleh seorang lelaki kepada adik ipar lelaki dan abang iparnya

definition_en: to shoo; to chase or drive away
example_semai: Amek eng kihalau cok, kinyek doh kidak ku simen.
example_en: My mother shooed the dog away, because she didn't want it to sleep on the cement floor of the house.
example_ms: Ibu saya menghalau anjing, (kerana) dia tidak mahu (anjing tersebut) tidur di (lantai) simen (rumah).

example_semai: Eng nggei nghalei ku padeg.
example_en: I live by myself at home.
example_ms: Saya tinggal seorang diri di rumah.

example_semai: "Halek, pek ma. Buleh ngbeh ya lieng."
example_en: "Never mind, I can do it myself."
example_ms: "Tidak mengapa, saya boleh buat sendiri."

definition_en: to pick or pluck; to harvest (vegetables, leaf shoots, etc.)
example_semai: "Bulehka eng ngham lada ku selai hek? Lada ku selai eng ya dij," Wak Ros kisemanj ku amek eng.
example_en: "Can I pick the peppers that are in your garden? The peppers in my garden are already gone," Wak Ros asked my mother.
example_ms: "Bolehkah saya memetik cili yang ada di kebun kamu? Cili di kebun saya sudah habis," Wak Ros meminta kepada ibu saya.

definition_en: to take; to get; to fetch
example_semai: "Bei tulog amek ham teu," amek kiurok nu jik.
example_en: "Come and help me fetch water," mother said to us.
example_ms: "Mari tolong ibu mengambil air," ibu berkata kepada kami.

definition_ms: kenapa; mengapa; untuk menanyakan sebab untuk seseuatu perkara atau hal
example_semai: "Hama hek jap?" tenek eng kitanya.
example_en: "Why are you crying?" my older brother asked.
example_ms: "Kenapa kamu menangis?" abang saya bertanya.

example_semai: Hamapen ilei kinyik, tapi Wak Selai lekat kaha-kerakjak juga.
example_en: Even though she is feeling feverish, Wak Selai still wants to work.
example_ms: Walaupun dia demam, tetapi Wak Selai masih mahu bekerja.

definition_en: title for Semai women, especially in the southern part of the state of Perak
definition_ms: panggilan bagi perempuan Semai, terutama sekali di bahagian negeri selatan Perak
example_semai: "Ma muh hek?" kitanyak la Bah Seri. "Muh eng Han Didek."
example_en: "What is your name?" Bah Seri asked. "My name is Han Didek."
example_ms: "Siapa nama kamu?" Bah Seri bertanya. "Nama saya Han Didek."

definition_ms: saudari; gadis jika tidak tahu namanya yang sebenar
example_semai: Eng ngsip la muh han anuk ajeh.
example_en: I forgot the lady's name.
example_ms: Saya lupa nama saudari itu.

definition_ms: geram; marah; panas hati; sakit hati
example_semai: Amen nus hangat mai bikenal ku renis hek.
example_en: If we are angry, others tell in our face.
example_ms: Jika hati geram orang tahu di muka kita.

example_semai: Hanoh hak bok?
example_en: This belongs to whom?
example_ms: Ini siapa punya?

example_semai: Bah Kari kingut jeoi teu medei ha kihaos, ha bilir kijar.
example_en: Bah Kari drank a lot of water because he was thirsty, as a result of running too much.
example_ms: Bah Kari meminum banyak air kerana dia haus, akibat terlampau berlari.

definition_ms: hafal; dapat mengingat apa yang dipelajari
example_semai: Bah Medan ya darjah 2, tapi lekat pek telas kihapal lebeh ju siper 3.
example_en: Bah Medan is already in the second grade, but still has not memorised the multiplication table beyond 3.
example_ms: Bah Medah sudah darjah 2, tapi masih tidak lepas menghafal lebih dari sifir 3.

definition_en: to press between two surfaces; to pinch; to clip
example_semai: Bah Ngah tengah kihapet isej pog ku bilah awat kaha-jop.
example_en: Bah Ngah is clipping the chicken meat between pieces of bamboo in order to roast it.
example_ms: Bah Ngah sedang menyepit daging ayam diantara bilah buluh untuk dipanggang.

definition_en: we two; both of us; 1st person dual (the speaker and the hearer) pronoun
definition_ms: kita berdua; kata ganti nama diri pertama duaan inklusif
example_semai: "Jok har haret nu Bota huper doh, eng ngha-bakek kawat eng," Bah Sani kiajak eng.
example_en: "Come let both of us go to Bota tomorrow, I want to meet my friend," Bah Sani invited me.
example_ms: "Jom kita berdua pergi ke Bota esok, saya mahu berjumpa kawan saya," Bah Sani mengajak saya.

definition_en: we; 1st person dual (the speaker and the hearer) agreement marker for verb
definition_ms: kita berdua; imbuhan ganti nama diri pertama duaan inklusif untuk kata kerja
example_semai: "Harok harha-et nu kedei amen manik lebat begei adeh?" apak kiurok nu amek eng.
example_en: "How are we going to get to the shop if it is raining heavily like this?" father asked mother.
example_ms: "Bagaimana kita berdua mahu ke kedai jika hujan lebat begini?" bapa berkata kepada ibu saya.

definition_ms: harga; nilai sesuatu benda (barang, perkhidmatan dll.)
example_semai: Amek eng pek pernah kibakli barang ku pasaraya medei iharga barang mahal, tapi kek selalo kibakli had ku pasar biasa taleh.
example_en: My mother never shops at the supermarket, because the prices are higher, but she always shops at the wet market only.
example_ms: Ibu saya tidak pernah membeli barang di pasaraya kerana harga barangnya mahal, tapi dia selalu membeli hanya di pasar biasa sahaja.

example_semai: Jap-jap hari jik jikerakjak kecuali hari minggu.
example_en: We work everyday except for Sunday.
example_ms: Kami bekerja setiap hari kecuali hari Ahad.

example_semai: Bah Sal birok ya iapak medei pek kipanei harok ha perder os ha reknag kak.
example_en: Bah Sal was scolded by his father because he did not know how to kindle the fire to grill fish.
example_ms: Bah Sal dimarahi oleh bapanya kerana tidak tahu bagaimana mahu menghidupkan api bagi membakar ikan.

definition_en: to shoo; to scare away an animal
definition_ms: halau; menghalau binatang ke sesuatu tempat atau arah dengan cara mengepungnya ke arah tersebut
example_semai: Bah Amin kiharok kucig de kaha-cak kak.
example_en: Bah Amin shooed away the cat that was trying to eat the fish.
example_ms: Bah Amin menghalau kucing yang mahu memakan ikan.

example_semai: Biasajeh ku kenurik caknak, tempat caknak has ha penganten bidek ku depan sekali.
example_en: Usually at the wedding feast, the table specially for the bride and groom is located at the very front.
example_ms: Biasanya di kenduri perkahwinan, tempat makan yang dikhaskan bagi pengantin terletak di hadapan sekali.

definition_ms: hasil; segala yang diperolehi; perolehan dari sesuatu perkara
example_semai: Amen hikerjak kuat hikep ihasel.
example_en: If we work hard, we will get results.
example_ms: Kalau kita bekerja keras, kita akan mendapat hasilnya.

example_semai: Bah Man kihasot eng punteng sekulah arik adeh. Jar jarcip nu taman, jarlepak ku pajeh lei dij waktu sekulah.
example_en: Bah Man incited me to skip school today. We both went to the park and hung out there until the end of school hours.
example_ms: Bah Man menghasut saya untuk ponteng sekolah pada hari ini. Kami berdua ke taman, melepak di sana sehingga tamat waktu persekolahan.

example_semai: Wak Salwa kihator bor-bor bob ajeh ku rak buku.
example_en: Wak Salwa organized the books neatly on the bookshelf.
example_ms: Wak Salwa mengatur buku-buku tersebut dengan kemas di rak buku.

definition_ms: sejenis tiram yang banyak didapati di kawasan lombong
example_semai: Mong jeoi hawap ku lumbong.
example_en: The are many oysters found in abandoned tin mines.
example_ms: Terdapat banyak tiram di lombong.

example_semai: Bob ajeh hayok, biangkat ya englag teg pen pek ma.
example_en: That book is light, it can even be lifted with one hand.
example_ms: Buku itu ringan, diangkat dengan sebelah tangan pun tidak mengapa.

example_semai: Mong jeoi cep naihengheg ku rahuk.
example_en: There are many birds flying in the sky
example_ms: Terdapat banyak burung berterbangan di langit.

definition_en: to be unable to listen because it is too noisy
definition_ms: tidak dapat mendengar kerana terlalu bising
example_semai: "Hiheg engrok radio, naipan-pasag," amek eng kikomplen ha engrok muzik ku tempat caknak kenduri de jiet cinek.
example_en: "We were not able to listen to the words from the radio, they turned it on too loudly," my mother complained about the music at the wedding we went to earlier.
example_ms: "Kami tak dapat mendengar kata dari radio, mereka memasang dengan sangat kuat," ibu saya komplain akan bunyi muzik di tempat kenduri yang kami pergi tadi.

definition_ms: tercekik; tersangkut sesuatu (makanan, objek dll.) di kerongkong
example_semai: "Eng ngheg ya jeag kak. Teros ngwes caknak eng ajeh," Bah Hali kicerite nu jik.
example_en: "I choked on a fish bone. I  straightaway stopped eating," Bah Hali told us.
example_ms: "Saya tercekik oleh tulang ikan. Saya terus berhenti makan," Bah Hali bercerita paa kami.

definition_en: frequently used when calling one's spouse; sometimes used for people of all ages, regardless of age
definition_ms: panggilan yang sering digunakan oleh pasangan suami isteri; kadangkala digunakan juga bagi orang yang sebaya umur tidak kira lelaki atau perempuan dewasa
example_semai: "Hei, hei, bulehka hangeng eng nu kedei?" Wak Ira kitanya nu igengsir.
example_en: "Dear, can you drive me to the store?" Wak Ira asked her husband.
example_ms: "Bang, bolehkah kamu menghantar saya ke kedai?" Wak Ira bertanya kepada suaminya.

definition_ms: hei; untuk untuk membuatkan orang lain berasa takut akan sesuatu atau seseorang
example_semai: "Hej! Haet belok ajeh? Iarik ya pukol 10 kelem lekat kaha-hol!" tenek eng kiurok nu ikenon litau.
example_en: "Hey! Where are you going? It's 10pm, but you still want to go out!" my sister said to her teenage son.
example_ms: "Hei! Kamu hendak ke mana? Waktu sudah jam 10 malam, tapi masih hendak keluar!" kakak saya berkata kepada anak terunanya.

definition_ms: terjerat; tersangkut; terperangkap (binatang terperangkap di dalam atau terkena jerat atau pancing)
example_semai: Hamapen Bah Sandin kigei ku beg teu ju pegelap lei dui, tapi ikedek langsong pek hek ya nuikor pen kak.
example_en: Although Bah Sandin had been by the river from morning to evening, there was not a single fish caught on his fishing rod.
example_ms: Walaupun Bah Sandin sudah berada di tepi sungai dari pagi hingga ke petang, tetapi tidak ada seekor pun ikan yang terjerant pada pancingnya.

definition_ms: awak; kamu; engkau; anda; kata ganti nama diri kedua tunggal
example_semai: "Hek haet belok?" amek kitanya nu tenek kerdor eng.
example_en: "Where are you going?" mother asked my older sister.
example_ms: "Kamu hendak ke mana?" ibu bertanya pada kakak saya.

definition_en: gibbon; a great ape that has long hands and no tail
definition_ms: siamang; sejenis beruk besar yang mempunyai tangan yang pandang dan tidak berekor
example_semai: Hel adeh iengrok kuat, ju kernyak ya buleh hicerengai iengrok. Mase jik jiet nu zuu padehjeh, ku ipintu miknuj zu ajeh ya buleh jicerengai engrok hel.
example_en: The sound of gibbons is loud, from afar their sound can be heard. When we went to the zoo that day, we already heard the sound of the gibbons at the entrance.
example_ms: Bunyi siamang adalah kuat, jadi kejauhan sudah boleh kedengaran bunyinya. Semasa kami pergi ke zoo tempoh hari, di pintu masuk zoo kami sudah mendengar bunyi siamang.

definition_en: classifier for something thin and flat (sheet, layer, etc)
definition_ms: helai; benda yang tipis dan nipis (daun, kain, kertas dll)
example_semai: Tik had nuhenglei taleh kertas.
example_en: There is only one piece of paper.
example_ms: Hanya ada sehelai sahaja kertas.

definition_en: very close; very soon; fast approaching
example_semai: "Ya hemberek caknak Wak Tira," amik eng kipeingat ha jik.
example_en: "Wak Tira's wedding is coming very soon," my aunt reminded us.
example_ms: "Kenduri perkahwinan Wak Tira sudah hampir," makcik saya memperingatkan kami.

definition_en: almost near; almost reached; very close
example_semai: "Pelahat gek, ya hemberek nu simpang kampug de hiha-tuju. Amen halaju doh, halalu isimpang ajeh," Bah Kakyeng kiurok nu Bah Melan.
example_en: "Slow down a little bit, we have almost reached the intersection of the village we want to go to. If you drive fast, you will miss the intersection," Bah Kakyeng said to Bah Melan.
example_ms: "Perlahan sedikit, kerana kita sudah hampir sampai ke simpang kampung yang kita mahu pergi. Jika kamu memandu dengan laju, kamu akan terlepas simpang tersebut," Bah Kakyeng berkata kepada Bah Melan.

example_semai: "Hemberekkan! Amen higaleh nek hikenale," Wak Reni kiurok nu ikenon litau de bipet ya igensir medei bibakek kinyok rukok.
example_en: "You deserved it! Stubborn people will certainly get their just desserts," Wak Reni said to her son who was beaten by her husband for smoking.
example_ms: "Padan muka! Orang yang degil memang mendapat balasan," Wak Reni berkata kepada anak terunanya yang dipukul oleh suaminya kerana kedapatan merokok.

example_semai: Tenek eng kijol hemog.
example_en: My brother hung the hammock.
example_ms: Abang saya menggantung buai rajut.

example_semai: Hempang ilei baha-beh ku Gupeg ya pek jadi medei mai kampug hik ku pajeh naiget.
example_en: The dam which was to be built in Gopeng was cancelled because the villagers there protested against it.
example_ms: Empangan yang mahu dibina di Gopeng tak jadi kerana orang kampung di situ melarangnya.

example_semai: "Heng..heng..heng!" tah ma kijel cok kelem jinek.
example_en: "Woof..woof...woof!" The dogs were barking last night.
example_ms: Bunyi anjing menyalak malam tadi.

definition_en: mobile phone; cell phone; hand phone
example_semai: Hunen, ya jeoi maay naiguna henpon.
example_en: Nowadays, many people are using mobile phones.
example_ms: Sekarang, sudah ramai orang yang menggunakan telefon bimbit.

definition_ms: hentak; menghentak sesuatu (kayu, kaki dll.) dengan kuat
example_semai: Bah Mining kijap sambel kihentak ijog.
example_en: Bah Mining cried as he jolted his foot.
example_ms: Bah Mining menangis sambil menghentak kakinya.

example_semai: "Mahentok de mong ku padeg hik. Bor hartoj tarog ha entek kelem doh," Wak Melah kiurok nu igensir.
example_en: "There's nothing in our house. Let's pick some tapioca leaves for a side dish for dinner tonight," Wak Melah told her husband.
example_ms: "Tiada apa yang ada di rumah kita. Baik kita memetik pucuk ubi bagi lauk kita malam nanti," Wak Melah berkata kepada suaminya.

definition_ms: kentut; angin yang dikeluarkan dari perut dari lubang dubur
example_semai: Hep adeh ges de kijadi mase caknak hik bihadam ku kateh ket.
example_en: Wind is gas produced by food digested in the abdomen.
example_ms: Kentut adalah gas yang terhasil akibat makanan dihadam di dalam perut.

example_semai: Apeka had manusia taleh de brenghep, tapi mong binatang juga de begei ajeh.
example_en: Not only do humans pass wind, but so do animals.
example_ms: Bukan hanya manusia sahaja yang kentut, tetapi terdapat juga binatang yang sedemikian.

definition_ms: heret; seret; menyeret; menarik sesuatu di atas tanah, lantai dll
example_semai: Bah Mali kipan her kerusi lei kijaga ianak buah de tengah kibukbet.
example_en: Bah Mali dragged the chair loudly until his nephew woke up from sleep.
example_ms: Bah Mali menyeret kerusi dengan kuat sehingga anak buahnya terbangun dari tidur.

example_semai: Ais ajeh deras kiher amen hidek liu ku luar.
example_en: Ice melts quickly when placed outside.
example_ms: Ais itu cepat mencair jika diletakkan di luar.

definition_en: to be surprised; to be startled; to be wondering
definition_ms: hairan; berasa pelik dan aneh kerana melihat atau mengalami sesuatu yang luarbiasa
example_semai: "Pek ngheran amen kek anen, mimang begei jijeh ikelaku," Wak Sunti ru Wak Era baiurok ha Wak Sila.
example_en: "I am not surprised at her, she naturally behaves like that," Wak Sunti and Wak Era talked about Wak Sila.
example_ms: "Saya tidak hairan dengan dia, dia memang berkelakuan sebegitu," Wak Sunti dan Wak Era bercakap mengenai Wak Sila.

definition_en: to creep; to drag oneself along the ground; to move slowly
example_semai: Ajok eng kihihet pelahat taleh ha pek lalu kicecip.
example_en: My grandmother dragged herself slowly along the ground because she couldn't walk.
example_ms: Nenek saya mengesot perlahan-lahan kerana tidak larat berjalan.

definition_en: we; 1st person plural (more than two, including the hearer) pronoun
definition_ms: kita; kata ganti nama diri pertama jamak inklusif ; 1st person singular pronoun
example_semai: "Hik hunen hiha-beh ma?" Bah Sudim kitanya ku itukeh.
example_en: "What should we do now?" Bah Sudim asked his boss.
example_ms: "Sekarang kita harus buat apa?" Bah Sudim bertanya pada majikannya.

example_semai: Jeoi hik Bah Karim ku imoh.
example_en: There are many blackheads on Bah Karim's nose.
example_ms: Ada banyak bintik hitam di hidung Bah Karim.

definition_en: to emerge; to appear; to float up
definition_ms: timbul; muncul atau naik ke atas dari dalam air; tidak tenggelam; naik ke permukaan (dari dalam air ke permukaan air)
example_semai: "Jeoi kak naihimul dui adeh," Bah Pedam kipeder nu Bah Rujen.
example_en: "There are many fish emerging at the surface this evening," Bah Pedam told Bah Rujen.
example_ms: "Banyak ikan timbul petang ini," Bah Pedam berkata kepada Bah Rujen.

example_semai: "Get hina kek, doh jeh kek lagi bejaye ju hik," Wak Sara kiurok nu ikekawat de selalo naihina ha Wak Mali de hajap.
example_en: "Don't despise him, he'll be more successful than we are," Wak Sara told her friends who always insulted Wak Mali who was poor.
example_ms: "Jangan hina dia, nanti dia lebih berjaya dari kita," Wak Sara berkata kepada rakan-rakannya yang selalu menghina Wak Mali yang miskin.

definition_en: henna; a type of shrub, its dampened leaves can be used as a dye and to color nails and hair
definition_ms: inai; sejenis pokok renek yang daunnya apabila dilumat hancur boleh dijadikan pewarna dan selalunya digunakan untuk mewarna kuku dan rambut
example_semai: Hinei adeh biasa bipakei ya penganten ku mase arik jekneg, naihias ha teg ru jog enai. Tapi, mase ku arik biasa pen mai kerdor biasa naipakei hinei adeh.
example_en: Henna is usually worn by the bride and groom on their wedding day, to decorate their hands and feet. However, on regular days women also wear henna.
example_ms: Inai biasanya dipakai oleh pengantin semasa hari perkahwinan, bagi menghias tangan dan kaki. Tapi, pada hari biasa pun orang perempuan biasa memakai inai.

example_semai: Hingar deg mai atah ha jengoi sanget ku musem cuti sekulah adeh.
example_en: Our neighbour's house up there is very noisy because there are many kids on school holidays now.
example_ms: Rumah orang di atas riuh-rendah kerana terdapat banyak kanak-kanak pada cuti sekolah sekarang.

example_semai: Hingge hunen pek jipanei Bah Karim ajeh booq ikenon.
example_en: Until now we do not know whose child Bah Karim is.
example_ms: Sehingga sekarang kami tidak mengetahui Bah Karim itu anak siapa.

definition_en: to care about; to pay attention to
example_semai: Ajok jik langsong pek kihirau ha jik bile jihol nu imadeg.
example_en: Our grandmother did not pay any attention to us when we came to her house.
example_ms: Nenek kami langsung tidak menghiraukan kami yang ke rumahnya.

definition_en: to be jealous; to be envious
example_semai: Wak Rita kihiri ha rantei mas de kilog ya Wak Masnah.
example_en: Wak Rita is envious of the gold chain worn by Wak Masnah.
example_ms: Wak Rita mencemburui rantai emas yang dipakai oleh Wak Masnah.

definition_ms: menghembus hingus keluar dari lubang hidung
example_semai: "His sih hek ajeh," Wak Diana kiurok ku ikenon.
example_en: "Blow your nose," Wak Diana told her son.
example_ms: "Hembus hingus kamu itu," Wak Diana berkata kepada anaknya.

definition_en: on a level (of); to the extent (of)
example_semai: "Koh hit anoh teu ajeh," apak kiurok nu Bah Adi.
example_en: "Pour the water only to this level," father told Bah Adi.
example_ms: "Tuang air pada takat ini sahaja," bapa berkata kepada Bah Adi.

example_semai: Bah Iwan makin arik makin gu hit iberog.
example_en: Day by day, Bah Iwan was getting fatter.
example_ms: Semakin hari bahan Bah Iwan semakin menggemuk.

example_semai: "Mase hek ha engrak doh, hahod hajadi ma?" apak eng kitanya ku kenon eng.
example_en: "When you are grown up, what do you want to be?" my father asked my daughter.
example_ms: "Jika kamu dewasa nanti, kamu mahu menjadi apa?" bapa bertanya kepada anak saya.

example_semai: Cok ajeh kihoh ha Bah Embi.
example_en: The dog was chasing Bah Embi.
example_ms: Anjing tersebut mengejar Bah Embi.

definition_en: no rain; dry season; scorching hot weather
definition_ms: musim panas; musim kering; cuaca panas terik

example_en: It is already dry season, no rain, for a week, so the laundry is easy to dry under the sun.
example_ms: Sudah seminggu musim kering tidak hujan, jemuran pun senang untuk mengering.

example_semai: Telas manik kihoj, baruleh buleh jisambong chet beneh lada.
example_en: After the rain stopped, only then could we continue to plant the pepper seeds.
example_ms: Setelah hujan berhenti, barulah kami boleh menyambung menanam benih lada.

definition_ms: sayang; cinta; kasih akan seseorang atau sesuatu

example_en: I love my parents.
example_ms: Saya menyayangi ibu dan bapa saya.

example_en: "After eating, dump the rambutan skin in the trash bin," mother told Wak Hima and her friends.
example_ms: "Selepas makan, buang kulit rambutan tersebut ke dalam tong sampah," ibu berpesan kepada Wak Hima dan rakan-rakannya.

example_semai: Bah Ali kihol nu itempat kerjak selalojeh ku pukol 9 pegelap.
example_en: Bah Ali usually arrives at work at 9am.

definition_en: container; a container for a storage
example_semai: "Kot hol, eng ngha-dek sej," apak kisemanj.
example_en: "Get a container, father asked," I want to keep the meat.
example_ms: "Ambil bekas, saya mahu simpan daging," ayah meminta.

example_semai: Mong hol kenik ku belakang almari.
example_en: There is a rat hole at the back of the closet.
example_ms: Terdapat lubang tikus di belakang almari.

definition_ms: kesan tapak kaki; jejak yang ditinggalkan (binatang, benda atau manusia)

example_en: While we were catapulting birds earlier, we saw a tiger's foot print in the forest.
example_ms: Semasa kami melastik burung tadi, kami nampak tapak kaki harimau di hutan.

definition_en: to exit; to leave; to go out
definition_ms: keluar; tidak lagi berada di dalam

example_en: "I'm going out, I'll be back soon," Wak Nani told us.
example_ms: "Saya keluar sebentar, nanti saya akan kembali lagi," Wak Nani memaklumkan kepada kami.

example_semai: Bah Deli kihong kicereg ha icok.

example_ms: Bah Deli bersiul memanggil anjingnya.

example_en: "I never feel satisfied when eating shellfish," Wak Pisah told Wak Siti.

example_semai: "Wak Sarah, et ham tarog nok," iamek kior. Wak Sarah kiberlag, "Hooh, mek."
example_en: "Wak Sarah, go and pick some tapioca shoots," her mother ordered. Wak Sarah replied, "Yes, mother."
example_ms: "Wak Sarah, pergi petik pucuk ubi kayu," ibunya menyuruh. Wak Sarah membalas "Ya, ibu."

example_semai: "Kradik panoh nok, ya nghop," Bah Leman kiurok nu iatok mase ubai cat cenan baiha-et nu dusot sempak.
example_en: "Take a break here for a moment, I'm out of breath," Bah Lemah told his grandfather as the two of them climbed the hill to go to the durian orchard.
example_ms: "Rehat di sini sekejap, saya sudah lelah," Bah Lemah memberitahu datuknya semasa mereka berdua mendaki bukit untuk ke kebun durian.

definition_en: to be suffocated due to strong odour
definition_ms: sesak nafas kerana bau yang kuat

example_en: I couldn't bear to enter Bah Zam's bedroom because I was suffocated by his cigarette smoke.
example_ms: Saya tidak tahan memasuki ke bilik tidur Bah Zam kerana nafas saya sesak oleh asap rokoknya.

definition_ms: hormat; mempunyai pandangan yang tinggi terhadap orang lain
example_semai: Hik kena hihormat mai de engrak ju hik.
example_en: We should respect those who are older than we are.
example_ms: Kita hendaklah menghormati orang yang lebih tua dari kita.

definition_ms: hospital; rumah sakit; tempat pesakit menerima perawatan
example_semai: Apak ru amek eng pai ubai jog ju hospitel, ubai sueg sedarak jik de kimuj wat.
example_en: My parents have just returned from the hospital, they visited our relative who has been admitted to the ward.
example_ms: Bapa dan ibu saya baharu pulang dari hospital, mereka melawat saudara kami yang dimasukkan ke dalam wad.

example_semai: "Ma huui hek?" Wak Milah ktanya nu Wak Samis.

example_semai: "Huat adeh amen ma-ma jadi ku hek, eng ngnyek betulog!" Wak Pia kiurok ku Wak Enis.
example_en: "Now, if anything happens to you, I will not help!" Wak Pia told Wak Enis.
example_ms: "Sekarang, jika terjadi apa-apa pada kamu, saya tidak akan menolong!" Wak Pia berkata kepada Wak Enis.

definition_ms: ubi; akar atau umbi yang membesar dan biasanya boleh dimakan (ubi kayu, ubi keledek dsb)

definition_en: to connect; to contact; to communicate
definition_ms: hubung; berkomunikasi dengan orang lain; ingin bercakap dengan orang lain

example_en: We can connect with people by phone.
example_ms: Kita boleh berhubung dengan orang jauh menggunakan telefon.

example_semai: Ku kampug eng walah pek berhug.
example_en: In my village there is no cave.
example_ms: Di kampung saya tidak ada gua.

definition_ms: hirup; menghirup (kuah masakan kari, sup dsb.)
example_semai: Ha bilir sedap, Bah Malu kipan huj kuah sop lei dij.
example_en: Because it was very delicious, Bah Malu sipped the soup until he finished it.
example_ms: Oleh kerana terlampau sedap, Bah Malu menghirup kuah sup sampai habis.

example_semai: Mai aska ajeh naiui latehan, naihuj timbok.

example_semai: Amen hijoi hukom, keral ru kerdor de pek ikenah atu igensir pek buleh ubai bet sama.
example_en: According to the law, men and women who are not married  should not sleep together.
example_ms: Jika menurut hukum, lelaki dan perempuan yang bukannya pasangan berkahwin tidak boleh tidur bersama.

example_semai: Ku jaman manah entem, keral ru kerdor de ubai bezinah buleh bihukom dat.
example_en: In ancient times, men and women who committed adultery could be sentenced to death.

definition_en: village regulations; regulations or code of the village; local custom
definition_ms: peraturan kampung; peraturan atau tatatertib kampung

example_en: We must follow village regulations if we want to live in this place for a long time.

definition_en: thud; describing something falling (balls, marbles, etc.)
example_semai: "Huldul, bula ajeh kiyog nu mati."
example_en: "Thud, the ball fell down."
example_ms: "Berdebap, bola itu jatuh ke bawah."

example_semai: "Humaka eng peder begei ajeh."
example_en: "That's what I told you."
example_ms: "Itulah saya beritahu begitu."

example_semai: "Jokle hunen hihol bakli," kenah eng kiajak.
example_en: "Let's go out shopping now," my wife suggested.
example_ms: "Marilah kita keluar membeli sekarang," isteri saya mengajak.

definition_ms: esok; besok; hari selepas hari ini

example_en: Tomorrow is Sunday.
example_ms: Esok adalah hari Ahad.

example_semai: Huper nek amek kiet beli laok ku pasar.
example_en: This morning my mother went to buy side-dishes at the market.
example_ms: Pagi tadi ibu pergi membeli lauk di pasar.

definition_en: two days hence; the day after tomorrow
example_semai: Huper uteh eng nghol nu madeg hek.
example_en: The day after tomorrow I'll go to your house.
example_ms: Lusa nanti saya akan ke rumah kamu.

definition_en: his; her; its; belonging to him/her/it
definition_ms: -nya; merupakan imbuhan ganti nama diri orang ketiga
example_semai: Bah Meran mong nik urag kenon. Ilei de sulog imuh Wak Sali. Ilei de kedua imuh Bah Gan. Ilei de iluj imuh Wak Manja.
example_en: Bah Meran has three children. The oldest is Wak Sali. The second is Bah Gan. The youngest is Wak Manja.
example_ms: Bah Meran mempunyai tiga orang anak. Anaknya yang sulung, namanya Wak Sali. Anaknya yang kedua, namanya Bah Gan. Anaknya yang bongsu, namanya Wak Manja.

example_semai: Kuceg eng kiperyos ikenon ayat ibek.

definition_en: the head of the representatives from the family for the marrige ceremony
definition_ms: ketua wakil dari keluarga dalam urusan perkahwinan

definition_ms: mengeja; menyebut dan menuliskan huruf secara berturutan dalam sesuatu perkataan

example_semai: "Sarak ilei pek eng canpor hal kek," Bah Mali kiberlag bile bitanya kisetujuka tok imenang kaha-jekjeg.

example_semai: "Baju ilei belok de hahod?" amek kitanyak nu Wak Lusi.

definition_en: to be concerned about; to pay attention to

definition_ms: ingat; tidak lupa; masih boleh memikirkan

definition_ms: insang; alat bernafas bagi kebanyakkan binatang yang hidup di dalam air

definition_ms: mengintai; lihat dengan bersembunyi-sembunyi untuk mengetahui gerak-geri sesuatu

definition_ms: merasa lemas atau sesak tidak dapat bernafas

example_semai: Keral ajeh iranak ku tempat adeh.

definition_ms: isi; kandungan sesuatu benda, objek dsb.

example_en: In my village there are Christians, Muslims and people who still adhere to traditional beliefs.
example_ms: Di kampung saya terdapat orang yang Kristian, orang yang Islam dan orang yang masih mempercayai kepercayaan tradisi.

example_semai: Istane ajeh tempat ginei raja.

example_ms: Istana adalah tempat kediaman bagi raja.

example_semai: Dewan ajeh it ya sengoi naiha-daftar undi.

example_ms: Dewan itu sesak dengan orang yang mahu mendaftar undi.

example_semai: Apak eng iitam ku kateh iberadek.

definition_en: name usually for the sixth child in a family
definition_ms: (I-) panggilan atau gelaran nama yang mengikut turutan kelahiran untuk anak ke-enam dalam keluarga

example_semai: "'Iyeu, iyeu,' kuceg eng kijap kisemanj caknak," Bah Man kiurok.
example_en: "'Meow, meow,' my cat cries for food," Bah Man said.
example_ms: "'Miau, miau,' kucing saya menangis meminta makanan," Bah Man berkata.

example_semai: "Ngtumpang isi butol teu eng adeh ku kateh jaben hek," Wak Mila kiurok nu Wak Nia.

example_semai: Renes Bah Rael ajeh begei jabeong.

example_ms: Bah Rael mempunyai muka iras seperti orang Melayu.

example_semai: Amek pog ajeh kijabug Bah Nami mendei ha kikacau ikekenon.
example_en: The mother hen attacked Bah Nami because he harassed her chicks.
example_ms: Ibu ayam tersebut menyerang Bah Nami kerana mengganggu anak-anaknya.

example_semai: "Jadi hunen, hik kena hipoj ilei kihol baruleh hipanei ma ikeputusan," Bah Man kiurok.
example_en: "So now, we have to wait for him to come before we know the outcome," Bah Man said.
example_ms: "Jadi sekarang, kita mesti menunggu dia datang baru kita tahu keputusannya," Bah Man berkata.

definition_ms: pijak; memijak; meletak, menekan kaki ke atas sesuatu
example_semai: "Aduh! Nyaknik kijag jog eng," Wak Mira kikarau.
example_en: "Ouch! It hurts, she has stepped on my foot," Wak Mira shouted.
example_ms: "Aduh! Sakitnya dia pijak kaki saya," Wak Mira menjerit.

definition_en: to be alert; to guard; to take care of
definition_ms: jaga; berjaga; menjaga sesuatu supaya selamat
example_semai: Hik kena hibejaga-jaga medei mai jehat bile-bile taleh naitiba.
example_en: We must be on our guard because bad people will come at any time.
example_ms: Kita hendaklah berjaga-jaga kerana orang jahat akan datang pada bila-bila masa sahaja.

definition_ms: tidak tidur; sudah bangkit dari tidur

example_semai: Ku Cameron Highland mong jeoi bijual jagug.
example_en: In the Cameron Highlands there is lots of corn for sale.
example_ms: Di Cameron Highland terdapat banyak jagung dijual.

definition_en: Jahut; an Orang Asli group living in Pahang
definition_ms: Jahut; salah satu suku kaum masyarakat Orang Asli yang tinggal di negeri Pahang
example_semai: "Kenah tenek eng mai Jahot," Wak Yan kipasal nu jik.
example_en: "My brother's wife is a Jahut," Wak Yan told us.
example_ms: "Isteri abang saya adalah orang Jahut," Wak Yan memberitahu kepada kami.

definition_en: type of jungle banana; wild banana
definition_ms: pisang hutan; sejenis pisang yang banyak tumbuh di dalam hutan dan saiznya kecil sedikit dari pisang biasa
example_semai: Jeoi keneng jai ku kebun amek eng.
example_en: There are many wild banana trees in my mother's garden.
example_ms: Terdapat banyak pokok pisang hutan di kebun ibu saya.

definition_en: to pull something such as grass with hands
definition_ms: mencabut sesuatu sebagai rumput dengan tangan
example_semai: Wak Sila kijaj rumpot ku idepan deg.
example_en: Wak Sila pulled up the grass in front of her house with her hands.
example_ms: Wak Sila mencabut rumput di depan rumahnya.

example_semai: "Jajak ajeh bok imai?" Bah Tam kitanya. "Ajeh enjop eng," kiberlag ya Wak Eri.
example_en: "Who is that old lady?" Bah Tam asked. "That is my grandmother," Wak Eri replied.
example_ms: "Perempuan tua itu siapa?" Bah Tam menanya. "Itu nenek saya," Wak Eri membalas.

definition_en: to peddle; to sell at a stall
definition_ms: menjaja; membawa barangan dagangan untuk dijual di merata-rata tempat
example_semai: Ku hospital pek buleh hijaje ma-ma pen ibarang.
example_en: In the hospital it is prohibited to peddle any form of goods.
example_ms: Di hospital dilarang menjaja sebarang bentuk barangan.

definition_ms: baring; tidur tetapi tidak melelapkan mata
example_semai: "Eng jajik nok, gikdoh baru hicip," tenek eng kiurok nu ikenah.
example_en: "I'll lie down for a bit, and then we'll go," my brother told his wife.
example_ms: "Saya baring sekejap, nanti barulah kita jalan," abang saya memberitahu kepada isterinya.

example_semai: Pog jik bicak ya jajog kelem jihnek.
example_en: Our chicken was eaten by a civet last night.
example_ms: Ayam kami dimakan oleh musang malam tadi.

example_semai: "Jak eng mai Kampug Kakuh," Wak Ani kipasal.
example_en: "My mother-in-law is from Kakuh village," Wak Ani told me.
example_ms: "Ibu mertua saya orang Kampung Kakuh," Wak Ani memberitahu.

definition_en: Jakun; an Orang Asli group living primarily in Pahang
definition_ms: Jakun; masyarakat Orang Asli yang kebanyakkannya tinggal di negeri Pahang
example_semai: "Eng mong kawat mai Jakon," Bah Selah kipasal.
example_en: "I have a Jakun friend," Bah Selah told us.
example_ms: "Saya ada kawan orang Jakun," Bah Selah memberitahu.

definition_ms: jala; sejenis alat yang di perbuat dari tali benang kasar dan digunakan untuk menangkap ikan dengan cara menebarnya ke permukaan air
example_semai: Pegelap cenek tenek eng kikot jala.
example_en: This morning my brother bought a net.
example_ms: Pagi tadi abang saya membeli jala.

example_semai: "Wak Jangkor kiet nu ikerjak kijalagka?" ajok kitanya nu amek.
example_en: "Does Wak Jangkor commute to work?" grandmother asked mother.
example_ms: "Wak Jangkor pergi kerja berulang-alikah?" nenek bertanya kepada ibu.

example_semai: "Diya!" Wak Kurti kicereg. "Jalek bihod?" kibalas ya Wak Diya.
example_en: "Diya!" Wah Kurti called. "What do you want?" Wak Diya answered.
example_ms: "Diya!" Wah Kurti memanggil. "Mahu apa?" Wak Diya menjawab.

definition_en: water container; container made of bamboo to store water
definition_ms: bekas air; bekas bagi menyimpan air yang diperbuat daripada buluh

example_en: My father was looking for bamboo to make a water container.
example_ms: Ayah saya mencari buluh bagi membuat bekas simpanan air.

definition_ms: jam; alat yang menunjukkan masa atau waktu
example_semai: Amen titok jam hikedei hunen tah pukol merip.
example_en: If there is no clock we don't know what time it is.
example_ms: Jika tiada jam kita tidak tahu sekarang pukul berapa.

definition_en: age; times; particular period of time in history
example_semai: Ku jaman hunen, ya susah hiha-bakek mai biguna kerite lembu.

definition_ms: pondok kecil sebagai tempat beristirahat; pondok sementara

definition_ms: jamu; menjamu (makanan atau minuman untuk orang lain atau tetamu yang berkunjung ke rumah)
