example_ms: Loceng sekolah sudah berdering, maksudnya waktu persekolahan telah tamat.

example_semai: Mase nget nu Sabah, eng ngbakek Lod Kinabalu ku kennyak.
example_en: When I went to Sabah, I saw Mount Kinabalu from afar.
example_ms: Semasa saya pergi ke Sabah, saya nampak Gunung Kinabalu dari jauh.

example_semai: Kui kuceg ajeh log ku pagar.
example_en: The cat's head was stuck in the fence.
example_ms: Kepala kucing tersebut tersekat di pagar.

example_semai: Bah Man kigemar log seluar jeans.
example_en: Bah Man likes to wear jeans.
example_ms: Bah Man suka memakai seluar jeans.

definition_en: particle giving emphasis to the root word or phrase
definition_ms: partikel yang diletakkan di akhiran sesuatu perkataan bagi menekan maksud perkataan tersebut
example_semai: Ajehloh ilei!
example_en: That's him!
example_ms: Itulah dia!

example_semai: Bah Yan kigemar liloi ku kulam.
example_en: Bah Yan likes to swim in the pool.
example_ms: Bah Yan suka berenang di kolam renang.

example_semai: "Bile haloi madeh?" Bah Wan kitanya nu Bah Jali.
example_en: "When did you arrive here?" Bah Wan asked Bah Jali.
example_ms: "Bila kamu sampai ke sini?" Bah Wan bertanya kepada Bah Jali.

example_semai: "Seh bawang adeh loi hancor," amek kipasal nu menang jik.
example_en: "Pound this onion until it is crushed," mum said to our sister.
example_ms: "Tumbuk bawang ini sehingga hancur," ibu berkata kepada adik kami.

example_semai: Sog Wak Mila biloj ya iamek medei ha jeoi icek.
example_en: Wak Mila's hair was shaved off by her mother because there were a lot of lice.
example_ms: Rambut Wak Mila dicukur oleh ibunya kerana terdapat banyak kutu.

definition_en: to try to get loose from bonds or from someone's clasp
definition_ms: meronta-ronta; berusaha melepaskan diri daripada pegangan
example_semai: Becog ajeh kiloj ju empag singha.
example_en: The mousedeer is trying to loose itself from the lion's mouth.
example_ms: Kancil tersebut meronta-ronta di mulut singa.

example_semai: Bah Atang ajeh kenon iloj.
example_en: Bah Atang is the youngest child.
example_ms: Bah Atang itu anak bongsu.

definition_ms: zakar; kemaluan bagi lelaki atau jantan
example_semai: Lok cok ajeh luka medei bikap mase naibelawan ratak liknai.
example_en: The dogs' genitals were injured because they were bitten while they were fighting with each other.
example_ms: Kemaluan anjing tersebut luka kerana digigit semasa mereka berlawan sesama sendiri.

example_semai: Eng lok kekdeh jar et madeh.
example_en: I came here with him.
example_ms: Saya dengan dia datang ke sini.

example_semai: "Eng kenon ilong ku kateh peringak eng," Bah Gani kipasal nu ikekawat.
example_en: "I am the oldest child in my family" Bah Gani told his friends.
example_ms: "Saya anak yang sulung dalam keluarga saya," Bah Gani memberitahu rakan-rakannya.

definition_en: name usually for the oldest child in a family
definition_ms: (L-) panggilan atau gelaran nama yang mengikut turutan kelahiran untuk anak sulung
example_semai: Ileli kek ilong ajeh imuh Bah Fira.
example_en: The name of the oldest child is Bah Fira.
example_ms: Yang sulung itu namanya Bah Fira.

example_semai: Bah Badal iteninoi ya long iapak.
example_en: Bah Badal is already the same size as his father.
example_ms: Saiz Bah Badal sudah sebesar bapanya.

definition_ms: gulung; menggulung; benda yang berlembar-lembar dan dapat dilipat untuk dijadikan bentuk silinder (kertas; daun dsb)
example_semai: Amekjop kilor ibakau sebelom mula kijinjaaj.
example_en: My grandmother rolled her tobacco before starting to weed.
example_ms: Nenek saya menggulung tembakaunya sebelum mulai mencabut rumput.

example_semai: Mat langsat ajeh ya lor, pek liu nij doh ya buleh bicet.
example_en: The langsat seed have sprouted, soon they can be planted.
example_ms: Biji langsat tersebut sudah bertunas, tidak lama lagi ianya boleh ditanam.

definition_en: to slither on the chest; to army-crawl
example_semai: Kenon eng ya kilor.
example_en: My baby already knows how to army-crawl.
example_ms: Bayi saya sudah pandai menyusur.

example_semai: "Tuntop butol adeh ya los," tenek eng kipasal nu amek.
example_en: "The bottle cap is already loose," my sister told my mother.
example_ms: "Penutup botol ini sudah longgar," kakak saya memberitahu ibu.

example_semai: Jik jilos nij nu madeg, pek jadi jihol nu pekan medei gertak ku nong entoi kitateh ya manik ru beag.
example_en: We returned home, not being able to go out to the town because the bridge on the main road had collapsed due to rain and flooding.
example_ms: Kami kembali ke rumah, tidak jadi kami mahu keluar ke pekan kerana jambatan yang di jalan utama sudah runtuh akibat hujan dan banjir.

example_semai: Mat jik lot jineng sanget ajeh kiyog.
example_en: Our eyes widened when we saw the boy fall.
example_ms: Mata kami terbeliak apabila melihat budak itu jatuh.

example_semai: Sanget pai yos kilot imem.
example_en: Newborns suck their milk.
example_ms: Bayi yang baru lahir menghisap susunya.

definition_ms: terang; jelas; nampak nyata (berkenaan dengan penglihatan)
example_semai: "Tules aentoi gek, pek lou ngbaca," apak kipasal nu menang eng.
example_en: "Write bigger, it's not clear enough for me to read it," my father said to my sister.
example_ms: "Tulis besar sedikit, tidak jelas saya membacanya," bapa berkata kepada adik saya.

example_semai: Bah Samil kitanya nu ikenah, cengroi mongka tok? Ikenah kibalas, "Ya luak."
example_en: Bah Samil asked his wife, "is there any rice left or not?" His wife replied, "it's getting less."
example_ms: Bah Samil bertanya kepada isterinya, beras masih ada atau tidak? Isterinya membalas, "Sudah berkurangan."

example_semai: Amek Wak Nani kigemar ha bunga. Ku kateh deg enai ru ku luar deg enai penuh ya bunga taleh.
example_en: Wak Nani's mother loves flowers. The insite and outsite of their house is just filled with flowers.
example_ms: Ibu Wak Nani menyukai bunga. Di dalam rumah dan di luar rumah mereka dipenuhi dengan bunga sahaja.

example_semai: "Padag kampug jik ihad luas, ngrasa muat nar padag bula," Bah Liko kipasal nu jik.
example_en: "The field in our village is very spacious, I think it could accommodate as many as two football fields," Bah Liko told us.
example_ms: "Padang di kampung kami sangat luas, saya rasa boleh memuatkan sebanyak dua padang bola sepak," Bah Liko memberitahu kepada kami.

example_semai: Nong nu Jalan Pahang jeoi lubang.
example_en: There are many potholes in the road in Jalan Pahang.
example_ms: Terdapat banyak lubang pada jalanraya di Jalan Pahang.

definition_ms: tanya berulang-ulang; menyoal suspek berulang-ulang untuk mendapat bukti
example_semai: Mase pulis naisiasat ha mai jahat, enai mesti nailuej lei mai jahat ajeh kimengaku.
example_en: While the police were investigating the bad guys, they must have asked them repeatedly until the bad guys confessed.
example_ms: Semasa polis menyiasat orang jahat, mereka mesti bertanya berulang kali sehingga orang jahat tersebut mengaku.

example_semai: Bah Adok kilug lei kaha-hol iteu mat.
example_en: Bah Adok laughed until he cried.
example_ms: Bah Adok ketawa sampai matanya berair.

example_semai: Mase arik beket, tenek keral eng kirasa beket ru kiluig.
example_en: When the weather is hot, my elder brother feels hot and dizzy.
example_ms: Semasa cuaca yang panas abang saya merasa panas dan pening.

example_semai: Wak Sali kilulos temuduga jihnek.
example_en: Wak Sali passed the interview earlier.
example_ms: Wak Sali lulus temuduga tadi.

example_semai: "Ku kampug eng mong jeoi lumbong," Bah Nan kipasal nu jik.
example_en: "In my village there are many abandoned tin mines," Bah Nan told us.
example_ms: "Di kampung saya terdapat banyak lombong tinggal," Bah Nan memberitahu kami.

example_semai: "Yayak lumom, pek kipanei ma-ma, tiba-tiba biles," Wak Sali kipasal ha Bah Meran nu jik, de biles ya Cekgu Ramlah medei mong sampah ku imije, tapi binakal ya sanget kilek de naipej sampah ku mije kek.
example_en: "Pity him, he didn't know anything about it, he was suddenly scolded just like that," Wak Sali told us about Bah Meran who was scolded by Cikgu Ramlah because there was rubbish on his desk. But there were other students who played a prank on him by throwing rubbish on his desk.
example_ms: "Kasihan dia, tidak tahu apa-apa tiba-tiba dimarahi begitu sahaja," Wak Sali memberitahu kami mengenai Bah Meran yang dimarahi oleh Cikgu Ramlah kerana terdapat sampah di mejanya. Tetapi terdapat pelajar lain yang menganiaya beliau dengan membuang sampah ke mejanya.

example_semai: "Agok main lumpat-lumpat, doh tesilap engkejer," atok kipasal nu jik.
example_en: "Don't jump around, you might fall," grandfather told us.
example_ms: "Jangan bermain lompat-lompat, nanti tersilap kalian boleh terjatuh," datuk berkata kepada kami.

example_semai: "Ihat iluniu bas kaha-hol, hik ya lingah hiha-et nu kerjak hunen," Bah Wira kimengeloh nu ikekawat.
example_en: "The bus is slow coming, we are already late to get to work now," Bah Wira complained to his friends.
example_ms: "Lambatnya bas mahu datang, kita sudah terlambat mahu ke tempat kerja sekarang," Bah Wira mengeluh pada rakan-rakannya.

example_semai: Bah Mali kikerjak sebegei pemandu luri.
example_en: Bah Mali works as a truck driver.
example_ms: Bah Mali berkerja sebagai pemandu lori.

example_semai: Kedei pelek biasajeh ku tepi lurong.
example_en: The customary fruit shop is on the side of the road.
example_ms: Kedai buah biasa yang terdapat di tepi lorong.

example_semai: Sog Bah Tam luros, tapi sog imenang kerinteng.
example_en: Bah Tam's hair was straight, but his sister's hair was curly.
example_ms: Rambut Bah Tam lurus, tetapi rambut adiknya kerinting.

example_semai: Ikui lut medei kiyog ku kemil batu.
example_en: His head became swollen because he fell on a rock.
example_ms: Kepalanya membengkak kerana terjatuh di atas batu.

example_semai: Ma igah ?
example_en: How are you? (lit. What is the news?)
example_ms: Apa khabar?

example_semai: Bah Sarip kisemanj maap ku Bah Aris ha ubai begadoh jihnek.
example_en: Bah Sarip asked Bah Aris for forgiveness because they had been squabbling just beforehand.
example_ms: Bah Sarip meminta maaf kepada Bah Aris kerana mereka bergaduh sebentar tadi.

example_semai: "Tugas mabek keral, bior terbor kak, pog ru sayo. Tugas mabek kerdor, bior berencet. Tugas mabek bujang, bior siksuj," tok penghulu kipasal tugas-tugas jik mase caknak teniba Menteri Besar nu kampug jik ku gecek keknit doh.
example_en: "The job of the men is to clean and prepare the fish, the chicken and the vegetables. The job of the women is to cook. The job of the unmarried is to wash all the dishes and utensils," the village chief told each group our duties during the feast in conjunction with the visitation of the Chief Minister to our village next month.
example_ms: "Tugas golongan lelaki adalah menyiang ikan, ayam dan sayur. Tugas golongan perempuan adalah memasak. Tugas golongan bujang adalah mencuci segala pinggan dan mangkuk," tok penghulu memberitahu tugas-tugas kami semasa kenduri sempena ketibaan Menteri Besar ke kampung kami pada bulan hadapan.

definition_en: bachang, horse mango, a type of fruit like mango that has a very strong smell
definition_ms: bacang; sejenis buah seperti mangga yang mempunyai bau yang amat kuat dan isinya bersabut
example_semai: Pelek macag keer irasa.
example_en: Horse mango tastes sour.
example_ms: Buah bacang rasanya masam.

example_semai: Ku pasar tani mong macam-macam jenis sayo bijual.
example_en: There are various types of vegetables sold in farmers' markets.
example_ms: Terdapat pelbagai jenis sayur dijual di pasar tani.

example_semai: Kenon kucig ilei pai yos ajeh macot ru kemek.
example_en: The newly born kitten is small and cute.
example_ms: Anak kucing yang baru lahir itu kecil dan comel.

example_semai: Madu ceet irasa.
example_en: Honey tastes sweet.
example_ms: Madu manis rasanya.

definition_en: to rush; to be in a hurry
example_semai: Bah Rizal kimagij kaha-et nu ikerejak.
example_en: Bah Risal was in a hurry to go to work.
example_ms: Bah Risal tergesa-gesa mahu ke tempat kerja.

definition_en: person or people belonging to a district or country
example_semai: "Ju ipenakei ru icare, ipanei kek ajeh mai ju bandar," Wak Nani kipasal nu Wak Jamilah ha pelajar pai ku kelas enai.
example_en: "From her clothes and the way she acts, we know that she is a person from the city," Wak Nani told Wak Jamilah about the new students in their class.
example_ms: "Dari pakaiannya dan gerak-gerinya, kita tahu bahawa dia itu ialah penghuni dari bandar," Wak Nani memberitahu Wak Jamilah berkenaan pelajar baharu di kelas mereka.

example_semai: Cekgu kelas eng mai Cina.
example_en: My class's teacher is from the Chinese race.
example_ms: Guru kelas saya berbangsa Cina.

example_semai: Mai jengoi ateh tengah nai jala kak.
example_en: A lot of people are fishing.
example_ms: Orang ramai itu sedang menjala ikan.

definition_en: "our people"; expression by the Semai to call their own race; Indigenous people; Orang Asli
definition_ms: orang kita; panggilan oleh Orang Semai kepada bangsa sendiri; Orang Asli
example_semai: "Kek ajeh mai ma? Ngtanya medei irenis pek begei ha mai hik, tapi buleh kibelwal engrok hik," Wak Hani kitanya nu Bah Tam.
example_en: "What is her race? I am asking because she does not look like the our people, but she can speak our language," Wak Hani asked Bah Tam.
example_ms: "Dia orang apa? Saya bertanya kerana rupanya tidak seperti orang kita Orang Asli, tetapi dia boleh bercakap dalam bahasa kita," Wak Hani bertanya kepada Bah Tam.

example_semai: Wak Risa kien ikekenon main ku taman permainan jap-jap dui.
example_en: Wak Risa brings her children to play in the children's playground every evening.
example_ms: Wak Risa membawa anak-anaknya bermain di taman permainan kanak-kanak pada setiap petang.

example_semai: "Patutle ngrasa relahej ku berog eng, paknya neh mong maj," Bah Jamil kipasal nu ikenah.
example_en: "I should feel my body itching, apparently there are chicken mites on my body," Bah Jamil told his wife.
example_ms: "Patutlah saya rasa badan saya gatal-gatal, rupa-rupanya terdapat hama ayam pada badan saya," Bah Jamil memberitahu isterinya.

example_semai: "Agok pej sampah nu majeh-manen, eng pai ngsapu!" tenek eng kiles memenang eng.
example_en: "Don't throw litter everywhere, I have just swept the floor!" My older sister scolded my younger siblings.
example_ms: "Jangan buang sampah merata-rata, saya baru menyapu!" kakak saya memarahi adik-adik saya.

definition_en: getting more or better, changing increasingly or progressively
example_semai: Bunga de ngcet ajeh gumakin entoi.
example_en: The flowers I planted are getting progressively bigger.
example_ms: Bunga yang saya tanam itu semakin membesar.

example_semai: Bah Rani bior jog madeg ya iamek, tapi kek kicip nij nu makgat.
example_en: Bah Rani was told to go home by his mother, but he still went elsewhere.
example_ms: Bah Rani disuruh untuk pulang ke rumah oleh ibunya, tetapi dia masih pergi ke tempat lain.

example_semai: Bah Sarul pek kipanei makne de biajar ya icikgu ajeh neh.
example_en: Bah Sarul does not know the meaning of the things taught by his teacher just now.
example_ms: Bah Sarul tidak tahu makna perkara yang diajar oleh cikgunya sebentar tadi.

example_semai: "Ya merip gencek hamakok?" Wak Ramlah kitanya nu Wak Nani.
example_en: "How many months have you been pregnant?" Wak Ramlah asked Wak Nani.
example_ms: "Sudah berapa bulan kamu mengandung?" Wak Ramlah bertanya kepada Wak Nani.

definition_ms: maksud; erti; tujuan; hajat; kehendak; niat
example_semai: "Ma maksot hek habual begei ajeh?" Bah Selah kitanya nu ikenah.
example_en: "What do you mean by that?" Bah Selah asked his wife.
example_ms: "Apa maksud kamu berkata sedemikian?" Bah Selah bertanya kepada isterinya.

example_semai: "Eng pernah ngburu malikat!" Bah Jali kiurok begei ajeh kaha-beseluro ru ikekawat.
example_en: "I've seen angels before!" Bah Jali said this because he wanted to joke with his friends.
example_ms: "Saya pernah melihat malaikat!" Bah Jali berkata sedemikian kerana mahu bergurau dengan kawan-kawannya.

example_semai: "Malok hek dek arap eng neh?" Wak Nee kitanya nu Wak Wajah.
example_en: "Where did you put my stuff earlier?" Wak Nee asked Wak Wajah.
example_ms: "Di mana kamu menyimpan barang saya tadi?" Wak Nee bertanya kepada Wak Wajah.

example_semai: "Mase jik jitengroj, jik jiburu mamek kilalu ku englag teu. Teros jijog madeg," amek kipasal nu ajok jik.
example_en: "While we were fishing, we saw a tiger pass by on the other side of the river. We immediately went home," my mother told our grandmother.
example_ms: "Semasa kami memancing, kami melihat seekor harimau sedang lalu di seberang sungai. Kami segera pulang ke rumah," ibu memberitahu nenek kami.

example_semai: Eng mamek bile ngngot penglai adeh.
example_en: This medicine suits me.
example_ms: Ubat ini serasi dengan saya.

example_semai: Mambek sanget naigemar man brencep-brencep.
example_en: Kids love to play hide-and-seek.
example_ms: Kanak-kanak suka bermain sorok-sorok.

definition_en: thing or animal that one takes care of, as a pet
example_semai: Bah Jali mong kidek man kenon rau.
example_en: Bah Jali keeps a baby monkey as a pet.
example_ms: Bah Jali menyimpan anak kera sebagai haiwan peliharaan.

example_semai: Wak Salmah kitod baju-baju de ya manah ru repot.
example_en: Wak Salmah burned old and decaying clothes.
example_ms: Wak Salmah membakar pakaian yang sudah lama dan mereput.

example_semai: Wah Milah ajeh ya liu kimanang.
example_en: Wah Milah has long been barren.
example_ms: Wah Milah sudah lama menjadi mandul.

example_semai: Ku kemil pelog ajeh jeoi deg maneng.
example_en: There are many spider webs on the roof.
example_ms: Di atas bumbung itu terdapat banyak sarang labah-labah.

example_semai: Sentak mangai ajeh bebisa.
example_en: The scorpion's tail is very venomous.
example_ms: Ekor kala jengking sangat berbisa.

example_semai: "Pelek mangge de bicet ya apak adeh keer," Bah Wan kiurok nu iamek.
example_en: "The mango that my father planted is sour," Bah Wan told his mother.
example_ms: "Buah mangga yang ditanam oleh ayah ini masam," Bah Wan berkata kepada ibunya.

example_semai: Mangges adeh ceet irasa.
example_en: This mangosteen tastes sweet.
example_ms: Buah manggis ini rasanya manis.

example_semai: Manghel de bebisa adeh ilei de besentol.
example_en: Poisonous spiders are hairy ones.
example_ms: Labah-labah yang berbisa adalah labah-labah yang berbulu.

example_semai: Wak Ramlah tesilap kicereh mangkok kaca mase kisiksuj, mangkok ajeh yog ju iteg de selej ya sabon.
example_en: Wak Ramlah mistakenly broke the bowl while washing the dishes. The bowl fell from her hands which were slippery because of the soap.
example_ms: Wak Ramlah tersilap memecahkan mangkuk kaca semasa mencuci pinggan. Mangkuk tersebut jatuh dari tangannya yang licin kerana sabun.

example_semai: Yah ajeh kimanik lebat ku Langkap.
example_en: It rained heavily in Langkap today.
example_ms: Hari ini hujan lebat di Langkap.

example_semai: Jik sanget manah jigemar muh teu manik.
example_en: When we were little kids, we liked playing in rain.
example_ms: Kami kecil-kecil dulu suka mandi air hujan.

example_semai: "Seumur sinui eng pek pernah ngneng manik relog, had pernah ngbakek ku TV taleh," ajok eng kipasal nu jik mase tengah jineng TV.
example_en: "All my life I have never seen hail, only seen it on TV," my grandmother told us while we were watching TV.
example_ms: "Seumur hidup saya tidak pernah melihat hujan batu, cuma pernah melihatnya di kaca TV sahaja," nenek saya memberitahu kepada kami semasa kami sedang menonton TV.

definition_en: to perform a Semai custom which is the event that a man sleeps for 3 days at a house of a woman who he intends to marry
definition_ms: melakukan adat Orang Semai iaitu peristiwa dimana lelaki tidur di rumah perempuan yang dia berniat untuk kahwini selama 3 hari
example_semai: "Keral tenek eng ya kihol nu madeg jik kaha-manjat ha tenek eng," Bah Deli kipasal nu jik.
example_en: "My sister's boyfriend has come to our house to sleep with my sister," Bah Deli told us.
example_ms: "Teman lelaki kakak saya sudah datang ke rumah kami untuk tidur kakak saya," Bah Deli memberitahu kepada kami.

example_semai: Bah Sani adeh kenon manja.
example_en: Bah Sani is a spoiled child.
example_ms: Bah Sani adalah anak manja.

example_semai: Muh manok Wah Enje ajeh, Wah Ida.
example_en: Wak Enje's sister-in-law's name is Wak Ida.
example_ms: Nama kakak ipar Wak Enje ialah Wak Ida.

definition_en: rattan manau, a type of rattan
example_semai: Apak eng kikek cog manou ku jeres, kaha-jual kaha-berkep duet.
example_en: My father looked for rattan manau in the forest to sell it to earn money.
example_ms: Ayah saya mencari rotan manau di hutan bagi menjualnya untuk mendapatkan wang.

example_semai: Manusia ku bumi adeh bemacam-macam bangsa ru engrok memaség.
example_en: Humans on this earth are made up of many different tribes and their respective languages.
example_ms: Manusia di bumi ini terdiri bermacam suku bangsa dan bahasa masing-masing.

definition_ms: saiz; sebaya; ukuran berkenaan besar atau kecilnya sesuatu benda seperti pakaian; tubuh badan atau objek;
example_semai: "Mar belok hek hakep kak lampap cinek?" Bah Gani kitanya nu Bah Rilan.
example_en: "How big is the lampam fish that you caught earlier?" Bah Gani asked Bah Rilan.
example_ms: "Sebesar mana ikan lampam yang kamu dapat tadi?" Bah Gani bertanya kepada Bah Rilan.

definition_en: to flare up (of a flame); to spread (of fire)
definition_ms: marak (api yang sedang membakar dengan kuat dan besar nyalaannya)
example_semai: Bah Man kitod sampah, tapi pek kisedar ios ajeh ya gumaken marak ku tempat kilek.
example_en: Bah Man burned the rubbish, but he did not realize the fire was spreading to other places.
example_ms: Bah Man membakar sampah, tetapi dia tidak menyedari api tersebut semakin marak ke tempat yang lain.

example_ms: Wak Hani mempunyai dua orang anak, seorang lelaki, seorang perempuan.

example_semai: Mong jeoi mon kicuai ku kenog Bah Kaling.
example_en: There are many pimples growing on Bah Kaling's back.
example_ms: Terdapat banyak jerawat yang tumbuh di belakang badan Bah Kaling.

definition_en: to be located; to have; to exist
example_semai: Ku Kuala Lumpur mong jeoi tempat kek de bor-bor, kek de buleh hiroh.
example_en: In Kuala Lumpur there are many interesting places we can visit.
example_ms: Di Kuala Lumpur terdapat banyak tempat yang menarik yang boleh kita lawati.

definition_en: to be alive; not to be dead yet
example_semai: "Apak jop ru amek jop eng lekat mong, tapi ubai ya engrak bernor," kiberlag ya Wak Nola bile bitanya ya ikawat kerjak.
example_en: "My grandparents are still alive, but they are very old," Wak Nola replied when asked by a colleague.
example_ms: "Datuk dan nenek saya masih hidup, tetapi mereka sudah amat tua," dijawab oleh Wak Nola bila ditanyai oleh kawan sekerjanya.

definition_ms: mop; sejenis penyapu untuk mengelap, membasuh lantai dan diperbuat dari lilitan benang-benang kain dan kayu digunakan sebagai batang pemegangnya
example_semai: "Amen haet nu kedei, tulog beli jehuk mop. Ilei de manah ya begot," Wak Gina kipasal nu igensir.
example_en: "If you go to the store, please buy a mop handle. The old one is broken," Wak Gina told her husband.
example_ms: "Jika kamu ke kedai, tolong belikan batang mop. Batang mop yang lama sudah patah," Wak Gina memberitahu kepada suaminya.

example_semai: Wak Sali tengah kimop ideg mase nghol nu majeh.
example_en: Wak Sali was mopping her house when I arrived there.
example_ms: Wak Sali sedang mengemop rumahnya semasa saya datang ke situ.

definition_en: serow, a type of wild goat that lives in the forests
definition_ms: kambing gurun, sejenis kambing liar yang tinggal di dalam hutan tebal
example_semai: "Kelem jiknek eng ngcerengai engrok mor ju jeres ku belakang deg jik," Bah Jali kipasal nu ikekawat.
example_en: "Last night I heard the sound of a serow from the forest behind our house," Bah Jali told his friends.
example_ms: "Malam tadi saya mendengar bunyi kambing gurun dari hutan di belakang rumah kami," Bah Jali memberitahu kepada rakan-rakannya.

example_semai: Entem, mase pekseg mong teu pili, jik jimuh ku teu perigi.
example_en: In the past, when there was no tap water, we bathed in well water.
example_ms: Dahulu, semasa belum ada air paip, kami mandi di air perigi.

example_semai: "Muh eng Wak Rosni," pelajar pai ku kelas jik kipekenal idiri.
example_en: "My name is Wak Rosni," said a new student in our class as she introduced herself.
example_ms: "Nama saya Wak Rosni," pelajar baharu di kelas kami memperkenalkan dirinya.

example_semai: Mai ku kampug selalo naicak muj jeer ru muj sawét. Tapi, mai de gigei ku pekan hajap naiha-berkep muj adeh.
example_en: People who live in the village always eat coconut pith or the pith from palm tree trunks. But, people who live in the town find it quite difficult to get the pith to eat.
example_ms: Orang yang tinggal di kampung selalu memakan umbut kelapa atau umbut dari batang pokok sawit. Tetapi, orang-orang yang tinggal di pekan agak sukar untuk mendapat umbut untuk dimakan.

example_semai: "Beileh muj nu madeg!" apak kijempot ikawat nu madeg.
example_en: "Come into the house!" the father invited his friend.
example_ms: "Marilah masuk ke rumah!" bapa menjemput rakannya ke rumah.

example_semai: Lumbe meskat Tour de Langkawi kimula ju Kuala Lumpur lei nu Langkawi.
example_en: The Tour de Langkawi bicycle race starts from Kuala Lumpur and ends at Langkawi.
example_ms: Perlumbaan basikal Tour de Langkawi bermula dari Kuala Lumpur sehingga ke Langkawi.

example_semai: Lima ikor kenon cok naimumem ku ikeneng.
example_en: Five puppies are suckling from their mother.
example_ms: Lima ekor anak anjing sedang menyusu pada ibu mereka.

definition_en: disloyal, not keeping promises or agreements

example_semai: "Neeng ajeh, mong bende biyeg begei ha abat langser ku kemil jehuk ajeh!" Wak Nira kimur.
example_en: "Look, there's a white object like a curtain on the tree!" Wak Nira pointed out.
example_ms: "Lihat, ada benda putih seperti kain langsir di atas pokok tersebut!" Wak Nira menegur.

example_semai: "Tok penghulu kiog bantuan ju kerajaan had mur nu peringak ilei taleh," Bah Aman kiurok ku mase mesyuarat kampug.
example_en: "The headman distributed the aids from the government only to his own family members," Bah Aman said in the village's meeting.
example_ms: "Tok penghulu membahagikan bantuan dari kerajaan cuma hanya kepada ahli keluarganya sahaja," Bah Aman berkata semasa mesyuarat kampung.

example_semai: Cikgu kiajar imureb benaca.
example_en: The teacher teaches her students to read.
example_ms: Cikgu mengajar murid-muridnya membaca.

example_semai: Hunen musem pelek ku jap-jap kampug.
example_en: Now is the fruit season in every village.
example_ms: Sekarang adalah musim buah-buahan di setiap kampung.

example_semai: Mase cuti sekulah padehjeh, Bah Zam kien ikenon-kenah cekcip nu musium.
example_en: During the school holidays, Bah Zam took his wife and children to the museum.
example_ms: Semasa cuti sekolah tempoh hari, Bah Zam membawa anak-anak dan isterinya berjalan-jalan ke muzium.

example_semai: Entem, mase ku jaman perag, musoh hik ajeh mai Jepon.
example_en: In the past, during the war, our enemies were the Japanese.
example_ms: Dahulu, semasa zaman perperangan, musuh kita adalah orang Jepun.

example_semai: "Hik pek buleh hibemusoh ru mai kilek," atok jik kibesat nu jik mase jik lekat jisanget entem.
example_en: "We can't be hostile to other people," our grandfather told us when we were kids.
example_ms: "Kita tidak boleh bermusuhan dengan orang lain," datuk kami berpesan kepada kami sewaktu kami masih kanak-kanak dahulu.

definition_en: to tap rubber to collect latex
definition_ms: menoreh getah dengan cara memotong kulit pokok getah
example_semai: Jap pegelap amek ru tenek eng naiet nu mutong.
example_en: Every morning, my mother and older sister go rubber tapping.
example_ms: Pada setiap pagi, ibu dan kakak saya pergi menoreh getah.

example_semai: Bah Wali kien mutu nu ikerjak.
example_en: Bah Wali uses a motorcycle to go to work.
example_ms: Bah Wali menggunakan motosikal ke tempat kerja.

example_semai: Ubai Bah Benny naas ku nong timbok cinek.
example_en: Bah Benny and his friend crashed on the road just now.
example_ms: Bah Benny dan rakannya nahas di jalan raya sebentar tadi.

definition_en: a prefix as a 3rd person plural (more than two) agreement marker for verb
definition_ms: mereka; imbuhan awalan bagi 'mereka' ganti nama diri ketiga jamak (lebih dari dua) untuk kata kerja
example_semai: "Naiha-et belok mambek ajeh?" Bah Padil kitanya nu ikenah sambel kineng ha ikekenon.
example_en: "Where are they going?" Bah Padil asked his wife while looking at his children.
example_ms: "Mereka hendak ke mana?" Bah Padil bertanya kepada isterinya sambil melihat kepada anak-anaknya.

definition_ms: mendidih; menggelegak; meruap (cecair) kerana sudah panas.
example_semai: "Teu ku cirek ajeh ya nam," Bah Jali kipasal nu ikenah.
example_en: "The water in the kettle is already boiling," Bah Jali told his wife.
example_ms: "Air yang di cerek tersebut sudah mendidih," Bah Jali memberitahu isterinya.

example_semai: "Nanek ju empat ikor kenon kuceg biperyos ajeh ya dat," Bah Halei kipasal nu iamek.
example_en: "One of the four kittens that was born dead," Bah Halei told his mother.
example_ms: "Satu dari empat ekor anak kucing yang dilahirkan tersebut telah mati," Bah Halei memberitahu kepada ibunya.

example_semai: Bah Gurek mong kicet nangkak ku ibelakang deg.
example_en: Bah Gurek is planting jackfruit in the backyard.
example_ms: Bah Gurek ada menanam nangka di belakang rumahnya.

example_semai: Mat Bah Gani as bisej ya naning.
example_en: Bah Gani's eye was swollen because it was stung by a giant honey bee.
example_ms: Mata Bah Gani bengkak disengat oleh lebah tualang.

definition_en: greater mouse-deer; a type of mouse-deer that is large and lives in low areas.
definition_ms: napuh; sejenis pelanduk yang bersaiz besar dan tinggal di kawasan yang rendah.
example_semai: Bah Jali kiberkep napoh mase kiet kidendep ku darat.
example_en: Bah Jali got a greater mouse-deer while going hunting in the forest.
example_ms: Bah Jali mendapatkan napuh semasa pergi memburu di hutan.

example_semai: Mong nar urag sengoi dat bikap ya mergas ku jeres yah ajeh nek.
example_en: Two people were devoured by tigers in the forest today.
example_ms: Terdapat dua orang mati yang dibaham oleh harimau di hutan hari ini.

example_semai: "Naseb mong hek halalu ku padeh, amen tok mesti jisusah," engrok Bah Wan de dij iminyak kerite nu Bah Gani de buleh kitulog beli petrol ha kek.
example_en: "Fortunately you are using this road too, otherwise we would be in trouble," Bah Wan who had run out of car fuel told to Bah Gani who could help buy petrol for him.
example_ms: "Mujur kamu ada melalui jalan ini, jika tidak kami mesti akan mengalami kesusahan," Bah Wan yang kehabisan minyak kereta berkata kepada Bah Gani yang boleh menolong membeli petrol bagi beliau.

definition_en: rice flour applied to the body to cure diseases after an incantation has been spoken over it
definition_ms: tepung beras yang telah dijampi yang disapu pada badan bagi mengubati penyakit
example_semai: "Mase jik lekat jisanget, amek jik selalo kikot gek cengroi ru kigerlet cengroi ajeh lei kijadi tepong. Telas ajeh tepong ajeh bijampi dea bigep ku berog ha perembor nyaknik," amek kicerite nu jik ikekenon.
example_en: "When we were children, our mother often took some rice and grinded the rice into flour. After that, an incantation was spoken over the flour so that it could be applied to the body to cure diseases," the mother told us, her children.
example_ms: "Semasa kami masih kanak-kanak, ibu kami sering mengambil sedikit beras dan menggiling beras tersebut sehingga menjadi tepung. Selepas itu tepung tersebut dijampi supaya boleh disapu pada badan bagi mengubati penyakit," ibu bercerita kepada kami, anak-anaknya.

definition_ms: negara; sesebuah kerajaan yang mempunyai raja, pemimpin atau pemerintah untuk mentadbir rakyat dan segala yang ada di dalamnya
example_semai: Negare hik Malaysia adeh ama nru damei.
example_en: Our country Malaysia is peaceful.
example_ms: Negara kita Malaysia adalah aman dan damai.

definition_en: particle suffix that strengthens the meaning of the base word
definition_ms: -lah; partikel pada akhir kata yang menguatkan maksud perkataan yang berimbuhan dengannya
example_semai: "Pai adeh neh nglei," kiberlag ya Bah Hari bile bitanya bile kilei nu majeh.
example_en: "I just arrived a moment ago," Bah Hari replied when asked when did he got to the place.
example_ms: "Baharu sebentar ini tadi saya sampai," Bah Hari membalas apabila ditanya bilakah dia sampai ke tempat tersebut.

definition_en: generic word for all things ugly or bad
definition_ms: perkataan generik bagi semua perkara yang buruk atau tidak elok
example_semai: "Ihat nej perangei hek adeh," Bah Gop kiurok nu ikenon.
example_en: "You have a very bad attitude," Bah Gop said to his son.
example_ms: "Sangat buruk perangai kamu," Bah Gop berkata kepada anaknya.

example_semai: "Ajeh nek hek haet nu belok?" apak kitanya nu tenek eng.
example_en: "Where did you go just now?" father asked my older brother.
example_ms: "Sebentar tadi kamu ke mana?" bapa bertanya kepada abang saya.

example_semai: Wak Mia kikek ha beleg tapi kikep had nek nukebek taleh.
example_en: Wak Mia looked for betel nuts but got only one.
example_ms: Wak Mia mencari buah pinang tetapi mendapat hanya sebiji sahaja.

definition_en: for 'doer' and to indicate one's inclination
definition_ms: bagi menunjukkan kecenderungan seseorang; bagi orang yang sering melakukan aksi kata kerja berikut
example_semai: "Bah Aru neklenglod ru Bah Risau nekbelnel. Wak Sanggop nij nekbejawab engrok mai engrak. Hat Bah Gali kihalei taleh de nekjinos engrok mai engrak," Wak Salmah kimerungut ha ikekenon nu igensir.
example_en: "Bah Aru likes to tell lies and Bah Risau is a drunkard. Wak Sanggup likes to talk back to his parents. Only Bah Gali likes to listen to his parents' words," Wak Salmah complained about her children to her husband.
example_ms: "Bah Aru suka menipu dan Bah Risau adalah pemabuk. Wak Sanggup pula suka menjawab perkara yang dikatakan oleh orang tua. Hanya Bah Gali seorang sahaja yang suka mendengar kata-kata orang tua," Wak Salmah merungut tentang anak-anaknya kepada suaminya.

example_semai: Bah Ari kien ikenon sanget neng kak ku kulam.
example_en: Bah Ari brought his little boy to see the fish in the pond.
example_ms: Bah Ari membawa anak kecilnya melihat ikan di kolam.

definition_en: not to stop looking; to gaze; to stare
example_semai: Bah Leman kinenneng ha kerdor ku piknoj bas ajeh, medei kirasa begei mong de pek kena ku kerdor ajeh. Paknyak neh, kerdor ajeh numom keral de kibeh ilei begei ha kerdor.
example_en: Bah Lemah did not stop looking at the woman at the bus stop, because he felt there was something strange about her. Apparently the woman was a man behaving as if he were a woman.
example_ms: Bah Lemah tidak putus-putus melihat perempuan di tempat perhentian bas tersebut, kerana dia merasakan terdapat sesuatu perkara yang pelik berkenaan dengan perempuan tersebut. Rupa-rupanya perempuan tersebut adalah seorang lelaki yang berkelakuan seperti seorang perempuan.

definition_en: ripe; condition of fruit that is ready to eat
definition_ms: masak; keadaan buah yang sudah cukup masak dan boleh dipetik serta dimakan; buah yang sudah cukup matang dan dapat dituai
example_semai: "Pelek betek ajeh ya nep. Kot en jog," ajok kipasal nu eng.
example_en: "The papaya is ripe. Take it home," my grandmother told me.
example_ms: "Buah betik itu sudah masak. Ambil bawa pulang ke rumahmu," nenek memberitahu kepada saya.

example_semai: "Amen jooi dusa hik, doh mesti himuj nu neraka," apak Bah Wan kiserngoh ha icecenok.
example_en: "If we have many sins, we will go to hell," Bah Wan's father said to frighten his grandchildren.
example_ms: "Jika kita mempunyai banyak dosa, kita akan masuki neraka," bapa kepada Bah Wan menakut-nakutkan cucu-cucunya.

definition_en: to show a sign with hand gesture, without a voice
definition_ms: memberi isyarat dengan tangan, tanpa suara
example_semai: Cekgu Azman kiog neuta ya iteg, kidek ijaras nu inyiyoi sebegei neuta kior pelajar-pelajar naikerakdik serakngek.
example_en: Teacher Azman gestured with his hand, by putting his finger on his lips as a sign he was telling the students to be silent.
example_ms: Cikgu Azman memberi isyarat dengan tangannya, dia meletakkan jari pada bibirnya sebagai tanda dia menyuruh pelajar-pelajar berdiam.

definition_en: I; 1st person singular agreement marker for verb
definition_ms: saya; imbuhan ganti nama diri pertama tunggal untuk kata kerja
example_semai: "Eng nghagu-cak caknak mai Korea," Wak Jura kipasal nu igensir.
example_en: "I really want to eat Korean food," Wak Jura told her husband.
example_ms: "Saya terigin mahu memakan makanan orang Korea," Wak Jura memberitahu suaminya.

definition_en: changing a descriptive verb to noun

example_semai: Kenon Wak Gina kijap medei kisengoh ha ngak de kimuj nu madeg.
example_en: Wak Gina's son cried out in fear of the beetles which were entering the house.
example_ms: Anak Wak Gina menangis kerana takut terhadap kumbang yang memasuki ke dalam rumah.

definition_en: name usually for the second child in a family
definition_ms: (N-) panggilan atau gelaran nama yang mengikut turutan kelahiran untuk anak kedua dalam keluarga
example_semai: "Tenek eng adeh imuh Wak Ngah medei ilei adehle de ingah ku kateh beradek eng," Bah Tam kipasal nu ikekawat.
example_en: "My sister's name is Wak Ngah because she is the second child among my siblings," Bah Tam told his friends.
example_ms: "Nama kakak saya ini adalah Wak Ngah kerana dialah anak yang kedua di kalangan adik-beradik saya," Bah Tam memberitahu kawan-kawannya.

example_semai: Jog Wak Siti luka medei pek kibakek yoj de mong ku ngar ilei, tesilap kijebat yoj ajeh.
example_en: Wak Siti's foot was injured because se did not see the knife that was in front of her, she mistakenly stepped on it.
example_ms: Kaki Wak Siti terluka kerana dia tidak nampak pisau yang ada dihadapannya, dia tersilap memijak pisau tersebut.

example_semai: Bah Ajoi kidek sekejap kak de kirag ku wal rangas, pek kisedar ikak ajeh ya ngej.
example_en: Bah Ajoi left the fish he was grilling in the firewood stove for a moment,   as a result he did not realise when the fish became charred.
example_ms: Bah Ajoi meninggalkan sekejap ikan yang dia bakar pada dapur kayu api, tidak dia sedari ikannya tersebut sudah hangus.

definition_en: to escort; to accompany as an escort
example_semai: Eng ngeng amek eng nu hospital.
example_en: I escorted my mother to the hospital.
example_ms: Saya hantar mak saya ke hospital.

example_semai: Bah Hashim kingeng bob Wak Rani nu imadeg medei kisip ku sekulah cinek. Telaas ya kilei nu madeg doh baruleh kiperjog bob ajeh nu ilei.
example_en: Bah Hashim brought Wak Rani's book to his house because she had left the book at school. Once he got home, he would return the book to her.
example_ms: Bah Hashim membawa buku Wak Rani ke rumahnya kerana dia tertinggal buku tersebut di sekolah. Setelah dia sampai ke rumah, barulah dia akan memulangkan buku tersebut kepadanya.

example_semai: "Tulog pej sampah ajeh ya hek, ingoi ya seeg," amek kiurok nu tenek keral eng.
example_en: "Please throw away the rubbish, it smells bad," my mother said to my older brother.
example_ms: "Tolong buang sampah tersebut, baunya sudah busuk," ibu berkata kepada abang saya.

example_semai: "Tulog kerngoi canak adeh ya hek, lekat borka ya barik? Eng ngkena sih, pek dapat ngkerngoi," Wak Rima kiurok nu igensir.
example_en: "Please smell this rice, is it still good or stale? I have a cold, so I can't smell it," Wak Rima said to her husband.
example_ms: "Tolong hidu nasi ini, masih elok atau sudah basi? Saya menghidap selsema, jadi tidak dapat menghidu," Wak Rima berkata kepada suaminya.

example_semai: Wak Nira kingop ikui bile kilalu ku kerop cenglei.
example_en: Wak Nira lowered her head when passing under the clothes line.
example_ms: Wak Nira menundukkan kepalanya apabila lalu di bawah pengampaian pakaian.

example_semai: Cok ajeh kingut teu lei jot ilehig.
example_en: The dog drank water until he drooled.
example_ms: Anjing tersebut meminum air sehingga air liurnya meleleh.

example_semai: "Eng ngbual begei ajeh pek ngbeniat ngha-pernyik ha sengik hek. Jadi hunen, ngsemanj maap amen haterasam," Wak Timah kiurok nu Wak Tipah.
example_en: "I didn't intend to hurt your feelings when I said that. So now, I apologize if you felt hurt," Wak Timah said to Wak Tipah.
example_ms: "Saya berkata demikian bukan berniat untuk meyakiti hati kamu. Jadi sekarang, saya meminta maaf jika kamu terasa," Wak Timah berkata kepada Wak Tipah.

example_semai: Wak Nira kicak nij bile kineng iapak kien jog jeoi caknak ju pasar malam, hamapen pai anoh nek kicakcak.
example_en: Wak Nira ate again when she saw her father bring home a lot of food from the night market, even though she had only just eaten.
example_ms: Wak Nira memakan lagi bila melihat bapanya membawa pulang banyak makanan dari pasar malam, walaupun baru sebentar ini sahaja dia telah makan.

example_semai: Wak Sabah kitambah nij teu nu kateh sup ajeh medei kirasa nek gek taleh, kisengoh doh pek cukop.
example_en: Wak Sabah added more water again to the soup because she felt that the soup was too little, she was worried it would not be enough.
example_ms: Wak Sabah menambah lagi air ke dalam sup tersebut kerana beliau merasakan ianya hanya sedikit, dia kuatir ianya tidak akan mencukupi.

example_semai: Wak Yaya mong nik urag imenang keral.
example_en: Wak Yaya has three younger brothers.
example_ms: Wak Yaya ada tiga orang adik lelaki.

definition_ms: itu sahaja; cuma itu saja; hanya itu sahaja
example_semai: "Hat aninen talehka cengroi de mong? Amen ya dij, ngha-beli nij," Bah Selan kitanya nu ikenah.
example_en: "Is that all the rice that is left? If it runs out, I want to buy it again," Bah Selan asked his wife.
example_ms: "Cuma itu sahajakan beras yang ada? Jika sudah habis, saya mahu membelinya lagi," Bah Selan bertanya kepada isterinya.

definition_en: just this; only this; that's all
definition_ms: hanya ini; cuma ini; sekadar ini (sahaja)
example_semai: "Hooh, had aninoh taleh cengroi de mong, etleh beli nij," Wak Lela kiberlag ha tenanyak igensir.
example_en: "Yes, that's all the rice thhat is left, go and buy some more," Wak Lela replied to her husband's question.
example_ms: "Ya, cuma itu sahaja beras yang tertinggal, pergilah beli lagi," Wak Lela membalas pertanyaan suaminya.

example_semai: Nik noh Bah Jetep kiet nu deg Bah Jamil, tapi lekat juga bok hentok de mong ku ideg.
example_en: Bah Jetep went to Bah Jamil's house three times, but there was still no one at his house.
example_ms: Tiga kali Bah Jetep pergi ke rumah Bah Jamil, tetapi masih tiada siapa yang ada di rumahnya.

example_semai: "Nyoi amek nok sebelom haet nu sekulah," Wak Ramlah kiurok nu ikenon.
example_en: "Kiss me first before you go to school," Wak Ramlah said to her son.
example_ms: "Cium ibu dahulu sebelum kamu ke sekolah," Wak Ramlah berkata kepada anaknya.

example_semai: Wak Mila kilap nom imenang de kaha-nanom pek kipasal canuk.
example_en: Wak Mila wiped up her little brother's urine when he did not tell her that he wanted to urinate.
example_ms: Wak Mila mengelap air kencing adiknya yang tidak memberitahu semasa mahu terkencing.

example_semai: "Hacip nong belok?" Han Tira kitanya ha igensir.
example_en: "Where do you want to go?" Han Tira asked her husband.
example_ms: "Kamu mahu ke mana?" Han Tira bertanya kepada suaminya.

example_semai: Nong ku Rumpaian Laut lecah.
example_en: The road to Rumpaian Laut is muddy.
example_ms: Jalan ke Rumpaian Laut berlumpur.

example_semai: "Nyik nos eng ngneng perangei enai ajeh," Wak Mila kiurok nu ikekawat ha mabek sanget keral de nairahnyah ku kateh kelas.
example_en: "It makes me angry to see their behaviour," Wak Mila told her friends about the male students making a noise in the classroom.
example_ms: "Sakit hati saya melihat kelakuan mereka," Wak Mila berkata kepada rakan-rakannya berkenaan pelajar-pelajar lelaki yang membuat bising di dalam kelas.

example_semai: Bah Tuas kijog nu deg iamek ku mase kicuti ju ikerjak.
example_en: Bah Tuas went back to his mother's house while he was on leave from work.
example_ms: Bah Tuas pulang ke rumah ibunya semasa dia bercuti dari kerjanya.

example_semai: Nuhenglei ju abat de bitil ya Wak Kila ya jer nu matik.
example_en: One piece of the clothes that being hang by Wak Kila to dry under the sun had fallen to the ground.
example_ms: Sehelai dari pakaian yang dijemur oleh Wak Kila telah jatuh ke tanah.

example_semai: "Ku kateh nukember mat taleh cep ajeh ya kiheg, pek sempat ngha-kot igambar," Wak Salwa kipasal nu igensir.
example_en: "In the blink of an eye, the bird has flown, I did not have time to take a picture," Wak Salwa told her husband.
example_ms: "Dalam sekelip mata sahaja burung tersebut sudah terbang, saya tidak sempat mengambil gambarnya," Wak Salwa memberitahu kepada suaminya.

example_semai: "Gei nulok agok cengor," Wak Mila kiurok nu ikenon mase ubai ku katek bank, ubai poj giliran numbor ubai bicereg.
example_en: "Say still, don't be naughty," Wak Mila said to her son while they were both in the bank, waiting for the queue number to be called.
example_ms: "Duduk setempat jangan nakal," Wak Mila berkata kepada anaknya semasa mereka berdua di dalam bank, ketika menunggu nombor giliran untuk dipanggil.

example_semai: "Eng kep numbor empat mase jernar ku sekulah cinek," Wak Para kipasal nu iapak.
example_en: "I got the fourth place in the running training at the school earlier," Wak Para told her father.
example_ms: "Saya dapat nombor empat dalam latihan lumba lari di sekolah tadi," Wak Para memberitahu kepada bapanya.

example_semai: Bah Wan mong had numom taleh ikenon kerdor, kilek dij keral.
example_en: Bah Wan has only one daughter, the rest are all boys.
example_ms: Bah Wan mempunyai hanya seorang saja anak perempuan, yang lain semua lelaki.

definition_en: one by one; one after another; one person after another
example_semai: Bah Selah kibahgi pelek legos numom-penumom nu ikekenon.
example_en: Bah Selah gave out the rambutans one by one to his children.
example_ms: Bah Selah membahagikan buah rambutan seorang demi seorang kepada anak-anaknya.

example_semai: eng
example_en: "Sakit hati saya melihat perangai mereka berdua tersebut, di khalayak ramai mereka berdua berpelukan," Wak Sila berkata kepada Wak Sara berkenaan dengan kelakuan pasangan kekasih di perhentian bas tersebut.

example_semai: Tempat kerenjak Bah Rani nyak ju bareh.
example_en: Bah Rani's workplace is far from the town.
example_ms: Tempat Bah Rani bekerja jauh dari pekan.

example_semai: "Wesloh agok teh, pelek mangge ajeh lekat nyam. Pojle dea kinep nok," ajok kiurok nu jik.
example_en: "Do not pick the mangoes, they are still unripe. Wait until they are fully ripe," grandmother told us.
example_ms: "Janganlah memetik buah mangga tersebut, ianya masih muda. Tunggulah sehingga ianya masak dahulu," nenek berkata kepada kami.

example_semai: Pelahat Wak Anis kinyam icanak medei ha pehod ilemunj.
example_en: Wah Anis slowly chewed her food because her teeth ached.
example_ms: Wah Anis perlahan-lahan mengunyah makanannya kerana giginya sakit.

example_semai: "Jok hiet nyampong tengah kibubet," ajok kiurok nu amek de ubai ha-et nu selai sekejap sementare menang sanget jik kibukbet.
example_en: "Let's go, fortunately she is sleeping," the grandmother told the mother that they both wanted to go to the garden for a while, while our little sister was sleeping.
example_ms: "Jom kita pergi mujur dia sedang tidur," nenek berkata kepada ibu yang mereka berdua mahu pergi ke kebun sekejap sementara adik kecil kami sedang tidur.

example_semai: Wak Wawa kigemar neng cerite nyanik hamapen ilei ajeh penakot.
example_en: Wak Wawa likes to watch ghost movies even though she is a coward.
example_ms: Wak Wawa suka menonton cerita hantu walaupun dia adalah seorang yang penakut.

definition_ms: kasihannya; kata untuk menyatakan perasaan kasihan
example_semai: Nyanyak, nen cok ajeh kicuag.
example_en: Poor thing, that dog is hungry.
example_ms: Kesiannya anjing itu lapar.

example_semai: "Bor suara Wahk Dia bile kibenyanyi," Cekgu Kamil kipateh.
example_en: "Wak Dia's voice is good when she sings," Mr. Kami said, praising her.
example_ms: "Sedap suara Wak Dia bila dia menyanyi," Cikgu Kamil memuji.

example_semai: Mai engrak manah naiurok, ku arik nyap adehleh mong jeoi nyanik naihol. Sebab ajehleh selalo naior jog madeg amen iarik nyap.
example_en: The elders said that when the sky was red at dusk many ghosts roamed around. That is why they always told us to go home when twilight came.
example_ms: Orang tua-tua dahulu berkata bahawa pada waktu senja merah terdapat banyak hantu-hantu berkeliaran. Oleh sebab itulah mereka selalu menyuruh kita pulang ke rumah pada waktu terjadi senja merah.

example_semai: "Amen hiha-et nu englag hik kena hinyeberang teu entoi ajeh canuk," Bah Long kiternyol ha Sg. Perak nu jik.
example_en: "If we want to go to the other side, we must cross the big river first," said Bah Long pointing to the Perak river to us.
example_ms: "Jika kita nak pergi ke sebelah, kita hendaklah menyeberang sungai besar itu dulu," Bah Long menuding ke Sg. Perak kepada kami.

example_semai: Bah Jali pek lalu kiangkat kerusi batu ajeh kihalei, jadi kiajak ikekawat kaha-jeg kerusi nyeh ajeh nu laman deg.
example_en: Bah Jali could not lift the stone chair alone, so he invited his friends to move that heavy chair to the yard.
example_ms: Bah Jali tidak mampu mengangkat kerusi batu tersebut seorang diri, jadi dia mengajak rakan-rakannya memindahkan kerusi yang berat itu ke halaman rumah.

definition_en: to tease; to joke; to kid
example_semai: Bha Duyen kinyeh ha bha Saki.
example_en: Bah Duyen teased Bah Saki.
example_ms: Bah Duyen mengejek terhadap Bah Saki.

example_semai: "Eng ngnyek pek ngha-cakcak," Bah Meran kipasal nu iamek.
example_en: "I do not want to eat," Bah Meran told his mother.
example_ms: "Saya tidak mahu makan," Bah Meran memberitahu kepada ibunya.

example_semai: "Kuceg jik ya nyep, entah nu belok kijar," Wak Lila kipasal nu jik.
example_en: "Our cat has disappeared, we don't know where it went," Wak Lila asked us.
example_ms: "Kucing kami telah hilang, entah ke mana dia lari," Wak Lila memberitahu kepada kami.

example_semai: "Gelpal eng nyik kenak nyijak," Bah Jali kipasal nu ikenah.
example_en: "My shoulder hurts because I hit the wall bracket," Bah Jali told his wife.
example_ms: "Bahu saya sakit terkena penyangga dinding," Bah Jali memberitahu isterinya.

example_semai: Jog Bah Halil nyik kitejebat paku.
example_en: Bah Hall's foot hurt because he had accidentally stepped on a nail.
example_ms: Kaki Bah Halil sakit terpijak paku.

example_semai: Nyok ku belakang deg ajok Sila ya cerek medei bok hentok ha misen.
example_en: The japanese bloodgrass that is behind grandma Sila house is long because no one is cutting it.
example_ms: Lalang merah di belakang nenek Sila sudah panjang kerana tiada siapa yang memotongnya.

example_semai: Bah Sali kinyok bakau.
example_en: Bah Sali smokes tobacco.
example_ms: Bah Sali menghisap tembakau.

definition_en: to take; to get; to carry
example_semai: Pegelap jinek amek eng kinyong teu ku perigi.
example_en: My mother took water from the well this morning.
example_ms: Pagi tadi, emak saya mengambil air di perigi.

example_semai: Wak Ramlah kinyui ikenon sanget.
example_en: Wak Ramlah kissed his little son.
example_ms: Wak Ramlah mencium anaknya yang kecil.

example_semai: Bah Sali kinyuk bakau.
example_en: Bah Sali smokes tobacco.
example_ms: Bah Sali menghisap tembakau.

example_semai: Mong deg og ajeh ku kemil keneng sempak.
example_en: There is a ground wasp's nest in the durian tree.
example_ms: Terdapat sarang tebuan tanah di atas pokok durian.

example_semai: "Baju ajeh biog ya Bah Nan ha hek," Wak Jari kiurok nu Wak Mila.
example_en: "The shirt was given by Bah Nan for you," Wak Jari said to Wak Mila.
example_ms: "Baju tersebut diberi oleh Bah Nan bagi kamu," Wak Jari berkata kepada Wak Mila.

definition_en: word to express that one does not know, perhaps, maybe
example_semai: "Oh," Bah Gurek kiberlag bile bitanya belok imemenang ya iamek.
example_en: "I don't know," Bah Gurek replied when his mother asked where his siblings had gone.
example_ms: "Entah," Bah Gurek menjawab bila ibunya bertanya ke mana adik-adiknya pergi.

definition_en: to be awake but not willing to get up; to be lazy to get up
example_semai: Iarik la yah tapi lekakt kiop.
example_en: It's already daytime but he's too lazy to get up.
example_ms: Hari sudah siang tetapi dia buat-buat tidur.

example_semai: Bah Hussin kior ianak buah kot bantal.
example_en: Bah Husin ordered his nephew to take a pillow.
example_ms: Bah Husin menyuruh anak buahnya mengambil bantal.

example_semai: Bah Midin kirup os ku deg pender mandei kaha-jop gale.
example_en: Bah Midin lit a fire in the hut because he wanted to roast the tapioca.
example_ms: Bah Midin menyala api di dalam pondok kerana mahu membakar ubi kayu.

definition_ms: di; untuk menunjukkan arah atau tempat

example_semai: Sempak Bah Jani ku pabel.
example_en: Bah Jani's durian trees are in the scrub.
example_ms: Pokok-pokok durian Bah Jani terdapat di belukar.

definition_ms: memacak; menusuk atau membenam benda tajam ke atas sesuatu (tanah dan sebagainya)
example_semai: Bah Kamil kipacak ichenol deg ku belakang deg iamek.
example_en: Bah Kamil staked his house pole behind his mother's house.
example_ms: Bah Kamil memacak tiang rumahnya di belakang rumah ibunya.

definition_en: to puncture; to open something by making a puncture
definition_ms: menebuk; membuka atau membelah sesuatu (tin, buluh dll.) dengan menggunakan benda yang tajam
example_semai: Wak Nani kipacok tong susu ajeh medei kaha-beh teh ha iapak.
example_en: Wak Nani punctured the condensed milk can because she wanted to make tea for his father.
example_ms: Wak Nani menebuk tin susu kerana mahu membuat teh bagi bapanya.

definition_ms: gelaran bagi landak; sejenis landak kecil
example_semai: Hajap hunen hiha bakek pacor mong pen ku Zoo taleh.
example_en: Now it is difficult to see a procupine in the wild, if we do, it will only be at the Zoo.
example_ms: Sekarang susah nak berjumpa dengan landak, kalau ada pun di Zoo sahaja.

example_semai: Mai sanget sekulah naimain bula ku padag.
example_en: School children play football on the field.
example_ms: Budak-budak sekolah bermain bola sepak di padang.

example_semai: Padehal Wak Maria kicerengai ikekenon naibekelahi ku kateh bilek liknai, tapi saja kibeh pek panei.
example_en: Actually Wak Maria heard her children fighting in their room, but she pretended that she didn't know about it.
example_ms: Sebenarnya Wak Maria mendengar anak-anakknya berkelahi di dalam bilik mereka, tetapi dia sengaja membuat tidak tahu.

definition_ms: pusat; lekuk di tengah perut; tempat di mana tali perut bersambung ke uri
example_semai: Jerek padek Bah Sita ajeh.
example_en: Bah Sita's navel is deep.
example_ms: Pusat Bah Sita adalah dalam.

definition_ms: fail; bekas atau tempat (selalunya diperbuat daripada kertas tebal atau plastik) untuk menyimpan sesuatu dokumen yang penting. Cth: surat, sijil kelahiran dll.
example_semai: Wak Sila kidek dikdij isijel de penteng ku kateh pael dea pek nyep.
example_en: Wak Sila keeps all of her important certificates in a file so that they do not go missing.
example_ms: Wak Sila menyimpan semua sijilnya yang penting di dalam fail supaya tidak hilang.

example_semai: Bah Selan kitulog itenek terbor paet de ceeh medei tesilap bilanggar ya luri sawet.
example_en: Bah Selan helped his brother repair the pipe that was broken because it had been accidentally  hit by a palm oil truck.
example_ms: Bah Selan menolong abangnya memperbaiki paip yang pecah akibat tersilap dilanggar oleh lori sawit.

definition_ms: pagar; sesuatu kayu, batu atau besi dawai yang dibuat bagi mendindingi sesuatu kawasan (rumah, tanah,kubur dsbnya) supaya tidak dimasuki atau dicerobohi oleh sesuatu
example_semai: Bah Cicak kibeh pagar ku idepan deg medei kinyek ilaman deg bimuj ya cok.
example_en: Bah Cicak made a fence in front of his house because he did not want dogs to come into his yard.
example_ms: Bah Cicak membuat pagar di depan rumahnya kerana tidak mahu halaman rumahnya dimasuki oleh anjing.

definition_ms: baru; belum ada sebelumnya dari yang ada sekarang; belum lama
example_semai: Bah Tam kibeli deg pai, tapi pek lei lima gencek naigei ya mong jeoi inerusak.
example_en: Bah Tam bought a new house, but in less than five months occupying the house, there was a lot of damage.
example_ms: Bah Tam membeli rumah baharu, tetapi tidak sampai sehingga lima bulan mereka meduduki rumah tersebut, telah terdapat banyak kerosakannya.

definition_en: to calculate everything; to be too calculating
example_semai: "Wak Sali ajeh kigemar bepajeh ha hik, tapi cuba hik nu ilei pek pernah hibual ma-ma pen," Wak Maria kiurok nu ikekawat.
example_en: "Wak Sali likes to calculate everything that she has spent for us, but if the other way around, we never say a word," Wak Maria told her friends.
example_ms: "Wak Sali suka berkira dengan kita, tapi jika kita dengan dirinya, kita tidak pernah berkata apa-apa pun," Wak Maria berkata kepada rakan-rakannya.

example_semai: Isi dij bob ajeh kateh pak.
example_en: Put all the books in the box.
example_ms: Isi semua buku itu ke dalam kotak.

example_semai: Bah Long kipakat iamek et nu kedei.
example_en: Bah Long invited his mother to go to the store.
example_ms: Bah Long mengajak ibunya untuk pergi ke kedai.

definition_ms: pakai; mengenakan sesuatu pada tubuh badan
example_semai: Wak Ati kigemar bepakei baju kurong nu tempat kerjak.
example_en: Wak Ati likes to wear a traditional style of dress to work.
example_ms: Wak Ati suka memakai baju kurung ke tempat kerja.

example_semai: "Paknyak Wak Sita ya manah kikerakjak ku Kuala Lumpur," Wak Mira kipasal nu Wak Lani mase ubai besimbang.
example_en: "Apparently Wak Sita has been working in Kuala Lumpur for a long time," Wak Mira told Wak Lani while they were chatting.
example_ms: "Rupa-rupanya Wak Sita sudah lama bekerja di Kuala Lumpur," Wak Mira memberitahu Wak Lani semasa mereka berdua bersembang.

example_semai: Wak Mila kipakse ikekenon naimamuh, amen tok, ikekenon ajeh naibukbet kelem langsong pek naimamuh canuk.
example_en: Wak Mila forces her children to take a shower, otherwise the children would sleep at night without taking a shower first.
example_ms: Wak Mila memaksa anak-anaknya untuk mandi, jika tidak, anak-anaknya tersebut akan tidur malam tanpa mandi terlebih dahuu.

example_semai: Bah Jali kibeli paku kaha-pahat ris.
example_en: Bah Jali bought the nails to nail down the floor of the house.
example_ms: Bah Jali membeli paku bagi memaku lantai rumah.

example_semai: Wak Samnah kipal roi de naiha-dep ha caknak.
example_en: Wak Samnah swatted the flies which were about to land on the food.
example_ms: Wak Samnah memukul lalat yang mahu menghinggapi makanan.

example_semai: Mabek sanget naimamuh palak ku pateu.
example_en: Children bath naked in the river.
example_ms: Kanak-kanak bermandi telanjang di sungai.

definition_ms: bayas; sejenis tumbuhan palma yang tumbuh liar di dalam hutan, isi batang tumbuhan ini boleh dijadikan makanan.
example_semai: Apak kikoh keneng palei, kaha-berkep iisi ha menasak.
example_en: The father cut the trunk of the bayas tree, to get the shoot to be cooked.
example_ms: Bapa memotong batang pokok bayas, bagi mendapatkan umbutnya untuk dimasak.

definition_en: the most; extremely; used with an adjective to make a superlative
example_semai: "Baru adehleh kimanik de paleng lebat telas memerip gencek ya pek kimanik begei adeh. Selame anoh, mong kimanik pen had sekejap-sekajap taleh," Bah Kali kibesimbang ru Bah Nua mase ubai pimpoj manik kihoj ku tempat piknoj bas.
example_en: "This time is the heaviest it has rained after several months of rain unlike it. So far, it has only rained intermittently," Bah Kali chatted with Bah Nua as they both waited for the rain to subside at the bus stop.
example_ms: "Baru kali inilah hujan yang paling lebat setelah berberapa bulan tidak hujan sebegini. Selama ini, hujan hanya turun dengan sekejap-sekejap sahaja," Bah Kali bersembang dengan Bah Nua semasa mereka berdua menunggu hujan reda di tempat perhentian bas.

example_semai: Bah Wan kipalet paya ku teg iamek.
example_en: Bah Wan smeared mud on his mother's hand.
example_ms: Bah Wan memalit paya di tangan ibunya.

example_semai: "Paloh ajeh ya kutor wes agok muh," ajok kiurok nu icecenok.
example_en: "The pool is dirty, do not bathe there," grandmother said to her grandchildren.
example_ms: "Kolam itu sudah kotor, jangan mandi di situ," nenek berkata kepada cucu-cucunya.

example_semai: "Pak ajeh ya palok, tutop nok ya hek," Wak Maznah kipasal nu Wak Nira.
example_en: "The box is open, please close it," Wak Maznah told Wak Nira.
example_ms: "Kotak tersebut sudah terbuka, tolong tutup," Wak Maznah memberitahu kepada Wak Nira.

definition_ms: cebisan kayu api; kayu api yang dipotong dari batangnya
example_semai: Bah Jan kikot palok kaha-jop kak.
example_en: Bah Jan collected pieces of firewood to grill the fish.
example_ms: Bah Jan mengambil cebisan kayu api bagi memanggang ikan.

definition_ms: pam; sejenis alat yang digunakan untuk mengeluarkan atau menyedut udara, air dsbnya.
example_semai: Bah Mali kiet nu stisen minyak medei kaha-pam itaya kerite, tapi pam ku stisen minyak ajeh ya rusak.
example_en: Bah Mali went to the petrol station to inflate the tyres of his car, but the pump at the petrol station was broken.
example_ms: Bah Mali pergi ke stesen minyak kerana mahu mengepam tayar keretanya, tetapi pam di stesen minyak tersebut telah rosak.

example_semai: Bah Rani kipam itaya meskar medei ya kurang ipoi.
example_en: Bah Rani pumped his bicycle tyres due to lack of air pressure.
example_ms: Bah Rani mengepam tayar basikalnya kerana sudah kekurangan angin.

example_semai: "Singah ku pamasi doh, ngha-kot penadol," Wak Mira kipasal nu igensir.
example_en: "Later, stop at the pharmacy, I want to buy panadol," Wak Mira told her husband.
example_ms: "Nanti singgah di farmasi, saya mahu membeli panadol," Wak Mira memberitahu kepada suaminya.

example_semai: Wak Nani kineng itaya mutu ya pamcat mase kaha-et kerjak pegelap ajeh.
example_en: Wak Nani saw that one of her motorcycle tires was punctured when she was about to go to work that morning.
example_ms: Wak Nani melihat tayar motosikalmnya telah pancit semasa dia hendak pergi ke tempat kerja pagi tersebut.

definition_en: for emphasis on the action of a verb
definition_ms: untuk penekanan pada tindakan kata kerja
example_semai: Wak Mila kipan jar mase biwag ya chok, mase kijog ju sekulah.
example_en: Wak Mila ran hard while being chased by a dog on her way home from school.
example_ms: Wak Mila berlari bersungguh-sungguh semasa dikejar oleh anjing ketika pulang dari sekolah.

example_semai: "Agok pan ngut teh ajeh, medei tik beket," Wak Salma kipasal nu ikenon.
example_en: "Don't drink the tea too fast, because it is still hot," Wak Salma told her son.
example_ms: "Jangan minum teh itu dengan tergesa-gesa kerana ianya masih lagi panas," Wak Salma memberitahu kepada anaknya.

definition_en: to catch fish using a spear
example_semai: Bah Nua ru ikekawat naiet naipanah kak ku teu batag arik.
example_en: Bah Nua and his friends went spearfishing in the big river.
example_ms: Bah Nua dan rakan-rakannya pergi memanah ikan di sungai besar.

example_semai: Bah Loli mong jeoi panau ku icelod.
example_en: On Bah Loli's back there are a lot of yeast infection spots.
example_ms: Di belakang badan Bah Loli terdapat banyak panau.

definition_ms: tawar; payau; makanan atau minuman yang tidak mempunyai rasa
example_semai: "Gulei sej ajeh pancik, eng pek lalu ngcak," Wak Manja kiurok.
example_en: "The meat dish is flavourless, I can't eat it," said Wak Manja.
example_ms: "Gulai daging itu tawar, saya tidak larat memakannya," Wak Manja berkata.

definition_en: spout; device used for channeling latex from the tap into the container
definition_ms: pancur; alat yang digunakan untuk menyalurkan susu getah yang telah ditoreh masuk ke dalam bekasnya
example_semai: Bah As kipasag pancor ku keneng ceber.
example_en: Bah As installed a spout on a rubber tree (to channel the latex).
example_ms: Bah As memasang pancur di pokok getah.

definition_en: pandan, a generic name given to the smaller screw-pines
definition_ms: pandan; sejenis tumbuhan yang mempunyai daun yang panjang dan berbentuk tirus serta berbau wangi; tumbuhan yang sering digunakan campuran bagi pembuatan sesetengah jenis kuih-muih atau untuk dicampur dalam nasi yang sedang dimasak
example_semai: Wangi ngoi canak amen hibercet ru selak pandan.
example_en: Rice smells fragrant when cooked with pandan leaves.
example_ms: Nasi berbau wangi jika dimasak dengan daun pandan.

example_semai: "Pandu ya hek, tah merek irasa gulei eng adeh nek," Wak Ria kior nu igensir.
example_en: "Try it, see how my cooking tastes," Wak Ria told her husband.
example_ms: "Kamu cuba, entah bagaimana rasanya gulai saya ini," Wak Ria menyuruh suaminya.

example_semai: Hamapen ilei pai nik tahut iumur, tapi Wak Nia ajeh ya panei bebaca.
example_en: Although she is only three years old, Wak Nia is already good at reading.
example_ms: Walaupun umurnya baharu tiga tahun, tetapi Wak Nia sudah pandai membaca.

example_semai: "Eng ngpanei hek tengah hasedeh, tapi agokle joi perasaan hek anen sangat," Wak Yana kinasihat ha Wak Ria.
example_en: "I know you are sad, but do not depend on your feelings too much," Wak Yana advised Wak Ria.
example_ms: "Saya tahu kamu sedang bersedih, tapi janganlah kamu ikut perasaan kamu sangat," Wak Yana menasihat Wak Ria.

definition_ms: situ; di situ; kata yang menunjukkan tempat yang tidak berapa jauh
example_semai: "Cinek eng ngdek bob hek ku panen, tapi adeh tah ku belok pek ngpanei," Wak Sila kipasal nu Bah Jali.
example_en: "Earlier I put your book there, but now I do not know where it went," Wak Sila told Bah Jali.
example_ms: "Tadi saya meletakkan buku kamu di situ, tetapi sekarang ianya entah ke mana, saya tidak tahu," Wak Sila memberitahu kepada Bah Jali.

example_semai: Bah Din kipangag kak ha caknak tengah arik adeh.
example_en: Bah Din grilled fish for lunch today.
example_ms: Bah Din memanggang ikan untuk makan tengah hari ini.

example_semai: Bah Deli kior ikenah panggak caknak ha tengloh de hol nu deg enai.
example_en: Bah Deli told his wife to serve food to the guests who came to their house.
example_ms: Bah Deli menyuruh isterinya menghidangkan makanan bagi tetamu yang hadir ke rumah  mereka.

example_semai: Semandang kipanggak abat de kijual ajeh ku ngar deg Bah Sali.
example_en: The vendor always piles up the clothes he sells in front of Bah Sali's house.
example_ms: Penjual itu selalu melonggokkan pakaian yang dijualnya itu di hadapan rumah Bah Sali.

example_semai: Bah Nami kibeh pangken ku idepan deg.
example_en: Bah Nami made a wooden rack in front of his house.
example_ms: Bah Nami membuat pangkin di depan rumahnya.

example_semai: Bah Man ya kirancang kaha-en ikekenon-kenah bekelah nu pantei ku hujong gecek adeh.
example_en: Bah Man is already planning to take his family for a picnic at the beach later this month.
example_ms: Bah Man sudah merancang untuk membawa keluarganya pergi berkelah di pantai pada hujung bulan ini.

example_semai: Mong jooi pantem ku belakang deg Wah Su.
example_en: There are many monkeys at the back of Wah Su's house.
example_ms: Ada banyak monyet di belakang rumah Wah Su.

definition_en: cut, scratched or pierced (by sharp and straight objects)
definition_ms: tercacak (oleh benda tajam dan lurus); tersusuk oleh benda yang lurus dan tajam
example_semai: Jog Bah Aris ajeh panting ya yoj.
example_en: Bah Aris' leg was cut with a knife.
example_ms: Kaki Bah Aris itu tercacak dengan pisau.

definition_en: a Malay poetic form that consists of a pair of rhyming couplets
definition_ms: pantun; sejenis puisi berangkap yang mempunyai persamaan bunyi dihujung pembayang dan maksudnya
example_semai: "Mase ilei lekat sui, enjop eng manahjeh kigemar bepantut," Bah Ramli kipasal nu ikenah.
example_en: "While she was still alive, my grandmother used to love Malay poetry," Bah Ramli told his wife.
example_ms: "Semasa dia masih hidup, nenek saya dahulu suka berpantun," Bah Ramli memberitahu kepada isterinya.

definition_en: to warm up body by the fire
definition_ms: memanaskan badan; berdiang; memanaskan badan di depan atau berdekatan dengan unggun api
example_semai: "Enjop eng tiap pegelap kipap os," Wak Tila kipasal nu igensir.
example_en: "Every morning my grandmother warms herself in front of the fire," Wak Tila told her husband.
example_ms: "Pada setiap pagi nenek saya memanaskan badan di depan unggun api." Wak Tila memberitahu kepada suaminya.

example_semai: Bah Tuni kilog ibaju papah.
example_en: Bah Tuni wore his shirt inside out.
example_ms: Bah Tuni memakai bajunya secara terbalik.

example_semai: Kuceg Wak Nira kidak papah betol-betol ku depan pintu deg enai.
example_en: Wak Nira's cat was lying across the doorstep of their house.
example_ms: Kucing milik Wak Nira baring terlentang betul-betul di depan pintu rumah mereka.

example_semai: Papan hitam ya susah hiha-bakek ku belok-belok pen sejak dikdij mai naiguna had papan puteh taleh.
example_en: Blackboards are hard to find anywhere since everyone uses only whiteboards.
example_ms: Papan hitam sukar untuk dijumpai di mana-mana pun sahaja semenjak semua orang hanya menggunakan papan putih sahaja.

example_semai: Bah Sani kibeh deg papat ku ienglag deg ha iamek medei iamek kinyek gigei ku deg simen.
example_en: Bah Sani constructed a house of wooden planks next to his own for his mother because his mother did not want to live in a cement house.
example_ms: Bah Sani membuat rumah papan di sebelah rumahnya bagi ibunya kerana ibunya tidak mahu tinggal di rumah simen.

example_semai: Betes ajeh ya mai papeh.
example_en: The mushrooms are already growing.
example_ms: Cendawan itu sudah bertumbuh.

example_semai: Jehuk ajeh papot ya pes dui.
example_en: The tree was split by the storm yesterday.
example_ms: Pokok itu terbelah oleh ribut semalam

definition_ms: para; tempat menyimpan barang-barang yang dibuat tinggi separas bahu atau kepala dan sebaliknya mengikut keperluan
example_semai: Bah Wan kibeh para ku ibilek, tempat kaha-dek iarap.
example_en: Bah Wan put up shelves in his room, for a place to put his belongings.
example_ms: Bah Wan membuat para di biliknya, iaitu tempat bagi meletakkan barang-barangnyanya.

example_semai: Mambek sanget naigemar bekutep mat para, naiha-beh mat ha man congkak.
example_en: The children like to collect rubber seeds to be used as the chips for congkak (a Malay game).
example_ms: Kanak-kanak suka mengutip biji getah bagi dijadikan biji-biji bagi permainan congkak.

definition_ms: garau; parau; kasar dan besar bunyinya
example_semai: Suara Wak Manik ajeh parau medei nyik irungkog ya sih.
example_en: Wak Manik's voice became hoarse and her throat hurt due to having a cold.
example_ms: Suara Wak Manik menjadi garau kerana kerongkongnya sakit akibat dari selsema.

example_semai: Kak pari ajeh kak de bebisa isentak.
example_en: Stingrays are fish that have poisonous tails.
example_ms: Ikan pari adalah ikan yang mempunyai ekor yang berbisa.

example_semai: Bah Kana ya nik gecek kigei ku wad hospitel medei iparu-paru ya rusak. Dokte kipasal, ipunca medei ilei kuat kinyuk rukok.
example_en: Bah Kana has already been in the ward at the hospital for three months, because his lungs are damaged. The doctor has advised that the reason is because he is a heavy smoker.
example_ms: Sudah tiga bulan Bah Kana tinggal di wad, di hospital kerana paru-parunya telah rosak. Doktor memaklumkan bahwa puncanya adalah kerana beliau kuat merokok.

example_semai: "Pegelap huper jok haret nu pasa, harkot lauk," Wak Ramlah kiurok nu igensir.
example_en: "Tomorrow morning, let's go to the market to buy side dishes Ramlah said to her husband.
example_ms: "Esok pagi, mari kita ke pasar bagi membeli lauk-pauk," Wak Ramlah berkata kepada suaminya.

definition_en: to install; to affix; to fasten; to turn on
example_semai: Bah Wan kitulog iapak pasag lampu ku belakang deg.
example_en: Bah Wan helped his father install lights in the backyard.
example_ms: Bah Wan menolong bapanya memasang lampu di belakang rumah.

definition_ms: pasak; tetap; tinggal di satu tempat sahaja
example_semai: "Sebenarnye, jik mai luar tapi ya jipasak ku kampug adeh ya liu, sejak jaman kemoj dot jik lagi," Bah Mali kipasal nu Bah Nan mase ubai besimbang.
example_en: "Actually, we are outsiders but we have been permanently settled in this village for a long time, since the time of our great grandfather," Bah Mali told Bah Nan while they were chatting.
example_ms: "Sebenarnya, kami merupakan orang luar tetapi sudah pasak di kampung ini sudah lama, sejak zaman mendiang moyang kami lagi," Bah Mali memberitahu kepada Bah Nan semasa mereka bersembang.

definition_en: to converse; to talk; to inform
example_semai: "Amen haet ku kedei, pasal nu eng. En ngha-joi," Wak Sali kiurok nu itenek.
example_en: "If you want to go to the store, please inform me. I want to go with you," Wak Sali said to her older brother.
example_ms: "Jika kamu mahu ke kedai, beritahu kepada saya. Saya mahu ikut," Wak Sali berkata kepada abangnya.

definition_en: passover; easter; one of the religious celebration by the Jewish and Christian
definition_ms: paskah; salah satu perayaan keagamaan bagi agama Yahudi dan Kristian
example_semai: "Ku senamut raya Paskah doh, jik mong jibeh caknak ku padeg. Amen engkesenang, beile hol cakcak," Wak Ani kipasal nu ikekawat.
example_en: "At the coming Easter celebration, we will have a feast at home. If you have time, please come and join us," Wak Ani told her friends.
example_ms: "Pada sambutan perayaan Paskah nanti, kami mengadakan jamuan di rumah. Jika kalian mempunyai kelapangan, datanglah untuk makan," Wak Ani memberitahu kepada kawan-kawannya.

example_semai: "Mongka haen paspot? Agok sip, sengoh doh entah mong pules," Bah Miran kipasal nu ikenah mase ubaiha-hol ubaiha-et nu pekan.
example_en: "Did you bring an identity card? Don't forget, because there may be a police check," Bah Miran told his wife as they wanted to go out to town.
example_ms: "Kamu ada membawa kad pengenalan? Jangan lupa, kerana nanti mungkin terdapat polis," Bah Miran memberitahu kepada isterinya semasa mereka mahu keluar untuk ke pekan.

definition_en: to break something to open or to pull oout
definition_ms: mengumpil; mengopak; menanggalkan atau membuka sesuatu supaya tercabut dan terbuka
example_semai: Bah Wan kipat pintu tandas medei hajap biha-buka.
example_en: Bah Wan was forced to break the toilet door because it was too difficult to open it.
example_ms: Bah Wan mengopak pintu tandas kerana susah hendak dibuka.

example_semai: "Amek eng selalo kipateh icecenok medei rajet naitulog bahbeh kerjak deg," Wak Nita kipasal nu igensir.
example_en: "My mother always praises her grandchildren because they are diligent in helping with housework," Wak Nita told her husband.
example_ms: "Ibu saya selalu memuji cucu-cucunya kerana mereka rajin menolong melakukan kerja-kerja rumah," Wak Nita memberitahu suaminya.

example_semai: Bah Nua pek kicak kak paten medei kak ajeh ingoi hanyir.
example_en: Bah Nua did not eat the catfish because of the fetid smell.
example_ms: Bah Nua tidak memakan ikan patin kerana bau ikan tersebut hanyir.

example_semai: Wak Bilung lekat kisuj iabat ku pateu hamapen hunen ya mong teu pili.
example_en: Wak Bilung is still washing her clothes in the river even though there is now tap water.
example_ms: Wak Bilung masih mencuci pakaiannya di sungai walaupun sekarang sudah terdapat air paip.

example_semai: "Agok hiran, keneng legos adeh mimang patik medei ha bikawen," Bah Selah kipasal nu ikekawat mase naihol saje naicekcip nu ikebon.
example_en: "Do not be surprised, this rambutan tree is indeed short because it is grafted," Bah Selah told his friends when they visited his garden.
example_ms: "Jangan hairan, pokok rambutan ini memang pendek kerana ianya rambutan kawin," Bah Selah memberitahu rakan-rakannya semasa mereka ke berjalan-jalan ke kebunnya.

example_semai: Wak Mira kidek ku patik engleg pingat de kikutep medei pek lalu kaha-angkot.
example_en: Wak Mira put down on the ground some of the plates that she had picked up because she didn't have the strength to carry them all.
example_ms: Wak Mira meletakkan di atas tanah sebahagian dari pinggan yang dia kutip kerana dia tidak mampu membawanya.

example_semai: Bah Gilan kipatoh, tok pek kidegel engrok iapak de kior akiperdij ibelajar ku universiti canuk sebelom kibekenah.
example_en: Bah Gilan compiled and did not oppose his father who asked him to finish his studies at university first before getting married.
example_ms: Bah Gilan mematuhi dan tidak membantah bapanya yang menyuruh dia menghabiskan pengajian di universiti terlebih dahulu sebelum mendirikan rumah tangga.

example_semai: Wak Mira mong jeoi iman patong de kikumpol sejak isanget lagi.
example_en: Wak Mira has many dolls that she has collected since childhood.
example_ms: Wak Mira mempunyai banyak patung mainan yang beliau kumpul sejak dari kanak-kanak lagi.

example_semai: Hamapen hik pek sependapat ru enai, tapi pek patot kibekasar nu mai engrak, baik ku bangwal atao ku bahneh hik.
example_en: Although we do not agree with them, we should not be rude to the elders, either in words or in deeds.
example_ms: Walaupun kita tidak bersependapat dengan mereka, tetapi kita tidak wajar untuk berkasar dengan orang tua, sama ada dengan kata-kata mahupun dengan perbuatan.

example_semai: Wak Nani kigemar cak kak patug, jadi selalo kiajak igensir tengroj medei kaha-berkep kak ajeh.
example_en: Wak Nani likes to eat malayan leaffish, so she always ask her husband to fish with her to get that fish.
example_ms: Wak Nani suka memakan ikan patung, jadi dia selalu mengajak suaminya memancing bagi mendapatkan ikan tersebut.

definition_en: bao; a type of yeast-leavened filled bun
example_semai: Wak Sara ihad kaha-belajar bahbeh koi pau medei igensir ru ikekenon naigemar cakcak koi ajeh.
example_en: Wak Sara really wants to learn how to make bao bun because her husband and children like to eat it.
example_ms: Wak Sara sangat ingin mempelajari cara membuat kuih pau kerana suami dan anak-anaknya suka memakan kuih tersebut.

example_semai: Bah Suan kisuj imutu telas kijog ju itengroj medei tebeg ya payak.
example_en: Bah Suan washed his motorcycle after returning from fishing because there was a lot of mud on it.
example_ms: Bah Suan mencuci motosikalnya setelah pulang dari memancing kerana pada motosikalnya itu terdapat banyak lumpur.

example_semai: "Iarik kaha-manik, bor hien payong siap-siap," Wan Ani kiurok nu Wak Nani.
example_en: "It's about to rain, we had better get ready and bring an umbrella," Wak Ani said to Wak Nani.
example_ms: "Hari mahu hujan, lebih baik kita siap-siap membawa payung," Wak Ani berkata kepada Wak Nani.

example_semai: Kipe kuhu-mamuh, tapi tok kimamuh pen lagi.
