example_en: He said (earlier) that he wanted to take a shower, but still has not done it!
example_ms: Dia berkata (tadi) dia mahu mandi, tapi masih tak lagi mandi.

example_semai: Bah Man mong kidek peas ku ideg mahnuh. Mase pai kikep entem, ipeas ajeh had mar tapar teg taleh. Hunen iteninoi ya mar lik entoi.
example_en: Bah Man is raising a shoftshell turtle (which is kept) in the bathroom. At the time he got it, it was only the size of a human palm. Now, it is the size of a large frying pan.
example_ms: Bah Man ada membela labi-labi (yang disimpan) di bilik mandi. Masa dia baharu mendapatnya, saiznya cuma sebesar tapak tangan sahaja. Sekarang, saiznya sudah sebesar kuali yang besar.

definition_en: to control oneself, to practice self-control

example_semai: Pek liu nij, Bah Suki bipecare ku mahkamah ha ikes kilanggar mai engen mutu ku nong timbok gecek canuk jeh.
example_en: Soon, Bah Suki will be tried in court for his case of hitting a motorcyclist on the road.
example_ms: Tidak lama lagi, Bah Suki akan dibicarakan di mahkamah terhadap kesnya yang melanggar pembonceng motosikal di jalanraya.

example_semai: "Mongka teu peceg? Eng ngha-ngut. Beket betol iarik, arik adeh!" Bah Lan kisemanj nu ikenah telas kijog ju iselai.
example_en: "Is there any cold water? I want to drink. The weather is very hot today!" Bah Lan asked his wife after he returned from his farm.
example_ms: "Ada air sejuk tak? Saya mahu minum. Cuaca sangat panas hari ini!" Bah Lan meminta kepada isterinya setelah dia pulang dari ladangnya.

example_semai: Restoran Radzi naiog caknak pecume nu mai jengoi medei kedei ajeh pai bibuka ku arik ajeh.
example_en: Radzi Restaurant provided free food to public in conjunction with the opening of the shop on that day.
example_ms: Restoran Radzi memberikan makanan percuma kepada orang ramai sempena pembukaan kedai itu pada hari tersebut.

definition_en: to play a prank; to do a joke or mischievous act
definition_ms: mendajal; perbuatan nakal terhadap orang lain sama ada bertujuan untuk bergurau atau sememangnya berniat sedemikian
example_semai: Bah Asim kigemar pedajal ha mai, pek kira sangetka engrak.
example_en: Bah Asim likes to play pranks on people, regardless of whether they are children or the elderly.
example_ms: Bah Asim suka mendajal orang, tidak mengira pada kanak-kanak atau pada orang tua.

example_semai: "Pedas betol sambal kak mileh menasak hek adeh!" Wak Lila kiurok nu ikenon menaleh.
example_en: "The anchovy sambal that you cooked is very spicy!" Wak Lila said to her daughter.
example_ms: "Sangat pedas sambal ikan bilis yang kamu masak ini!" Wak Lila berkata kepada anak gadisnya.

example_semai: "Tulog peder nu apak dea kinyong eng ku sekulah," Wak Lia kipasal nu iamek ku kateh talipon.
example_en: "Please tell dad to meet me up at the school," Wak Lia told his mother via a phone call.
example_ms: "Tolong beritahu kepada bapa supaya dia menjemput saya di sekolah," Wak Lia memberitahu ibunya menerusi panggilan telefon.

example_semai: Bah Nan kipediam ilei hamapen bitanya bekali-kali ya iapak medei kipanei ilei ya kibeh salah.
example_en: Bah Nan remained silent despite being repeatedly asked by his father because he knew he had done something wrong.
example_ms: Bah Nan mendiam diri (sahaja) walaupun ditanya berkali-kali oleh bapanya kerana dia tahu dia telah berbuat salah.

example_semai: Bah Atan pek kipeduli amen mong mai naihol nu imadeg medei kek mimang kigei kihalei.
example_en: Bah Atan does not care whether anyone comes to his house or not, because he lives alone.
example_ms: Bah Atan tidak mempeduli jika ada sesiapa yang datang ke rumahnya kerana dia memang tinggal bersendirian.

example_semai: "Ngoi kak piya ajeh peeg hamapen ya bigureng. Pek lalu ngha-cak," engrok amek Wak Tia.
example_en: "The smell of the talipia fish is fishy even though it has been fried. I can't eat it," said Wak Tia's mother.
example_ms: "Bau ikan talipia itu hanyir walaupun ianya sudah digoreng. Tidak boleh saya memakannya," kata ibu kepada Wak Tia.

definition_ms: bocor; mempunyai lohong, lubang tiris pada bahagian permukaan atau bahagian dalam
example_semai: Bah Gani kitukar ipelog deg medei ilei de manah ya jeoi peg amen kimanik.
example_en: Bah Gani changed the roof of his house because if it rained, there were many leaks.
example_ms: Bah Gani menukar atap rumahnya kerana sekiranya hujan, terdapat banyak kebocoran pada atap yang lama.

example_semai: "Peg sanget ajeh," amek eng kiurok nu eng ha menang jik mase jiha-lintas nong.
example_en: "Lead that child by the hand," my mother said to me about my younger sister as we were about to cross the street.
example_ms: "Pimpin (tangan) budak itu," ibu saya berkata kepada saya mengenai adik kami semasa kami mahu melintas jalan.

example_semai: "Angkat baju ku cenglei, iarik ya kaha-peg!" Wak Nita kiurok nu ikenon.
example_en: "Get the clothes on the washing line, it's about to rain!" Wak Nita said to her daughter.
example_ms: "Angkat baju di ampaian, hari sudah mahu hujan!" Wak Nita berkata kepada anaknya.

example_semai: "Og madeh engleg, eng ngtulog pegag arap hek," Wak Yan kiurok nu Wak Kina.
example_en: "Give me some of your things, I can help you hold them," Wak Yan said to Wak Kina.
example_ms: "Beri sebahagian barang-barang kamu, saya boleh tolong pegang," Wak Yan berkata kepada Wak Kina.

definition_ms: pagi; menggambarkan keadaan pagi pada pukul 8 pagi sehingga 11 pagi, iaitu ketika matahari baru terbit.
example_semai: Bor pegelap!
example_en: Good morning!
example_ms: Selamat pagi!

example_semai: Cep peger adeh begei ha pog, tapi lagi lawa ikale.
example_en: The pheasant looks like chicken, but its color is more beautiful.
example_ms: Ayam pegar adalah seperti ayam, tetapi warnya lebih cantik.

definition_en: to boil (as a way of cooking)
definition_ms: merebus; memasak sesuatu menggunakan air panas dengan cara merendam atau membiarkan bahan-bahan tersebut di dalam air yang sedang panas
example_semai: Wak Mali kipegoh kak puyu ru tarog ha caknak tengah arik enai.
example_en: Wak Mali boiled the puyu fish with the tapioca shoots as their lunch.
example_ms: Wak Mali merebus ikan puyu dengan pucuk ubi sebagai makanan tengah hari mereka.

definition_ms: menjerang (air); memanaskan air di dalam cerek atau sesuatu bekas yang lain supaya masak
example_semai: Bah Tam kipegoh teu kaha-beh teh.
example_en: Bah Tam boils water to make tea.
example_ms: Bah Tam menjerang air bagi membuat air teh.

example_semai: Cengroi pulot biasajeh bipegop nukenglem canuk sebelom bimasak.
example_en: Usually the glutinous rice will be soaked overnight before being cooked.
example_ms: Biasanya beras pulut akan direndam semalaman sebelum dimasak.

example_semai: Seluar de bilog ya Bah Lan ajeh peh lei kijag engleg imol seluar ajeh.
example_en: The trousers worn by Bah Lan were so long that he stepped on one trouser leg.
example_ms: Seluar yang dipakai oleh Bah Lan adalah labuh sehingga dia memijak sebahagian hujung seluar tersebut.

definition_en: to break; to snap in two
example_semai: Bah Kamal kipeh memerip batag rangas, kaha-beh bara os ha reknag kak.
example_en: Bah Kamal broke some pieces of  firewood to make kindling to roast a fish.
example_ms: Bah Kamal mematahkan berberapa batang kayu api bagi membuat bara api untuk memanggang ikan.

example_semai: "Cekgu, Bah Man kiurok ilei pek kisekulah arik adeh medei pehod iej," Bah Rani kipasal nu icekgu kelas.
example_en: "Teacher, Bah Man said he did not come to school today because his stomach hurt," Bah Rani told his class teacher.
example_ms: "Cikgu, Bah Man berkata dia tidak bersekolah pada hari ini kerana perutnya sakit," Bah Rani memberitahu kepada guru kelasnya.

example_semai: Baju kurong Wak Pina ha raya tahut adeh iwarne pehog.
example_en: Wak Pina's aditional dress for this year's Eid celebration is yellow.
example_ms: Baju kurung Wak Pina bagi sambutan hari raya pada tahun ini bewarna kuning.

definition_en: to crush; to squeeze; to press
definition_ms: menghimpit; memicit; menyebabkan sesuatu ruang pada sesuatu (samada benda atau sebagainya) menjadi lebih sempit dengan cara menghimpit atau memicit
example_semai: Bah Kamil kijap medei bipeit ya itenek mase naimain gusti-gusti, engrok liknai.
example_en: Bah Kamil cried because he was being crushed by his brother while they were playing. They said they were only pretending to wrestle.
example_ms: Bah Kamil menangis kerana dihimpit oleh abangnya semasa mereka bermain. Mereka berkata, mereka hanya berolok-olok sedang gusti.

example_semai: Wak Bella kipej batu nu kateh kulam, de kibeh dij kak de mong ku kulam ajeh naikekjet.
example_en: Wah Bella threw a stone into the pond which scared all the fish in it.
example_ms: Wah Bella melempari batu ke dalam kolam yang membuatkan semua ikan yang terdapat di dalam kolam tersebut menjadi terkejut.

example_semai: Pejabat Pos bitutop ku arik Sabtu ru minggu.
example_en: The Post Office is closed on Saturdays and Sundays.
example_ms: Pejabat Pos ditutup pada hari Sabtu dan Ahad.

example_semai: Wak Siti kipejam imat sekejap medei imat kijadi leteh ha telampau kineng ha laptop.
example_en: Wak Siti closed her eyes for a moment because her eyes were tired due to looking at the laptop screen.
example_ms: Wak Siti memejamkan matanya sekejap kerana matanya letih akibat terlampau melihat kepada skrin laptop.

definition_en: to act like a man in the way she dressed, talk and behaved (to describe a woman)
definition_ms: sengaja berkelakuan seperti lelaki dari segi cara berpakaian, percakapan dan tingkahlaku (untuk menggambarkan seorang perempuan)
example_semai: "Wak Mila ajeh kipenjantan ha ilei, kipiker bor gamak kilog baju begei ha keral anen?" Wak Tila kipasal nu iamek ha imenang ajeh.
example_en: "Wak Mila behaves like a man. Does she think dressing like that man is a good thing to do?" Wak Tila told her mother about her sister.
example_ms: "Wak Mila berkelakuan seperti lelaki, dia fikir dengan berpakaian seperti lelaki itu adalah tindakan yang baik?" Wak Tila memberitahu ibunya tentang adiknya tersebut.

example_semai: Wak Mary kigemar cak pejet, tapi doh mesti deras nyik iej. Hamapen begei ajeh, kek lekat kigemar cak pejet.
example_en: Wak Mary likes to eat spicy food, but afterwards she will suffer from stomach ache. Even so, she still enjoys eating spicy food.
example_ms: Wak Mary suka memakan makanan pedas, tetapi selepas itu mesti mengalami sakit perut. Sungguhpun begitu, dia masih tetap suka memakan makanan pedas.

example_semai: "Lalu jog hek ajeh, eng ngha-pejos jog-ng," Wak Sara kipasal nu Wak Mar.
example_en: "Move your feet to the side a bit, I want to straighten my legs," Wak Sara told Wak Mar.
example_ms: "Ketepikan sikit kaki kamu itu, saya meluruskan kaki saya," Wak Sara berkata kepada Wak Mar.

example_semai: Bah Sirap tok pek kipoj lei manik kihoj canuk sebelom kijog nu madeg, kek kibercip taleh imutu ku balak manik.
example_en: Bah Sirap did not wait until the rain stopped first before returning home, but he kept riding his motorcycle in the rain.
example_ms: Bah Sirap tidak menunggu sehingga hujan berhenti terlebih dahulu sebelum pulang ke rumah, tetapi dia tetap menunggang motosikalnya di dalam hujan untuk pulang.

example_semai: "Eng ngha-et nu pekan, entah mong ma-ma de haha-kirep?" Bah Dahlan kiurok nu ikenah.
example_en: Bah Dahlan said to his wife, "I want to go to town. Do you have anything that you want me to buy?"
example_ms: "Saya mahu pergi ke pekan, kamu ada apa-apa pesanan yang mahu saya belikan?" Bah Dahlan berkata kepada isterinya.

definition_en: to act like a man in the way she dressed, talk and behaved (to describe a woman)
definition_ms: sengaja berkelakuan seperti lelaki dari segi cara berpakaian, percakapan dan tingkahlaku (untuk menggambarkan seorang perempuan)

example_semai: Wak Rina kipekjot telas kikos ju ibuknet.
example_en: Wak Rina stretched as soon as she woke up from sleep.
example_ms: Wak Rina meregangkan badannya sebaik sahaja bangun dari tidur.

example_semai: "Peklat duet eng de ngdek ku mije sulek hek adeh kelemnek. Tah bok de kekkot, ya hekka?" Bah Ramli kitanya nu ikenah.
example_en: "The money I put on your makeup table has vanished. I wonder who took it. Did you?" Bah Ramli asked his wife.
example_ms: "Duit yang saya simpan di atas meja solek kamu ini sudah hilang. Siapakah yang mengambilnya, kamukah?" Bah Ramli bertanya kepada isterinya.

example_semai: Bah Salam bidenda ya cekgu medei pekseg kiseryap ikerjak sekulah de biog minggu canukjeh.
example_en: Bah Salam was punished by his teacher because he had not yet finished the homework that had been given the previous week.
example_ms: Bah Salam didenda oleh gurunya kerana belum menyiapkan kerja sekolah yang diberikan pada minggu lepas lagi.

example_semai: Taya mutu Bah Kupei peladoh mase ku iceknip kaha-et nu kerjak. Kerana ajehleh kek lingah kitiba nu itempat kerjak.
example_en: Bah Kupei's motorcycle tyre exploded while he was on his way to work. Because of that he was late arriving there.
example_ms: Tayar motosikal Bah Kupei meletup semasa dia dalam perjalanan mahu ke tempat kerja. Kerana itulah dia lambat tiba ke tempat kerjanya.

example_semai: "Biar cip pelahat, asal taleh selamat halei," Wak Nila kibesat nu ikenon litau mase kaha-et nu ikerjak.
example_en: "It's okay if you are slow, as long as you arrive safely," Wak Nila instructed her son when he wanted to go to work.
example_ms: "Tidak mengapa jika perlahan, asalkan kamu selamat sampai," Wak Nila berpesan kepada anaknya ketika beliau mahu ke tempat kerja.

definition_ms: pelan; rajah yang menggambarkan bentuk atau gambar sesuatu (bangunan, rumah, kawasan tanah dsbnya)
example_semai: "Pelan deg jik ya kaha-siap. Telas ajeh, ya bulehle mula bibeh deg jik," Bah Nua kipasal nu ikekawat.
example_en: "The floor plan of our house is almost ready. Only after that, the construction of our house can begin," Bah Nua told his friends.
example_ms: "Pelan rumah kami sudah hampir siap. Setelah itu, baharulah pembinaan rumah kami boleh dijalankan," Bah Nua memberitahu rakan-rakannya.

example_semai: Perut Wah Eca pelcos mendei bilir kibahek.
example_en: Wa Eca's stomach is bulging because he is too full.
example_ms: Perut Wa Eca buncit sebab dia terlalu kenyang.

definition_ms: melecur; melepuh atau mengelupas kerana terkena benda panas (cth: air panas, api, dsbnya)
example_semai: Teg Wak Ima kipeleh medei tekena ya teu beket.
example_en: Wak Ima's hand was burnt by hot water.
example_ms: Tangan Wak Ima melecur kerana terkena air panas.

example_semai: Wak Hani kipelehem cerek kineng ha ragam ikekenon de naipesipah tempat buknet enai de ya kisiap bor-bor pegelap cinek.
example_en: Wak Hani sighed after seeing the behavior of her children who had made their bedroom messy which she had tidied up in the morning.
example_ms: Wak Hani mengeluh melihat perangai anak-anaknya yang menyepahkan bilik tidur mereka sendiri yang sudah dia kemas pada waktu pagi dengan sangat baik.

example_semai: "Seminggu sebelom kidat, jik ya jiperasan ilei iperangei ya pelek. Selalo kitemenung ru kibelwal kihalei," Wak Rina kipasal nu ikekawat ha iapak jop.
example_en: "A week before he died, we noticed that he was behaving strangely. He was always daydreaming and talking to himself," Wak Rina told her friends about her grandfather.
example_ms: "Seminggu sebelum dia meninggal dunia, kami sudah perasan bahawa sudah berkelakuan dengan pelik. Dia selalu termenung dan bercakap bersendirian," Wak Rina memberitahu kawan-kawannya mengenai datuknya.

example_semai: Wak Norah kigemar cak pelek sempak.
example_en: Wak Norah likes to eat durians.
example_ms: Wak Norah suka memakan buah durian.

example_semai: Ku gecek canuk, Bah Rilan bipermuj nu hospitel medei baha-bedah, baha-buang ipelek wog ilei englag de ya rusak ajeh.
example_en: Last month, Bah Rilan was admitted to hospital for surgery to remove one of his kidneys which had malfunctioned.
example_ms: Pada bulan yang lepas, Bah Rilan dimasukan ke hospital kerana mahu dibedah bagi membuang sebelah buah pinggangnya yang telah rosak.

example_semai: Pelep adeh jeoi naihol telas arik manik.
example_en: Leeches become active after rain.
example_ms: Pacat selalunya menjadi aktif setelah waktu hujan.

example_semai: Wak Nani pek kicak kak paten medei pek kilalu ha ingoi de pelih.
example_en: Wak Nani did not eat the iridescent shark fish because she could not stand the nauseating smell.
example_ms: Wak Nani tidak memakan ikan patin kerana tidak tahan akan baunya yang hanyir.

example_semai: "Canak ajeh lekat pelit, medei pai kinam. Poj sekejap dea ya kurang gek kibeket, baruleh hicakcak," amek kiurok nu jik.
example_en: "The rice is still hot because it has been freshly cooked. Wait for a while until it becomes a little less hot then we eat," my mother told us.
example_ms: "Nasi tersebut masih panas kerana ianya baharu masak. Tunggu sekejap sehingga menjadi kurang sedikit hangat baharulah kita makan," ibu berkata kepada kami.

example_semai: "Beket betol ku luar, lei pelit hirasa kena ya pengrag!" Bah Wan kiurok nu ikekawat.
example_en: "It's very hot outside now until one feels a stinging pain when exposed to the sun!" Bah Wan said to his friends.
example_ms: "Di luar sekarang sangat panas, sehingga terasa pedih jika terkena pancaran matahari!" Bah Wan berkata kepada kawan-kawannya.

example_semai: Mai manah entem naiguna pelite sebelom mong lektreg.
example_en: People in ancient times used lamps before the advent of electricity.
example_ms: Orang pada zaman dahulu menggunakan pelita sebelum adanya elektrik.

definition_ms: atap; penutup rumah bahagian atas yang diperbuat daripada zink, daun pokok bertam, daun rumbia, daun nipah, dsbnya.
example_semai: "Pelog deg jik de bibeh ju beltop ya peg. Adeh hunen gensir eng ya kiet kibeli pelog zink dea tahan liu gek," Wak Ramlah kipasal nu iamek.
example_en: "The roof of our house which is made of bertam leaves has leaked. Now my husband is going to buy a zinc roof so that it will last longer," Wak Ramlah told her mother.
example_ms: "Atap rumah kami yang diperbuat dari bertam sudah bocor. Sekarang suami saya pergi membeli atap zink supaya tahan lebih lama," Wak Ramlah memberitahu ibunya.

example_semai: "Hapeloh nij empoj, lekat beloj gulei kak hek adeh," Wak Nita kiurok nu ikenon menaleh.
example_en: "Put more salt into the fish dish that you have cooked because the taste is still bland," Wak Nita said to her daughter.
example_ms: "Kamu letakkan lagi garam ke gulai ikan yang kamu masak ini kerana rasanya ianya masih tawar," Wak Nita berkata kepada anak gadisnya.

example_semai: Pelong deg Bah Rani biperheg ya pinoi mase manik kelem jihnek.
example_en: The roof of Bah Rani's house was blown away by the wind during the rain last night.
example_ms: Atap rumah Bah Rani kiterbangkan oleh angin semasa hujan malam tadi.

example_semai: Wak Wajah ajeh numom pelukes ru kek kigemar belukes gambar pemandangan begei ha gambar deg ku tepi pantei.
example_en: Wak Wajah is a painter and she loves to draw pictures of scenes such as pictures of houses by the beach.
example_ms: Wak Wajah adalah seorang pelukis dan dia suka melukis gambar pemandangan seperti gambar rumah di tepi pantai.

example_semai: Amen hiha-beli peluru, hod mong lisen canuk. Pek buleh hibeli sebarang, doh bicap ya pules.
example_en: We must have a license if we want to buy ammunition and can not just buy it without because we will be arrested by the police.
example_ms: Kita haruslah mempunyai lesen jika mahu membeli peluru dan tidak boleh dengan sebarangan membelinya kerana akan ditangkap oleh polis.

example_semai: "Ha pinjam pembares hek, bulehka?" Wak Ana kitanya nu Wak Jiji.
example_en: "Can I borrow your ruler?" Wak Ana asked Wak Jiji.
example_ms: "Bolehkah saya meminjam pembaris kamu?" Wak Ana bertanya kepada Wak Jiji.

definition_en: to warm up body by the fire
example_semai: Telas ya sudah naibepanah kak, Bah Kudil ru ikekawat naipempom os canuk naiha-berket berog enai sebelom naijog nu madeg.
example_en: After they had finished fishing with spears in the river, Bah Kudil and his friends warmed up by the fire before returning home.
example_ms: Setelah mereka memanah ikan di sungai, Bah Kudil dan kawan-kawannya memanaskan badan pada unggun api terlebih dahulu sebelum pulang ke rumah.

definition_en: also; attached to a word that it is intended to emphasize
example_semai: "Eng pen nghod juga!" Wak Lila kiurok nu igensir bile kineng igensir ajeh kicak aes krem.
example_en: "I want one, too!" Wak Lila said to her husband when she saw him eating an ice cream.
example_ms: "Saya pun mahu juga!" Wak Lila berkata kepada suaminya apabila melihat suaminya tersebut memakan ais krim.

example_semai: Bah Lan bipet ya iapak medei kipenaas ha cok.
example_en: Bah Lan was beaten by his father for tormenting a dog.
example_ms: Bah Lan dipukul oleh bapanya kerana dia menyeksa anjing.

definition_en: panadol; a brand of paracetamol; generic name for painkilling medicines
example_semai: Wak Laili kingut Penadol medei ha tengor ikui.
example_en: Wak Laili took a Panadol due to dizziness.
example_ms: Wak Laili meminum Panadol kerana pening kepala.

example_semai: "Agok pendak caknak, amen hapanei pek lalu haha-perdij, bahagik nu mai kilek," amek kiurok nu menang jik mase jicakcak ku restoren.
example_en: “Don’t waste food, if you know you can’t finish your food, give the half to someone else,” mother said to our sister when we ate at the restaurant.
example_ms: "Jangan membazir makanan, jika kamu tahu kamu tidak mampu menghabiskan makanan kamu, berikan separuh kepada orang lain," ibu berkata kepada adik kami semasa kami makan di restoran.

example_semai: Bah Lan kibeh deg pender ku ibelakang deg, tempat kaha-jekjop.
example_en: Bah Lan built a hut behind his house as a place to roast fish, chicken or meat.
example_ms: Bah Lan membuat pondok di belakang rumahnya sebagai tempat untuk membakar ikan, ayam atau daging.

example_semai: Gerig gemar naipendig ku balak pengrag medei naiha-berket berog enai.
example_en: Monitor lizards like to sunbathe because they want to warm their bodies.
example_ms: Biawak suka berjemur di tengah panas kerana ingin memanaskan badan mereka.

example_semai: Mai kampug sama-sama naikor penep nukali nutahut.
example_en: The villagers work together to clean the cemetery once a year.
example_ms: Orang kampung bekerjasama menebas (membersihkan) kawasan perkuburan sekali bagi setiap tahun.

example_semai: Bah Kala kipengaroh ikekawat sij besi.
example_en: Bah Kala enticed his friends to steal metal.
example_ms: Bah Kala mempengaruhi kawan-kawanya untuk mencuri besi.

definition_ms: pembaris; kayu pembaris; kayu pengukur; sejenis alat untuk membuat garisan lurus dsbnya yang diperbuat daripada kayu, logam, besi, dll
example_semai: "Mongka penggares hek? Ngha-pinjam sekejap," Bah Gani kisemanj nu Wak Kila.
example_en: "Do you have a ruler?" Bah Gani asked Wak Kila. "I want to borrow it for a while."
example_ms: "Kamu ada pembariskah? Saya mahu pinjamnya sebentar," Bah Gani meminta kepada Wak Kila.

definition_ms: kefahaman; pengetahuan tentang sesuatu perkara; sesuatu penjelasan yang boleh diterima pakai
example_semai: Aman hijoi ju pengham mai engrak manah, sej binatag ju darat tok buleh bicak sama ru sej binatag ju kenteu, medei mong ipantag-larang.
example_en: Based on the understanding of the ancients, meat of animals that live in the forest should not be eaten together with the meat of animals that live in water because it is taboo.
example_ms: Berdasarkan kefahaman orang tua dahulu kala, daging dari binatang yang tinggal di hutan tidak boleh dimakan bersama-sama dengan daging binatang yang tinggal di dalam air kerana terdapat pantang-larangnya.

example_semai: Ku jap-jap kampug mesti mong numom penghulu de kijaga ha kampug ajeh.
example_en: In each village there must be a headman who takes care of the village.
example_ms: Di setiap kampung mesti terdapat seorang penghulu yang menjaga kampung tersebut.

example_semai: "Agok nyek, ngut penglai amen hahod deras dea habor," Bah Tali kiurok nu ikenon de kinyek ngut penglai.
example_en: "Do not refuse, take the medicine if you want to recover quickly," Bah Tali said to his son who was refusing to take it.
example_ms: "Jangan menolak, minum ubat jika kamu mahu cepat sembuh," Bah Tali berkata kepada anaknya yang enggan meminum ubat.

example_semai: Wak Lila kigureng pengleg ha ianak buah.
example_en: Wak Lila fried the eggs for her niece.
example_ms: Wak Lila menggoreng telur bagi anak buahnya.

example_semai: "Ipengliu Wak Ujas kimamuh! Eng ya lingah ngha-et kerjak!" itenek kipasal nu amek enai.
example_en: "Wak Ujas has been in the bathroom a long time! I'm already late for work!" her sister said to their mother.
example_ms: "Wak Ujas mandi sangat lama! Saya sudah terlambat untuk ke tempat kerja!" kakaknya berkata kepada ibu mereka.

definition_ms: sinaran matahari; keadaan panas (kepanasan) matahari yang menyinar
example_semai: "Kuat pengrag arik adeh, pek lei 2 jam ya sot abat hik," Wak Nita kipasal nu iamek.
example_en: "The sun is very strong today, in less than 2 hours our clothes will be dry," Wak Nita told her mother.
example_ms: "Sinaran matahari sangat kuat hari ini, tidak sampai 2 jam pakaian kita sudah kering," Wak Nita memberitahu ibunya.

example_semai: "Pengres pog amek eng jooi," Bah Uda kisureh nu ikekawat.
example_en: "There are a lot of domesticated chickens raised by my mother," Bah Uda told his friends.
example_ms: "Terdapat banyak ayam ternakan ibu saya," Bah Uda memberitahu rakan-rakannya.

definition_ms: anak angkat; anak orang lain yang diambil menjadi anak sendiri dan dipelihara serta dianggap seakan-akan anak sendiri
example_semai: Bah Uda ajeh kenon pengres, kek pek kikenal iamek-apak ilei de sebenar.
example_en: Bah Uda is an adopted child, he does not know his biological parents.
example_ms: Bah Uda adalah anak angkat, dia tidak mengenali ibu bapanya yang sebenar.

definition_ms: pengumpan; sesuatu yang digunakan untuk menarik perhatian ikan supaya menghampiri mata kail, jaring, perangkap atau umpan yang dipasang
example_semai: "Penegros ma de haguna ajeh?" Bah Bam kitanya nu Bah Lee mase ubai tengroj.
example_en: "What feeder bait are you using?" Bah Bam asked Bah Lee while they were fishing.
example_ms: "Pengumpan apakah yang kamu gunakan?" Bah Bam bertanya kepada Bah Lee semasa mereka berdua memancing.

example_semai: "Lekat ngoi pengus abat adeh padehal pai ngsah," Wak Manik kiurok nu kateh isengik.
example_en: "This cloth still smells musty even though I just washed it," Wak Manik said to herself.
example_ms: "Kain ini masih berbau hapak walaupun saya baharu mencucinya," Wak Manik berkata di dalam hatinya.

example_semai: "Peninah punya cok! Kicoh ratah ku simin deg hik," engrok Bah Jamil kiberiknus kihalei-halei.
example_en: "Wretched dog! Its poop is everywhere on the cement," Bah Jamil said angrily to himself.
example_ms: "Celaka punya anjing! Berak merata-rata di atas simen rumah," kata Bah Jamil yang marah-marah seorang diri.

example_semai: Bah Kamil kinyok rukok ru ikekawat ku piknoj bas ku simpang kampug telas naiceleh ju bas sekulah. Aleh-aleh iapak kilalu ku pajeh. Ku peninen juga Bah Kamil bitampar ya iapak medei bibakek kinyok rukok.
example_en: Bah Kamil was smoking cigarettes with his friends at the bus stop, at the village junction after getting off the school bus. Suddenly his father passed by that place. At the very same place his father slapped him because he found him smoking.
example_ms: Bah Kamil menghisap rokok dengan kawan-kawannya di perhentian bas di simpang kampung setelah turun dari bas sekolah. Tiba-tiba bapanya lalu di tempat itu. Di situ juga dia ditampar oleh bapanya kerana kedapatan dia merokok.

example_semai: "Eng ngha-gei ku peninoh taleh, ngsegat jekjeg nu diteh. Pekma nggei nghalei, hamapen nyak ju mai kilek," ajok kiurok nu amek jik mase ilei bipujok dea kigei ru jik.
example_en: "I want to stay just here, I'm too lazy to move over there. It does not matter if I live alone, even far from other people," our grandmother said to our mother as she was being persuaded to live with us.
example_ms: "Saya mahu tinggal di sini sahaja, saya malas untuk berpindah ke sana. Tidak mengapa jika saya tinggal bersendirian, walaupun jauh dari orang lain," nenek berkata kepada ibu kami semasa dia dipujuk untuk tinggal bersama-sama dengan kami.

definition_ms: Pentakosta; salah satu dari perayaan utama agama Kristian
example_semai: Pentakosta adeh raya mai Kristian de naisamut ha peringatan teniba Kelog Santen nu pengikot-pengikot Yesus.
example_en: Pentecost is a Christian celebration that they celebrate in remembrance of the coming of the Holy Spirit on the followers of Jesus.
example_ms: Pentakosta adalah perayaan orang Kristian yang mereka sambut bagi mengingati kedatangan Roh Kudus ke atas pengikut-pengikut Yesus.

example_semai: "Bah Ami ajeh mai penteng ku kateh mesyuarat adeh, yama ilei pek kihol?" bos jik kitanya mase jibemesyuarat cinek.
example_en: "Bah Ami is the important person in this meeting, why is he not present?" our boss asked during our meeting just now.
example_ms: "Bah Ami adalah orang yang penting di dalam mesyuarat ini, kenapa dia tidak hadir?" majikan kami bertanya semasa kami bermesyuarat sebentar tadi.

example_semai: "Baca cerite adeh nar ayat penumom," Wak Ani kiurok nu ianak murid.
example_en: "Everyone should read two verses from this story," Wak Ani said to her students.
example_ms: "Setiap orang harus membaca dua ayat pada cerita ini," Wak Ani berkata kepada anak muridnya.

example_semai: "Pog hik dij nainyik, tah kena penyaket ma?" Wak Lila kiurok nu iamek.
example_en: "Our chickens are all sick, don't you know what disease they have?" Wak Lila said to her mother.
example_ms: "Ayam kita semua sakit, tidak tahu mereka terkena penyakit apa?" Wak Lila berkata kepada ibunya.

definition_en: to cook food by using a bamboo stem as a container to keep the food while heating up
definition_ms: memasak makanan dengan kaedah menggunakan batang buluh sebagai bekas untuk menyimpan makanan tersebut semasa pemanasan
example_semai: "Pog de bipeoh adeh sedap icaknak," Cikgu Muhammad kipasal nu Cikgu Azman.
example_en: "Chicken that is put in a bamboo stem and then roasted is delicious," Cikgu Muhammad said to Cikgu Azman.
example_ms: "Ayam yang dimasukkan di dalam buluh, kemudian dibakar ini sedap rasanya," Cikgu Muhammad berkata kepada Cikgu Azman.

example_semai: "Pep itemagik, beketka tok?" Bah Derus kipasal nu ikenah dea kirasa kenon ubai ajeh kinyaknyikka tok.
example_en: "Touch his forehead, is it hot?" Bah Derus told his wife to find out if their child had a fever.
example_ms: "Sentuh dahinya, panaskah tidak?" Bah Derus memberitahu kepada isterinya bagi mengetahui anak mereka demam atau tidak.

example_semai: Wak Jalina kibaca pepa kelemnek, medei kelemnek pek sempat kibaca.
example_en: Wak Jalina is reading yesterday's newspaper because she did not have the time to read it yesterday.
example_ms: Wak Jalina membaca suratkhabar semalam kerana tidak sempat membacanya semalam.

example_semai: Pepegelap Bah Tam ya kikus kaha-et nu mutong.
example_en: Bah Tam already woke up at dawn to go rubber tapping.
example_ms: Awal-awal pagi lagi Bah Tam sudah bangun dari tidur untuk  pergi menoreh.

example_semai: Wak Kila pek pernah kibakek iapak jop medei ya kidat ku mase jaman perag Jepon entem.
example_en: Wak Kila never met her grandfather because he had died at the time of the war with the Japanese.
example_ms: Wak Kila tidak pernah berjumpa dengan datuknya kerana beliau telah meninggal dunia pada waktu perperangan dengan pihak Jepun dahulu.

example_semai: Wak Ati ru ikekawat naiet nu serag naiha-kek pelek perah.
example_en: Wah Ati and his friends went to the forest to look for the tapos fruit.
example_ms: Wah Ati dan kawan-kawannya pergi ke hutan bagi mencari buah perah.

example_semai: Bah Jali ru Bah Nana ubai huj perahu ubai res teu dem.
example_en: Bah Jali and Bah Nana used a boat to cross the dam.
example_ms: Bah Jali dan Bah Nana menaiki perahu menyeberangi empangan.

definition_en: splash in (e.g. of rain) into a room, etc.
definition_ms: tempias; air hujan yang termasuk sedikit ke dalam sesuatu (rumah, bangunan, kenderaan, dll)
example_semai: "Tutop tingkap ajeh doh perai nu kateh deg," Wak Mila kipasal nu imenang mase iarik ya mula kimanik.
example_en: "Close the window, otherwise the rain will splash into the house," Wak Mila told her sister as it started to rain.
example_ms: "Tutup tingkap tersebut, jika tidak akan tempias ke dalam rumah," Wak Mila memberitahu adiknya semasa hujan mulai turun.

definition_ms: sedikit demi sedikit; melakukan sesuatu secara beransur-ansur sehingga menjadi selesai atau menjadi banyak
example_semai: Bah Lan kiterbor ideg perakgek-prakgek medei pek cukop iduet.
example_en: Bah Lan repaired his house little by little because he did not have enough money to pay all at once.
example_ms: Bah Lan memperbaiki rumahnya sedikit demi sedikit kerana tidak mempunyai duit yang mencukupi.

example_semai: Peralet-ralet lampu raya ku deg Wah Siti.
example_en: The decorative lights for the festival at Wak Siti's house were flashing.
example_ms: Lampu perhiasan perayaan di rumah Wak Siti itu berkelip-kelip.

example_semai: Peralit baju Wak Ugus ya manig.
example_en: Wak Ugus's shirt looks shiny because of the beads.
example_ms: Baju Wak Ugus kelihatan berkilauan kerana manik-manik.

example_semai: "Jok hijog aderas, peralit ngrasa bikap ya kemet!" engrok Wak Tina nu itenek mase baileh lada ku kebon.
example_en: "Let's go home immediately, I feel itchy (which is painful) because I have been bitten by a mosquito!" Wak Tina said to her sister as they both picked peppers in the garden.
example_ms: "Jom segera pulang, saya rasa gatal (yang memedihkan) digigit oleh nyamuk!" Wak Tina berkata kepada kakaknya semasa mereka berdua memetik lada di kebun.

definition_ms: satu persatu; satu demi satu mengikut giliran; tiap satu
example_semai: "Ajok eng ya kiengrak, iumur ya 88 tahut. Ilei kenon de iloj ru kihalei taleh de lekat sui ju dikidj iberadek. Dij kirasa peranek-peranek itetenek naidat," Bah Miran kipasal nu ikenah mase ubai becerite ha keluarge liubai memaseg.
example_en: "My grandmother is old, she is 88 years old. She is the youngest child and she is the only one that is still alive among the siblings. One by one, she experienced the loss of each of her siblings," Bah Miran told his wife as they talked about their respective families.
example_ms: "Nenek saya sudaha tua, umurnya sudah 88 tahun. Dia anak bongsu dan hanya dia seorang sahaja yang masih hidup di antara adik-beradiknya. Dia merasai setiap kehilangan satu persatu abang-abang dan kakak-kakaknya," Bah Miran memberitahu isterinya semasa mereka bercerita mengenai keluarga mereka masing-masing.

example_semai: Bah Mali ihad kibelal ru perangei ikenon de pek raktik. Ku kedei caknak ikenon ajeh kijerjar ditah-madeh, langsong pek buleh kigei tempat.
example_ms: Bah Mali amat marah dengan perangai anaknya yang tidak faham bahasa. Anaknya tersebut berlari ke sana-sini di restoran dan tidak boleh duduk setempat.

definition_ms: perangkap; sesuatu untuk menahan benda (binatang, benda atau manusia) yang hendak ditangkap
example_semai: Wak Tila kidek perangkap kenik ku kerop ikatel medei  jeoi kenik ku kateh ibilek.
example_en: Wak Tila placed a rat trap under her bed as there were many rats in her bedroom.
example_ms: Wak Tila meletakkan perangkap tikus di bawah katilnya kerana terdapat banyak tikus di dalam biliknya.

example_semai: Bah Sam pek kitutop tingkap mase manik, jadi ibilek keaj ya perasoi teu manik.
example_en: Bah Sam did not close the window of his room when it rained, so his room was wet due to the rainwater splashing in.
example_ms: Bah Sam tidak menutup tingkap biliknya semasa hujan turun, jadi biliknya basah kerana tempias air hujan.

example_semai: "Bor hijog canuk jampong lekat perasoi, doh ya manik lebat susah hiha-jog madeg," Bah Dali kipasal nu ikekawat mase enai ku padag bula.
example_en: "We had better go home now while the rain is still drizzling, if it rains heavily, it will be difficult," Bah Dali told his friends while they were at the football field.
example_ms: "Baik kita harus pulang sekarang ke rumah sementara hujan masih renyai, jika hujan sudah lebat, susah kita hendak pulang ke rumah," Bah Dali memberitahu rakan-rakannya semasa mereka di padang bolasepak.

example_semai: "Waktu senanget jik, jik selalo jiperat krekbag ya abat warne oren," ajok jik kibecerite nu jik ha mase isenanget.
example_en: "When we were kids, we always lured the butterflies with an orange cloth," my grandmother told us about the time when she was a child.
example_ms: "Semasa kami masih kanak-kanak, kami selalu mengumpan rama-rama dengan menggunakan kain berwarna jingga," nenek bercerita kepada kami mengenai zaman kanak-kanaknya.

definition_ms: garau (suara); bunyi yang semakin kasar dan besar; parau; suara yang pecah dan tidak sedap didengar atau tidak merdu
example_semai: Perat suara Bah Samil mandei ha kikehol.
example_en: Bah Samil's voice became hoarse because he was suffering from a cough.
example_ms: Suara Bah Samil menjadi garau kerana dia sedang mengalami batuk.

definition_en: to pay attention to; to watch carefully; to see
example_semai: Cikgu Ramli kiperatik ianak muret naimain bula, kaha-panei ilei belok de buleh kipileh sebegei pemain sekulah.
example_en: The teacher Mr. Ramli carefully watched his students playing football to find out which one could represent the school.
example_ms: Cikgu Ramli memerhatikan anak-anak muridnya bermain bola sepak bagi mengetahui yang mana satu yang boleh dipilih sebagai pemain sekolah.

definition_en: to release, to pass, to hand

example_semai: "Agok sangka kibet ajeh, had kiperekbet taleh medei kisegat bohha-kos ju ibuknet. Et beryol ya hek dea kikos!" amek Bah Selan kiurok nu Bah Ian mase Bah Ian kiet nu deg Bah Selan, kaha-ajak hol nu pekan.
example_en: "Don't imagine that he is sleeping, he is just pretending to sleep because he doesn't want to wake up. Go and wake him up so he will get up!" Bah Selan's mother said Bah Ian when Bah Ian went to Bah Selan's house because he wanted to take him out to town.
example_ms: "Jangan menyangka bahawa dia tidur, dia berpura-pura tidur sahaja kerana malas untuk bangun dari tidur. Pergi kejutkan dia supaya dia bangun!" ibu Bah Selan berkata Bah Ian semasa Bah Ian pergi ke rumah Bah Selan kerana mahu mengajaknya keluar ke pekan.

definition_en: to show off; to display someone or something with the object of boasting
definition_ms: menunjuk-nunjuk; memperlihat sesuatu kepada orang lain bagi tujuan untuk berlagak
example_semai: Mai kampug naiperasan Bah Wan kibeh diteh-madeh ku nong kampug medei kaha-perekneng ha imutu pai. Tapi, naibeh pek naibakek taleh kerana ya naipanei ilei mimang begei ajeh.
example_en: The villagers noticed that Bah Wan had gone back and forth on the village's road with the aim of showing off his new motorcycle. But, they pretended not to see him because they already knew that he is that kind of person.
example_ms: Penduduk kampung telah perasan bahawa Bah Wan telah berberapa kali ke hulu-hilir jalan kampung tersebut dengan tujuan untuk menunjuk-nunjuk motosikal baharunya. Tetapi, mereka berpura-pura tidak nampak kerana sudah mengetahui bahawa beliau memang berperangai sebegitu.

example_semai: "Hampen hunen ya moden, ajok eng lekat pek kipecaya perembor ya dokte ku kedei. Ilei lekat kipecaya nu penglai serag begei ha herba," Wak Mila kipasal nu ikekawat.
example_en: "Even though now we live in the modern age, my grandmother still does not trust the treatment from the doctors in the town. She still believes in traditional medicines from the forest like herbs," Wak Mila told her friends.
example_ms: "Walaupun sekarang sudah maju, nenek saya masik tidak mempercayai pengubatan dari doktor di pekan. Dia masih mempercayai ubatan tradisi dari hutan seperti herba-herba," Wak Mila memberitahu rakan-rakannya.

example_semai: Abat de bitil ya Wak Nina perengheg nu matik medei bipoi kuat ya pinoi.
example_en: The cloth being dried by Wak Nina on the line flew all the way down to the ground because they were blown hard by the wind.
example_ms: Kain yang dijemur oleh Wak Nina terterbang hingga ke atas tanah kerana ditiup kuat oleh angin.

definition_en: one who cause something to come to life or to function
definition_ms: orang yang membuat sesuatu menjadi hidup atau berfungsi
example_semai: "Bok iperengsui mesin jehuk ajeh nek?" Bah Lalil kitanya nu ikekawat kerjak.
example_en: "Who is the person who started the woodworking machine on?" Bah Lalil asked his co-workers.
example_ms: "Siapakah yang menghidupkan mesin kayu itu?" Bah Lalil bertanya kepada kawan-kawan kerjanya.

definition_en: to keep fruit till ripe; to store fruit until it ripens
example_semai: Bah Lalei kiperep ipelek nangkak dea nep.
example_en: Bah Lalei keeps the jackfruit so that it is ripe enough.
example_ms: Bah Lalei memeram buah nangka supaya ianya cukup masak.

example_semai: Bah Ude kiperes 50 ikor kambeg biri-biri.
example_en: Bah Ude raised 50 sheep.
example_ms: Bah Ude membela 50 ekor kambing biri-biri.

definition_en: to bring out; to publish; to produce; to discharge
example_semai: Wak Minah kiperhol abat lalek ju ipuket baju.
example_en: Wak Minah took out a small piece of cloth from her shirt pocket.
example_ms: Wak Minah mengeluarkan sehelai kain kecil dari saku bajunya.

definition_en: to peek; to spy on someone
example_semai: Bah Sula kiperibet ha ikenon litau de tengah kinyok rukok ku belakang deg.
example_en: Bah Sula peeked at his son who was smoking in the backyard.
example_ms: Bah Sula mengintip anak lelakinya yang sedang merokok di belakang rumah.

example_semai: Pules naibeh roblog, naiperiksa lisen mai bemutu, bekerite ru beluri.
example_en: The police have set up roadblocks to check drivers' driving licenses.
example_ms: Polis membuat sekatan jalanraya bagi memeriksa lesen memandu pengguna jalanraya.

example_semai: Bah Bob kien ikenah perilai nu halak ku Cameron Highland ha nyaknik ikenah de lumpoh pek bor-bor hamapen ya kien nu dokte.
example_en: Bah Sani took his wife to seek treatment from a witch doctor in the Cameron Highlands for her paralysis from which she did not recover even though she had already seen a doctor.
example_ms: Bah Sani membawa isterinya berjampi dengan bomoh di Cameron Highland bagi penyakit isterinya yang lumpuh dan tidak sembuh walaupun sudah berjumpa dengan doktor.

example_semai: "Kampug jik lalek, had mong 6 peringak taleh ku kampug jik," Wak Eri kipasal bile bitanya ya ikekawat.
example_en: "Our village is small, there are only 6 families in our village," Wak Eri said when asked by her friends.
example_ms: "Kampung kami kecil, hanya ada 6 keluarga sahaja di kampung kami," Wak Eri memberitahu apabila ditanya oleh rakan-rakannya.

example_semai: "Mahkamah ya kiperhol perintah or pekusong deg adeh ku arik adeh juga medei pek dapat hipelangsei ihutag," Bah Tali kipasal nu ikenon-kenah.
example_en: "The court has issued an order that this house be vacated today because we cannot settle the debt (of this house)," Bah Tali told his wife and children.
example_ms: "Mahkamah sudah mengeluarkan perintah menyuruh rumah ini dikosongkan pada hari ini juga kerana kita tidak dapat melangsaikan hutang rumah ini," Bah Tali memberitahu kepada anak-anak dan isterinya.

example_semai: Wak Jamilah jeoi kicet periye medei kaha-jual nu pasa.
example_en: Wak Jamilah grows a lot of bitter gourds because she wants to sell it to the market.
example_ms: Wak Jamilah banyak menanam peria kerana mahu menjualnya ke pasar.

definition_ms: memulangkan; memberi kembali apa yang dipinjam buat sementara waktu kepada pemilik asalnya; pulangkan; kembalikan kepada seseorang atau ke sesuatu tempat; memulangkan kembali; menghantar (seseorang, objek dsbnya) ke tempat yang lain
example_semai: Wak Ayu kiperjog bob cerite de kipinjam ju perpustakaan sekulah medei bob ajeh ya dij kibaca.
example_en: Wak Ayu returned the story book that she had borrowed from the school library because she had finished reading it.
example_ms: Wak Ayu memulangkan buku cerita yang dipinjamnya dari perpustakaan sekolah kerana buku itu sudah habis dibacanya.

definition_en: to treat; to give medicine to
example_semai: Bah Sani kiperlai temak ku ijog ya penglai de biog ya dokte.
example_en: Bah Sani treated the scabies on his feet with the medicine given by the doctor.
example_ms: Bah Sani mengubati kudis di kakinya dengan ubat yang diberikan oleh doktor.

definition_en: to familiarize; to get used to
definition_ms: membiasakan diri (atau seseorang) dengan sesuatu perkara atau perbuatan
example_semai: "Agok perlej kenon hek ru bende mewah dea naijadi sengoi de sederhana doh," amek kiurok nu tenek eng.
example_en: "Don't let your children get used to luxury items. If you don't, they will later be unpretentious," the mother said to my brother.
example_ms: "Jangan membiasakan anak-anak kamu dengan benda-benda yang mewah, supaya mereka nanti menjadi orang yang sederhana," ibu berkata kepada abang saya.

definition_en: to make fun of; to mock; to jeer
example_semai: Memerip urag ju engkap enai naiperli ha Bah Alif.
example_en: Some of them made fun of Bah Alif.
example_ms: Beberapa orang antara mereka mengolok-olokkan Bah Alif.

example_semai: "Kelem adeh buleh hibakek jeoi perloi, medei ilangit pek besagop," Wak Mia kipasal nu igensir.
example_en: "We can see a lot of stars in the sky tonight because there are no clouds," Wak Mia told her husband.
example_ms: "Kita boleh melihat banyak bintang di langit pada malam ini kerana tidak terdapat awan," Wak Mia berkata kepada suaminya.

definition_ms: berlubang; sesuatu yang bertebuk atau berliang
example_semai: Seluar Bah Zamri ajeh perlong-perlong, tah gaye ma bahrip.
example_en: Bah Zamri's trousers have holes, not sure what kind of fashion that is.
example_ms: Seluar Bah Zamri berlubang-lubang, tidak pasti fashion apa.

example_semai: Hamapen ilei kerdor, tapi Wak Minah kigemar beh ikui perlong.
example_en: Although she is a woman, Wak Minah likes to make her head bald.
example_ms: Walaupun dia perempuan, tetapi Wak Minah suka membuat kepalanya botak.

definition_en: to lose; to forget where something is; to misplace
example_semai: Bah Fauzi birok ya iapak medei kipernyep yoj mutong iapak.
example_en: Bah Fauzi was scolded by his father because he lost his father's rubber tapping knife.
example_ms: Bah Fauzi dimarahi oleh bapanya kerana menghilangkan pisau penoreh getah milik bapanya.

example_semai: Bah Mali kipeberseh ideg pog medei ha jeoi perog.
example_en: Bah Mali cleaned his chicken coop because there were a lot of rats in it.
example_ms: Bah Mali membersihkan reban ayamnya kerana terdapat banyak tikus.

example_semai: "Entem jik mong jik dek perog, tapi hunen ya dat," Bah Mia kiurok nu Bah Lan.
example_en: "We used to keep a squirrel, but it's already dead now," Bah Mia told Bah Lan.
example_ms: "Dahulu kami ada membela tupai, tapi ianya sudah mati sekarang," Bah Mia berkata kepada Bah Lan.

definition_en: to spout or spray forcefully from the mouth
definition_ms: menyembur (api, air, nasi dsb) keluar dari mulut
example_semai: "Kuceg eng de lawa ajeh ya dat medei biperoh ya selik," Wak Tia kipasal nu Wak Sah.
example_en: "My beautiful cat is dead because the cobra sprayed it with poison," Wak Tia told Wak Sah.
example_ms: "Kucing saya yang cantik itu sudah mati kerana ular tedung menyemburnya dengan bisa," Wak Tia memberitahu kepada Wak Sah.

definition_ms: garing; rangup; sesuatu yang dimasak atau digoreng (dengan menggunakan minyak) agak kering dan keras tetapi rapuh sedikit (Cth: keropok goreng, biskut dll)
example_semai: Wak Tila kigureng kak kembong lei peroj.
example_en: Wak Tila fried the kembong fish until crispy.
example_ms: Wak Tila menggoreng ikan kembong sehingga garing.

example_semai: Bah Selah kiperop Bah Yan nu serag, kaha-kek awat kaha-beh ideg pog.
example_en: Bah Selah accompanied Bah Yan to the forest looking for bamboo to make a chicken coop.
example_ms: Bah Selah menemani Bah Yan ke hutan yang mencari buluh bagi membuat reban ayam.

example_semai: Wak Lila ya perper kikos medei kaha-masak caknak ha igengsir nu kerjak.
example_en: Wak Lila woke up at dawn because she wanted to cook food for her husband who wanted to go to work.
example_ms: Wak Lila bangun pada waktu subuh kerana dia mahu memasak makanan bagi suaminya yang mahu ke tempat kerja.

example_semai: Wak Manis kipersop caknak nu ikenon de iumur 2 tahut ajeh.
example_en: Wah Manis fed food to his 2-year-old son.
example_ms: Wah Manis menyuapi makanan kepada anaknya yang berumur 2 tahun tersebut.

example_semai: Bah Bedoi kigemar put perut medei ha isej.
example_en: Bah Bedoi likes to hunt the silver lutung (with a blow gun) for its meat.
example_ms: Bah Bedoi suka menyumpit lotong kerana dagingnya.

example_semai: Sanget ajeh kiperwas isengik ru kipanjap.
example_en: The child gratified himself by crying out loud.
example_ms: Kanak-kanak tersebut memuaskan hatinya dengan menangis sekuat-kuatnya.

example_semai: Bah Kamil kipes ibilek medei kaha-lai idaknak.
example_en: Bah Kamil swept his bedroom because he wanted to spread out a place to sleep.
example_ms: Bah Kamil menyapu biliknya kerana mahu membentangkan tempat tidurnya.

example_semai: Pelog deg Bah Wira biperheg ya pinoi kuat mase pes ku kelem jihnek.
example_en: The roof of Bah Wira's house was blown away by a strong wind during a storm last night.
example_ms: Bumbung rumah Bah Wira diterbangkan oleh angin kuat semasa kejadian ribut pada malam tadi.

example_semai: Wak Tila kipeselamat kuceg de yog ku kateh lungkang.
example_en: Wak Tila saved the cat that fell into the drain.
example_ms: Wak Tila menyelamatkan kucing yang jatuh ke dalam longkang.

example_semai: Bah Mat ajehleh peselamat de kipeselamat sengoi nyep ku serag, de kinyep mase naibeh perkhemahan ku serag.
example_en: Bah Mat is a rescuer who saves people who are lost in the jungle while participating in camping in the jungle.
example_ms: Bah Mat adalah penyelamat yang menyelamatkan orang hilang di hutan, yang hilang ketika menyertai perkhemahan di hutan.

example_semai: Bah Yan bicuet ientag ya iamek medei ha kipet kenon cok.
example_en: Bah Yan's ear was twisted by his mother because he hit the puppy.
example_ms: Telinga Bah Yan dipulas oleh ibunya kerana memukul anak anjing.

example_semai: Mai kampug naipetahan tik enai ju biteroke ya mai luar.
example_en: The villagers defend their land from being explored by the outsiders.
example_ms: Orang kampung mempertahankan tanah mereka daripada diterokai oleh orang luar.

example_semai: Wak Mila kikep numbor petame ku kateh ikelas.
example_en: Wak Mila got first place in her class.
example_ms: Wak Mila mendapat nombor pertama di dalam kelasnya.

definition_en: firstly, to begin with, at the first step
example_semai: "Petame, hik kena higaul gula ru planta canuk," kiurok ya amek mase kiajar jik beh koi.
example_en: "First, we must mix sugar with margerine," my mother said when she was teaching us to make cookies.
example_ms: "Mula-mula, kita mesti menggaul gula dengan planta terlebih dahulu," ibu berkata semasa menajar kami membuat kuih.

example_semai: "Hek silap dek, deg Bah Akil ku petei. Adeh deg eng," Wak Ramlah kiurok nu sengoi de kikek ha deg Bah Akil.
example_en: "You got the wrong house, Mister. Bah Akil's house is over there. This is my house," Wak Ramlah said to the person who is looking for Bah Akil's house.
example_ms: "Encik silap rumah, rumah Bah Akil di sana. Ini rumah saya," Wak Ramlah berkata kepada orang yang mencari rumah Bah Akil.

definition_en: to pay attention; to observe; to watch
example_semai: Wak Nira pek kisedar paknyakneh liu ilei kipetie ha ikekenon kuceg naibemain ku rak daknek kasot.
example_en: Wak Nira did not realize that she had actually spent a long time to pay attention to her kittens playing at the shoe rack.
example_ms: Wak Nira tidak menyedari rupa-rupanya dia mengambil masa yang lama memerhatikan anak-anak kucingnya bermain di rak kasut.

example_semai: Wak Mia kijap medei bipet ya itenek, medei kipetir ha bengwal itenek ajeh.
example_en: Wak Mia cried because she was beaten by her sister, because she imitate mockingly her sister's words.
example_ms: Wak Mia menangis kerana dipukul oleh kakaknya, kerana dia mengajuk kata-kata kakaknya tersebut.

example_semai: Mai keral ku kampug Tisong ajeh naipetrol ku jap kelem medei mong kes mai siksij ku kampug enai.
example_en: The men in Tisong village patrol every night because there are cases of theft in the village.
example_ms: Orang-orang lelaki di kampung Tisong membuat rondaan pada setiap malam kerana terdapat kes kecurian di kampung tersebut.

example_semai: Bah Samdin kiisi petrol ku kateh butol ha minyak imisen rumpot.
example_en: Bah Samdin filled a bottle with petrol to use as the fuel for his lawn mower.
example_ms: Bah Samdin mengisi petrol ke dalam botol sebagai minyak untuk mesin rumputnya.

definition_en: loofah, a type of vine cucumber
example_semai: Bah Derin mong kicet jeoi petula medei kaha-jual nu pasa.
example_en: Bah Derin has planted a lot of loofah plants because he wants to sell it to the market.
example_ms: Bah Derin ada menanam banyak petola kerana mahu menjualnya ke pasar.

example_semai: Kak pia adeh jenis de tahan ru buleh kisui ku ma taleh jenis teu, pek kira ku pateuka ku lumbong.
example_en: Tilapia is a hardy fish and can live in any type of fresh water, whether in the river or abandon mines.
example_ms: Ikan tilapia adalah ikan yang tahan lasak dan boleh hidup pada sebarang jenis air tawar, tidak mengira pada air sungai ataupun air lombong.

example_semai: "Ya sudahka hacakcak?" kitanya ya iamek. Kiberlag ya ikenon, "Pidok."
example_en: "Have you eaten?" mother asked. "Not yet," her son replied.
example_ms: "Sudahkah kamu makan?" ibunya bertanya. Anaknya menjawap, "Belum."

definition_ms: pada masa akan datang; di kemudian hari; hari-hari mendatang
example_semai: "Engkek hod engkeog cuntoh de bor, medei engkek pigoi adehleh de jadi cuntoh nu mai sanget de keknit," engrok Cikgu Rama de kiog motivasi nu pelajar sekulah menengah.
example_en: "You should set a good example, because later in the future you will be a role model to the young generation," said Cikgu Rama who was giving a motivational talk to the high school students.
example_ms: "Kamu hendaklah memberikan teladan yang baik, kerana pada masa akan datang kamulah yang menjadi contoh kepada golongan muda yang kemudian," kata Cikgu Rama yang memberikan motivasi kepada pelajar-pelajar sekolah menengah.

definition_en: to pay attention; to observe; to watch
example_semai: "Ju cinek keral ajeh kipijoi ha hik. Tah yama?" Wak Lila kipasal nu ikekawat mase tengah naipoj bas.
example_en: "He has been watching us for a while. Why, I don't know," Wak Lila told her friends while they were waiting for the bus.
example_ms: "Dari tadi dia memerhatikan kita. Entah kenapa?" Wak Lila memberitahu kepada rakan-rakannya semasa mereka sedang menunggu bas.

example_semai: Wak Sali kipiker kaha-masak nasi lemak ha sarapan pegelap huper.
example_en: Wak Sali thinks that she wants to cook nasi lemak for breakfast tomorrow morning.
example_ms: Wak Sali berfikir bahawa dia mahu memasak nasi lemak untuk sarapan esok pagi.

definition_en: to faint; to have a seizure
example_semai: Bah Gundur kipilak ku itempat kerjak.
example_en: Bah Gundur had a seizure at his workplace.
example_ms: Bah Gundur pengsan di tempat kerjanya.

example_semai: Wak Rina kipileh abor-bor bawang de kaha-beli ku kedei runcit ajeh medei mong de sok.
example_en: Wak Rina is carefully choosing the onion that she wants to buy at the grocery store because there are onions that are already rotten.
example_ms: Wak Rina memilih dengan betul-betul bawang yang ingin dia beli di kedai runcit tersebut kerana terdapat bawang yang sudah rosak.

example_semai: Bah Karat pek jemu-jemu kiulag neng pilem P. Ramlee. Amen mong mase, kek mesti kineng pilem-pilem de bilakon ya P. Ramlee.
example_en: Bah Karat was never tired of watching P. Ramlee's movie over and over again. If he had the time, he would definitely watch any films that starred P. Ramlee.
example_ms: Bah Karat tidak jemu-jemu menonton ulang filem P. Ramlee. Jika dia mempunyai kelapangan, dia pasti akan menonton filem-filem yang dilakonkan oleh P. Ramlee.

example_semai: Lagi 5 tahut nij Wak Ciki kipincen ju ikerjak.
example_en: In another 5 years Wak Ciki will retire from her job.
example_ms: Lagi 5 tahun Wak Ciki akan berpencen dari kerjanya.

definition_en: to borrow; to own someone else's property with the permission for a certain period of time.
definition_ms: pinjam; memiliki sesuatu hakmilik orang lain dengan kebenaran individu terbabit untuk suatu tempoh yang tertentu.
example_semai: Eng ha pinjam bob hek buleh ka?
example_en: I want to borrow your book, can I?
example_ms: Saya nak pinjam buku kamu, bolehkah?

example_semai: Wak Maria kigadok pintu bilek ipensyarah sebelom kimuj.
example_en: Wak Maria knocked on the door of her lecturer's room before entering it.
example_ms: Wak Maria mengetuk pintu bilik pensyarahnya sebelum memasukinya.

definition_en: nickname of the middle son, which is not the youngest nor the oldest son
definition_ms: nama panggilan bagi anak lelaki di tengah, iaitu bagi anak yang bukan bongsu dan bukan sulung
example_semai: "Eng mong nik urag kenon. Wak Long, Bah Piog ru Bah Luj," kipasal ya Wak Nani bile bitanya ya ikawat kerjak ku tempat kerjak pai.
example_en: "I have three children, namely Wak Long, Bah Piog and Bah Luj," Wak Nani said when asked by a colleague at her new workplace.
example_ms: "Saya mempunyai tiga orang anak, iaitu Wak Long, Bah Piog dan Bah Luj," Wak Nani memberitahu apabila ditanya oleh kawan sekerja di tempat kerjanya yang baharu.

example_semai: "Piog, bulehka hatulog angkat arap eng adeh, pek nglalu medei ya ngengrak," ajok Bah Sali kiurok nu litau de kibakek ku simpang kampug.
example_en: "Son, can you please lift my belongings, I am not strong enough because I am old," Bah Sali's grandmother said to the young man she saw at the village's entrance.
example_ms: "Nak, bolehkah kamu tolong angkat barang saya ini, saya tidak larat kerana sudah tua," nenek kepada Bah Sali berkata kepada pemuda yang dia nampak di simpang kampung.

example_semai: Bah Duan kitejaga ju ibuknet medei kikekjet ha suara Bah Pani de tiba-tiba kipipui mase naibukbet.
example_en: Bah Duan woke up from his sleep because he was shocked by Bah Pani who suddenly became delirious (and started raving) while they were sleeping.
example_ms: Bah Duan terjaga dari tidurnya kerana terkejut dengan suara Bah Pani yang tiba-tiba mengigau semasa mereka sedang tidur.

example_semai: Mase kaha-beros ilemunj, Bah Ran kipan-pit ubat gigi de ya kaha-dij ajeh medei kisip bakli ilei de pai.
example_en: Bah Ran squeezed the toothpaste that was about to run out as hard as he could when he wanted to brush his teeth, because he had forgotten to buy a new toothpaste.
example_ms: Bah Ran memicit ubat giginya yang mahu habis tersebut dengan sekuat-kuatnya semasa dia mahu memberus gigi, kerana dia terlupa untuk membeli ubat gigi yang baharu.

example_semai: "Pelit bikap lempak eng ya pit," Bah Ewan kipasal nu iapak.
example_en: "My thighs hurt from being bitten by a mite," Bah Ewan told his father.
example_ms: "Peha saya terasa pedih digigit oleh tungau," Bah Ewan memberitahu bapanya.

example_semai: "Hek adeh kenon bok?" Bah Dul kitanya. Bah Rani kiberlag, "Eng adeh pitak Bah Lobang ju Kampar."
example_en: "Whose son are you?" Bah Bul asked. Bah Rani replied, "I am from the Bah Lobang tribe, which is from Kampar."
example_ms: "Kamu anak siapa?" Bah Bul bertanya. Bah Rani menjawab, " Saya dari puak Bah Lobang, yang dari Kampar."

definition_en: party; a specific group of people
example_semai: "Adeh ya seminggu pitak aska naibeh latihan ku serag kampug jik, tapi pèq jipanei lei bile kaha-dij," Bah Man kipasal nu ikekawat.
example_en: "It's been a week now that the soldiers have been training in the forest at our village, but we do not know when they will finish," Bah Man told his friends.
example_ms: "Sekarang sudah seminggu pihak askar membuat latihan di hutan di kampung kami, tapi kami tidak mengetahui sampai bila akan tamat," Bah Man memberitahu rakan-rakannya.

example_semai: "Mong kerite kieksiden kelemnek mase ngha-jog madeg ju tempat kerjak. Rimdij sengoi ju kerite ajeh planteg nu luar! Besengoh hineng, mong behip majeh-manen!" Wak Rika kipasal nu ikekawat ku tempat kerjak.
example_en: "There was a car that had an accident when I was about to go home from work yesterday. Everyone from the car was thrown out of the car! It was scary because the place was covered in blood!" Wak Rika told her friends at the workplace.
example_ms: "Terdapat kereta mengalami kemalangan semasa saya mahu pulang dari tempat kerja semalam. Kesemua orang dari kereta tersebut tercampak ke luar kereta! Sangat menakutkan kerana tempat tersebut berlumuran dengan darah!" Wak Rika memberitahu kawan-kawan di tempat kerjanya.

example_semai: "Og plasteg ajeh madeh, ngha-isi sampah," Wak Salwa kisemanj nu imenang.
example_en: "Give me the plastic bag, I want to put the trash in it," Wak Salwa asked her sister.
example_ms: "Beri beg plastik tersebut kepada saya, saya mahu mengisi sampah," Wak Salwa meminta kepada adiknya.

definition_en: shroud ghost; a kind of ghost relating to a corpse that has just been buried and is still wrapped and therefore trapped in its shroud
definition_ms: pocong; sejenis hantu, iaitu mayat yang baharu dikebumikan dan masih berbungkus dengan kain kapan
example_semai: "Bok imai taleh de nailalu ku nong ajeh mesti naibekek pocong, medei ku pajeh mong penep Gop," Bah Kurat kiserngoh ha icecenok de selalo naihol ru naicekcip ku arik kelem.
example_en: "Anyone who passes by the road will definitely see a pocong, because there is an Islamic cemetery there," Bah Kurat frightened his grandchildren because they often going out at night.
example_ms: "Sesiapa sahaja yang melalui jalan tersebut pasti akan ternampak pocong, kerana di situ terdapat perkuburan Islam," Bah Kurat menakut-nakutkan cucu-cucunya kerana mereka sering keluar dan berjalan-jalan di waktu malam.

definition_en: to open a container that has a lid like tupperware
definition_ms: membuka sesuatu bekas yang berpenutup seperti tupperware
example_semai: Wak Imah pek lalu kaha-pog tuntop mangkok de pai kiperhol ju peti ais medei ya melekat ya caknak de mong ku ikateh. Jadi kirendam canuk mangkok ya teu beket.
example_en: Wak Imah was not able to open the lid of the food container that she had just taken out of the fridge because it was stuck to the food that was inside it. So, she soaked the container with a hot water.
example_ms: Wak Imah tidak berdaya untuk membuka penutup mangkuk yang baharu beliau keluarkan dari peti sejuk kerana ianya sudah melekat oleh makanan yang ada di dalamnya. Jadi, dia merendam mangkuk tersebut terlebih dahulu dengan air panas.

example_semai: Bah Melan bipog ikaming ya iamek medei kilase nu iajok.
example_en: Bah Melan was slapped on the cheek by his mother for behaving rudely towards his grandmother.
example_ms: Bah Melan ditampar pipinya oleh ibunya kerana berperangai kurang ajar terhadap neneknya.

example_semai: Telas manik kihoj, mesti mong betes pog naihol.
example_en: Once the rain stops, you will surely find mound mushrooms growing.
example_ms: Setelah hujan berhenti, mesti terdapat cendawan busut yang tumbuh.

example_semai: Wak Melati mong jeoi kibelak pog medei kaha-jual nu pasa.
example_en: Wak Melati has raised a lot of chickens because she wants to sell them to the market.
example_ms: Wak Melati ada membela banyak ayam kerana mahu menjualnya ke pasar.

example_semai: Jik jipok teg bile jik cergai lagu ajeh.
example_en: We clapped our hands when we heard the songs.
example_ms: Kami bertepuk tangan apabila mendengar lagu itu.

example_semai: Bah Caca kipoh selik ajeh lei dat medei kisengoh doh kisot sengoi atao binatag daknek.
example_en: Bah Caca beat the cobra to death because he feared that it would bite a human or a pet.
example_ms: Bah Caca memukul ular tedung tersebut sehingga mati kerana takut ianya akan mematuk manusia atau binatang peliharaan.

example_semai: Wak Ain kipoh periok canak tapi kineng ya dij icanak.
example_en: Wak Ani opened the rice cooker but saw that the rice in it was finished.
example_ms: Wak Ani membuka periuk nasi tetapi melihat nasi di periuk tersebut sudah habis.

definition_en: to blow (of wind, not human agency)
example_semai: "Sendap bipoi ya pinoi," kiurok ya Bah Miran mase kigigei ku kerop jehuk ru ikekawat.
example_en: "The wind feels good," Bah Miran said while sitting under a tree with his friends.
example_ms: "Sedapnya ditiup oleh angin," Bah Miran berkata semasa sedang duduk di bawah pokok dengan rakan-rakannya.

example_semai: Ihat liu sanget-sanget sekulah ajeh naipoj bas, tapi pek gulei-lei.
example_en: The school children waited for the bus for a long time, but it never arrived.
example_ms: Sangat lama kanak-kanak sekolah tersebut menunggu bas, tetapi ianya tidak kunjung tiba.

definition_en: to grope; to touch with the palm of the hand
example_semai: Ku balak kenglap Wak Nina kiperpom, kikek ha iabat gebar.
example_en: In the darkness Wak Nina groped around to find her blanket.
example_ms: Di dalam kegelapan Wak Nina meraba-raba bagi mencari selimutnya.

definition_ms: pam; sejenis alat yang digunakan untuk mengeluarkan atau menyedut udara, air, dsbnya.
example_semai: Bah Ruma kaha-et nu kedei medei kaha-tukar pom de pai kibeli ajeh, ipam ajeh ya rusak.
example_en: Bah Ruma wanted to go to the store because he wanted to switch the pump he had just bought, because it was damaged.
example_ms: Bah Ruma mahu ke kedai kerana mahu menukar pam yang baharu dibelinya tersebut, kerana pam tersebut sudah rosak.

example_semai: Bah Suan kisingah ku stisen minyak medei kaha-pom itaya mutu.
example_en: Bah Suan stopped at the petrol station because he wanted to pump his motorcycle tires.
example_ms: Bah Suan singgah di stesyen minyak kerana hendak mengepam tayar motosikalnya.

definition_en: female vampiric ghost relating to a woman who died in childbirth
definition_ms: pontianak, sejenis hantu yang berasal daripada perempuan yang mati semasa mengandungkan anaknya
example_semai: Sejak mambek keral naibakek pontianak, sejak ajeh walah pek nailepak nij ku tempat ajeh ku ikelem arik.
example_en: Since the boys saw vampires, they have not hung out in that place at night.
example_ms: Sejak budak-budak lelaki ternampak pontianak, sejak itulah mereka tak melepak lagi di tempat itu pada waktu malam.

example_semai: Bah Kelli kikep kerjak sebegei anggote pores.
example_en: Bah Kelli got a job as a forestry ranger.
example_ms: Bah Kelli mendapat kerja sebagai pegawai perhutanan.

example_semai: Pos adeh ya hajap biha-berkep medei ya sil.
example_en: Barking deer are hard to find because they are extinct.
example_ms: Kijang sudah susah untuk dijumpai kerana ianya sudah pupus.

example_semai: Wak Manig kiprimai ha mai jelwal kapet de kicereg ha ilei ju luar deg.
example_en: Wak Manig ignored the carpet seller who was calling her from outside of the house.
example_ms: Wak Manig tidak mengedahkan penjual karpet yang memanggil dirinya dari luar rumah.

example_semai: Wak Rima kingut teu lei kipuas medei ihad kiselot.
example_en: Wak Rima drank water until she was satisfied because she was very thirsty.
example_ms: Wak Rima meminum air sehingga merasa puas kerana dia sangat dahaga.

definition_ms: puasa; menahan diri dari makan atau minum mengikut tempoh waktu tertentu
example_semai: Mai Muslim naibepuas nugencek canuk, telas ajeh baruleh naiberaya.
example_en: Muslims fast for a month first, then they celebrate.
example_ms: Orang Muslim berpuasa selama sebulan terlebuh dahulu, selepas itu baharulah mereka beraya.

example_semai: Wak Bilah ru iamek ubai berkep pucok betek ha menasak dui doh.
example_en: Wak Bilah and her mother picked the papaya shoots to be cooked later in the evening.
example_ms: Wak Bilah dan ibunya memetik pucuk betik untuk dimasak petang nanti.

example_semai: Bah Ude kigemar cak pug gureng.
example_en: Bah Ude likes to eat fried chicken.
example_ms: Bah Ude suka memakan ayam goreng.

example_semai: Bah Lima kikena pug, jadi ilei bipermuj nu wad medei baha-bedah.
example_en: Bah Lima suffered from a hernia, so he is being admitted to the ward because he will be undergoing surgery.
example_ms: Bah Lima mengalami burut, jadi beliau dimasukkan ke wad kerana akan menjalani pembedahan.

example_semai: Bah Aiman kipujok iamek dea kibeli meskar pai ha kek.
example_en: Bah Aiman persuaded his mother to buy a bicycle for him.
example_ms: Bah Aiman memujuk ibunya supaya membeli basikal bagi dirinya.

example_semai: "Pukal lagu adeh taleh de bipasag, titokka lagu kilek de bor gek?" Wak Han kiurok nu ikenon menaleh.
example_en: "You often play just this song; is there no other song that is better than this?" Wak Han said to her daughter.
example_ms: "Lagu ini sahaja yang sering dimainkan, tiadakah lagu lain yang lebih baik dari yang ini?" Wak Han berkata kepada anak gadisnya.

example_semai: Bah Ewan kijaet ipuket seluar kerjak de betej.
example_en: Bah Ewan sewed the torn pocket of his work pants.
example_ms: Bah Ewan menjahit poket seluar kerjanya yang koyak itu.

example_semai: Bah Rilan kicak ipenglai jap kali ku pukol 10 pegelap ru 10 kelem.
example_en: Bah Rilan always took his medicine at 10 am and 10 pm.
example_ms: Bah Rilan setiap kali memakan ubatnya pada pukul 10 pagi dan 10 malam.

example_semai: Ku hujong tahut adeh, Wak Cina ru iperingak naicadang naiha-et nu Pulau Pangkor.
example_en: At the end of this year, Wak Cina and her family plan to go to Pangkor Island.
example_ms: Pada hujung tahun ini, Wak Cina dan keluarganya bercadang mahu pergi ke Pulau Pangkor.

example_semai: Mambek pules ajeh naibeh roblog medei naiha-kek mai de berompak ha bank cinek.
example_en: The police set up roadblocks because they wanted to find the thieves who robbed the bank earlier.
example_ms: Para polis tersebut membuat sekatan jalan raya kerana mahu mencari perompak yang merompak bank tadi.

example_semai: Regak kasot sekulah ajeh tige puloh lima.
example_en: The price of the school shoes is thirty-five ringgits.
example_ms: Harga kasut sekolah itu tiga puluh lima.

example_semai: Wak Eh kirendam cengroi pulot nukenglem canuk sebelom kigap ku ipegelap huper.
example_en: Wak Eh soaked the glutinous rice overnight before cooking it on the following day.
example_ms: Wak Eh merendam beras pulut semalaman terlebih dahulu sebelum memasaknya pada keesokan harinya.

example_semai: "Punggah dij kutak ajeh nu luar, eng ngha-buong ihabok," Wak Pia kiurok nu imemenang mase naikemas stor deg enai.
example_en: "Unload all the boxes outside because I want to get rid of the dust," Wak Pia said to her sisters as they tidied up the storage room in their house.
example_ms: "Punggah kesemua kotak tersebut ke luar kerana saya hendak membersihkan habuk," Wak Pia berkata kepada adik-adiknya semasa mereka mengemas stor rumah mereka.

definition_en: clump; cluster; bunch of plants that seem to have the same root
definition_ms: rumpun; perdu; kumpulan pokok yang sama jenisnya dan tumbuh berkelompok (buluh dll)
example_semai: Wak Liheg kikot nupungkak keneng pandan medei kaha-beh teu gula ha caknak kenduri ku kampug enai.
example_en: Wak Liheg took a clump of pandan leaves because he wanted to make sugar water for the feast in their village.
example_ms: Wak Liheg mengambil serumpun pokok pandan kerana dia mahu membuat air gula bagi kenduri di kampung mereka.

example_semai: Bah Jali ajeh pengluk kampug mai Cengrig.
example_en: Bah Jali is the headman in Chenderiang.
example_ms: Bah Jali adalah penghulu kampung di Chenderiang.

definition_ms: puntung; lebihan (rokok, kayu, dll) yang bersisa kerana sebahagiannya telah terbakar
example_semai: Wak Ramlah kipes puntog rukok de besipah bipej diteh-madeh ku depan ikedei.
example_en: Wak Ramlah swept up the cigarette butts that had been thrown around in front of her shop.
example_ms: Wak Ramlah menyapu puntung-puntung rokok yang bersepah dibuang merata-rata di depan kedainya.

example_semai: "Bok ipunyak kasot adeh? Nglog nok ya eng," apak kiurok.
example_en: "Who owns these shoes? I used them for a while," the father said.
example_ms: "Siapa punya kasut ini? Saya gunakannya sebentar," bapa berkata.

definition_ms: puru; sejenis penyakit kulit pada unggas
example_semai: Penyaket puru adeh biasa amen kikena ku pog, hineng begei ha kahang ku berog enai.
example_en: Fowl pox disease is common in chickens, it looks like warts on their skin.
example_ms: Penyakit puru adalah biasa terjadi pada ayam, ianya kelihatan seperti ketuat pada kulit mereka.

example_semai: Pusat entoi negare hik mong ku Putrajaya.
example_en: The head offices for our country are located in Putrajaya.
example_ms: Ibu pejabat bagi negara kita terletak di Putrajaya.

example_semai: "Maap, lingah jilei. Bepuseng jicip, tapi pek jibakek nong nu madeh," apak jik kiurok nu ibos mase mong jamuan ku deg ibos ajeh.
example_en: "Sorry, we arrived late. We kept circling and searching, but could not find the way here," our father said to his employer during a banquet at his employer's house.
example_ms: "Maaf, kami lambat sampai. Berpusing-pusing kami mencari, tetapi tidak dapat menjumpai jalan untuk ke sini," bapa berkata kepada majikannya semasa terdapat jamuan di rumah majikannya tersebut.

example_semai: Dij nyep put ceber Bah Kala medei bitulak ya lek.
example_en: All of Bah Kala's the rubber cups were missing because a wild boar had prodded them.
example_ms: Semua cawan susu getah Bah Kala hilang kerana disodok oleh babi.

definition_en: to shoot with a blowpipe; to blow a blowpipe
example_semai: Bah Keli pai kibelajar pekput, jadi kiput cep ku keneng legos tapi pek lei.
example_en: Bah Keli just learned to hunt using a blow pipe, so he blew a blowpipe at a bird in the rambutan tree, but it did not reach the target.
example_ms: Bah Keli baharu belajar menyumpit, jadi dia menyumpit burung yang terdapat di pokok rambutan, tetapi ianya tidak sampai ke sasaran.

definition_ms: putik; buah yang masih awal dan muda (putik jambu, putik manggis dll)
example_semai: Lekat iputeg lagi pelek mangge ajeh, tapi Wak Salwa ya kahagu-cak juga.
example_en: The mango is still unripe, but Wak Salwa still wants to eat it.
example_ms: Buah mangga tersebut masih berputik, tapi Wak Salwa masih mahu memakannya juga.

definition_en: princess; the daughter of a king
example_semai: Hamapen Putri Salwa ajeh numom putri, tapi ilei bor isengik ru pek sumbong ru mai biasa.
example_en: Although Puteri Salwa is a princess, she is kind and not arrogant towards ordinary people.
example_ms: Walaupun Puteri Salwa adalah seorang puteri, tetapi dia baik hati dan tidak sombong dengan orang kebanyakkan.

definition_ms: puyuh; sejenis burung kecil tanpa ekor
example_semai: Mong jeoi pengleg puyoh bijual ku pasar.
example_en: There are many quail eggs being sold at the market.
example_ms: Terdapat banyak telur puyuh dijual di pasar.

definition_en: to grope; to touch with the palm of the hand
example_semai: Kuceg ajeh kibiar ilei bicap, bileu ru birabe ya mabek sanget medei ilei jenis kuceg de manja.
example_en: The cat allows itself to be held, stroked and groped by the children because it is indeed a type of cat that is spoiled.
example_ms: Kucing tersebut membiarkan dirinya dipegang, diusap-usap dan dirabai oleh kanak-kanak kerana ianya memang jenis kucing yang manja.

example_semai: "Mongka rabe hek? Eng ngha-pinjam," Bah Piul kisemanj nu Bah Jani.
example_en: "Do you have a pencil eraser?" Bah Piul asked Bah Jani. "I want to borrow it."
example_ms: "Kamu mempunyai pemadam pensil? Saya mahu pinjam," Bah Piul meminta kepada Bah Jani.

definition_ms: racun; bahan berbisa dan boleh mematikan sesuatu (binatang atau orang)
example_semai: Bah Jamil manahjeh kidat medei kingut racot rumput.
example_en: Bah Jamil died because he drank weed poison.
example_ms: Bah Jamil meninggal dunia kerana meminum racun rumput.

example_semai: Bah Harry kiracot lalag ku balairaye, medei minggu depan mong mesyuarat kampug ku pajeh.
example_en: Bah Harry poisoned the weeds at the village hall, as a village meeting will be held at the place next week.
example_ms: Bah Harry meracun lalang yang terdapat di balairaya, kerana mesyuarat kampung akan dijalankan di tempat tersebut pada minggu hadapan.

definition_ms: panggang; memanggang (daging); memasak di atas bara api
example_semai: Wak Milah kigemar rag kak ku kemil os, medei kek pek kigemar kak amen bigureng.
example_en: Wak Milah likes to grill fish, because she does not like to eat fried fish.
example_ms: Wak Milah suka memanggang ikan di atas api, kerana dia tidak suka memakan ikan yang digoreng.

example_semai: Bah Seli kien yoj ru raga nu serag medei kaha-kek rangas.
example_en: Bah Seli brought a machete and a basket to the forest to find firewood.
example_ms: Bah Seli membawa parang dan raga ke hutan kerana hendak mencari kayu api.

example_semai: Bah Jamal kipoj ku kateh ikerite taleh ru ikenon mase ikenah kibakli ku kateh kedei medei pek kitahan ha ragam ikenon de pek buleh kigei nutempat taleh amen naibakli ku kedei.
example_en: Bah Jamal waited in the car with his son while his wife is shopping at the grocery shop, because he could not stand his son's behaviour. He did not know to sit quiet if they went out shopping.
example_ms: Bah Jamal menunggu di dalam kereta sahaja bersama dengan anaknya semasa isterinya membeli barang-barang di dalam kedai, kerana tidak tahan dengan tingkah laku anaknya yang tidak reti untuk duduk diam jika mereka keluar membeli di kedai.

example_semai: Wak Rara kikot baju de iragi renganj medei kiminat ragi ajeh.
example_en: Wak Rara bought a red shirt because that color is her favorite color.
example_ms: Wak Rara membeli baju yang berwarna merah kerana warna tersebut merupakan warna kegemarannya.

example_semai: "Borle ragi seluar hek adeh, ngminat ngneng. Ku belok habeli?" Wak Ria kitanya nu Wak Mia.
example_en: "The pattern on your trousers is beautiful, I like it. Where did you buy them?" Wak Ria asked Wak Mia.
example_ms: "Cantiknya corak seluar kamu, suka saya melihatnya. Di mana akmu membelinya?" Wak Ria bertanya kepada Wak Mia.

definition_ms: ragum; alat penyepit; sejenis alat penyepit daripada besi memegang sesuatu benda
example_semai: Bah Oli kiguna ragum, kisepet paep besi de kaha-gergaji.
example_en: Bah Oli uses a clamp to hold the iron rod he wants to saw.
example_ms: Bah Oli menggunakan ragum bagi menyepit batang besi yang mahu digergajinya.

example_semai: "Agok siyet tebal raha ajeh, doh lingah kaha-masak amen entoi-entoi," amek kipasal nu menang eng de tengah kisiyet sej kerbau.
example_en: "Do not cut like that, it will take a long time to cook if the pieces are large," my mother told my sister as she cut the water buffalo meat.
example_ms: "Jangan potong seperti itu, nanti ianya akan lambat masak jika saiznya besar," ibu memberitahu adik saya yang sedang memotong daging kerbau.

definition_ms: raja; pemerintah atau ketua bagi sesebuah kerajaan (negara atau negeri)
example_semai: Setiap lengrik ku Malaysia mong iketue, biasajeh numom raja.
example_en: Each state in Malaysia has its own head of state, usually a king.
example_ms: Setiap negeri di Malaysia mempunyai ketuanya sendiri, biasanya seorang raja.

definition_en: Rajah Brooke's birdwing butterfly; a type of butterfly which has black, green and yellow wings
